name: Hillsdale College via Restore Our Nation
subject: Help us understand CRT’s influence
body:
I’m reaching out because you’re an informed patriot who cares about this country.

This sponsored message from Restore Our Nation is on behalf of Hillsdale College

| | | [](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/spe9wYUPNXrako_nviNqww~~/AARclAA~/RgRpEIOKP0SdaHR0cHM6Ly9pbmZvLmhpbGxzZGFsZS5lZHUvY3J0LXN1cnZleS8_c2M9MDE1NzJOMDI0MkwwQURPREVFUyZ1dG1fc291cmNlPXByb3NwZWN0aW5nJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPWNydHN1cnZleSZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1MT05HX1BBLVJPTi0yMDI0MTEwOFcDc3BjQgpnLITtLWdSxilkUhRkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbVgEAAAAAA~~)
---
| | Dear Fellow American,

[**Hillsdale College has selected you to participate in its National Opinion Survey on Critical Race Theory (CRT).**](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/spe9wYUPNXrako_nviNqww~~/AARclAA~/RgRpEIOKP0SdaHR0cHM6Ly9pbmZvLmhpbGxzZGFsZS5lZHUvY3J0LXN1cnZleS8_c2M9MDE1NzJOMDI0MkwwQURPREVFUyZ1dG1fc291cmNlPXByb3NwZWN0aW5nJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPWNydHN1cnZleSZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1MT05HX1BBLVJPTi0yMDI0MTEwOFcDc3BjQgpnLITtLWdSxilkUhRkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbVgEAAAAAA~~)

I'm reaching out because you're an informed patriot who cares about this country. The situation is urgent, and your response will only take a few moments.

CRT has been around for decades.

Feminist academics used to make arguments about "unpacking their invisible knapsacks of privilege." However, at the time, CRT was rejected for being "just _another_ crazy fringe academic theory."

**Today, the situation is much different ...and much worse.**

_Now_ , CRT infiltrates our schools and workplaces. It's not so laughable for you to have to sit in a meeting or seminar that explains to you your "invisible knapsack of privilege."

To speak up often means you can lose your job, or worse...

What's frightening for the direction of this country is that federal agencies have labeled some parents who oppose CRT as "domestic terrorists."

**This should NOT be happening in America.**

Hillsdale College--where I work--teaches the principles of liberty and justice that our country was founded on. And we're fighting to defend them on the battlefield of education.

**But we need YOUR help to defend our constitutional principles against CRT.** Sharing your opinion with us is one important way you can take action.

**Complete your National Opinion Survey on Critical Race Theory now:[https://info.hillsdale.edu/crt-survey/](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/spe9wYUPNXrako_nviNqww~~/AARclAA~/RgRpEIOKP0SdaHR0cHM6Ly9pbmZvLmhpbGxzZGFsZS5lZHUvY3J0LXN1cnZleS8_c2M9MDE1NzJOMDI0MkwwQURPREVFUyZ1dG1fc291cmNlPXByb3NwZWN0aW5nJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPWNydHN1cnZleSZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1MT05HX1BBLVJPTi0yMDI0MTEwOFcDc3BjQgpnLITtLWdSxilkUhRkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbVgEAAAAAA~~)**

When you participate in our survey, you will help us gather the information needed to address CRT's threats to our liberty and find solutions that restore our constitutional principles.

When you finish taking your survey, please consider giving a generous gift.

Our NATIONAL EDUCATION CAMPAIGN--which includes _Imprimis_ , free online courses, and K-12 curriculum and school network--educates millions of citizens about our Constitution and founding principles.

We need your help expanding these initiatives. And because we do not take a single penny of taxpayer funding, even indirectly through federal and state student grants loans--all of our work is funded by informed patriots like you.

Thank you for joining us in our mission on behalf of liberty.

Warm regards,

Bill Gray, '01
Vice President
Institutional Advancement
Hillsdale College

P.S. Your participation and support are essential in our fight against CRT. Please complete the survey and consider a generous gift to help us continue our educational mission at this secure link:

[**https://info.hillsdale.edu/crt-survey/**](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/spe9wYUPNXrako_nviNqww~~/AARclAA~/RgRpEIOKP0SdaHR0cHM6Ly9pbmZvLmhpbGxzZGFsZS5lZHUvY3J0LXN1cnZleS8_c2M9MDE1NzJOMDI0MkwwQURPREVFUyZ1dG1fc291cmNlPXByb3NwZWN0aW5nJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPWNydHN1cnZleSZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1MT05HX1BBLVJPTi0yMDI0MTEwOFcDc3BjQgpnLITtLWdSxilkUhRkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbVgEAAAAAA~~)
---
| |
---

Restore Our Nation
---
_PO Box 1177 Fort Collins, CO 80522_
_[**unsubscribe**](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/yb-R5u2O7SBiezgjPdLQlQ~~/AARclAA~/RgRpEIOKP0RpaHR0cHM6Ly9vcHRvdXQucmVzdG9yZS1vdXItbmF0aW9uLmNvbS91bnN1YnNjcmliZS8_Yj1SZXN0b3JlLU91ci1OYXRpb24tT2ZmZXJzJmU9ZHB3aWxsaXM2NyUyNTQwZ21haWwuY29tVwNzcGNCCmcshO0tZ1LGKWRSFGRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tWAQAAAAA)_ | _[manage data preferences](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/PMVS9KiehvwSDjYqjk-cEw~~/AARclAA~/RgRpEIOKP0QraHR0cHM6Ly9yZXN0b3JlLW91ci1uYXRpb24uY29tL3ByaXZhY3kuaHRtbFcDc3BjQgpnLITtLWdSxilkUhRkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbVgEAAAAAA~~)_

clean_body: I'm reaching out because you're an informed patriot who cares about this country. This sponsored message from Reclaiming American Freedom is on behalf of Hillsdale College | | | | | Dear Fellow American, I'm reaching out because you're an informed patriot who cares about this country. The situation is urgent, and your response will only take a few moments. CRT has been around for decades. Feminist academics used to make arguments about "unpacking their invisible knapsacks of privilege." However, at the time, CRT was rejected for being "just another crazy fringe academic theory." Today, the situation is much different ...and much worse. Now , CRT infiltrates our schools and workplaces. It's not so laughable for you to have to sit in a meeting or seminar that explains to you your "invisible knapsack of privilege." To speak up often means you can lose your job, or worse... What's frightening for the direction of this country is that federal agencies have labeled some parents who oppose CRT as "domestic terrorists." This should NOT be happening in America. Hillsdale College--where I work--teaches the principles of liberty and justice that our country was founded on. And we're fighting to defend them on the battlefield of education. But we need YOUR help to defend our constitutional principles against CRT. Sharing your opinion with us is one important way you can take action. Complete your National Opinion Survey on Critical Race Theory now: When you participate in our survey, you will help us gather the information needed to address CRT's threats to our liberty and find solutions that restore our constitutional principles. When you finish taking your survey, please consider giving a generous gift. Our NATIONAL EDUCATION CAMPAIGN--which includes Imprimis , free online courses, and K-12 curriculum and school network--educates millions of citizens about our Constitution and founding principles. We need your help expanding these initiatives. And because we do not take a single penny of taxpayer funding, even indirectly through federal and state student grants loans--all of our work is funded by informed patriots like you. Thank you for joining us in our mission on behalf of liberty. Warm regards, Bill Gray, '01 Vice President Institutional Advancement Hillsdale College P.S. Your participation and support are essential in our fight against CRT. Please complete the survey and consider a generous gift to help us continue our educational mission at this secure link: | | Reclaiming American Freedom PO Box 461 Fort Collins, CO 80522

Friend, none of this would be possible without your generous support. Every prayer, every vote, every survey response, and every generous donation have been instrumental in our victory last night. I mean it when I say it - THANK YOU! But the work is far from over. As I watch returns continue to trickle in, our key races to keep the majority are looking very, _very_ close – and some could even wind up in a recount. clean_body: Every minute counts: Democrats will stop at nothing to steal this election, but with your help, we can ensure every legal vote is counted accurately in Arizona. | URGENT UPDATE RE: ARIZONA SENATE ELECTION Fellow Conservative, We're down to the wire in Arizona, and I need your help . As you know, campaign for US Senate is still UNDECIDED. With every passing minute, the stakes are getting higher, and we can't afford to lose this crucial seat. Just yesterday, , but we can't celebrate yet. We need to ensure that is counted accurately in Arizona. The Democrats will stop at nothing to steal this election, and we can't let them get away with it. That's why I'm counting on you, Fellow Conservative, to to support Kari Lake's teams and help nationwide. name: Kevin Lincoln
subject: Not over yet!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY KEVIN LINCOLN FOR CONGRESS
body:
Only half the votes for my battleground House race in CA-9 are in, and as you can see, it’s a dead heat.

| [](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zbu2a76b72542g8qjrt9r7s1rbfc8l3g9udrspm0_rp22sh2s8i6epj4c1gecpj4shh2bs)
Fellow American,

Across the nation, we’re celebrating huge wins for President Trump and a new Republican Senate Majority.

**But the battle for the House isn’t over yet.**

Only half the votes for my battleground House race in CA-9 are in, _**and as you can see, it’s a dead heat.**_

[](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zlnink4micrksfmblm5i3rgh0i5kkmp1i28eemio_rp22sh2s8i6epj4c1gecpj4shh2bs)
This seat is needed to ensure we have a strong House Majority that can pass the Republican agenda, [**and we still need your help.**](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zelnkqq41uc47jopbhvnipfv3vq8ak13mr2f9l18_rp22sh2s8i6epj4c1gecpj4shh2bs)

We are dispatching field teams to cure mail ballots and make sure our votes are counted. And if necessary, we need to be able to take action. [**But we also need your help to pay for this.**](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zj1aho58pdddpd5epmpbbf2oknkgefat66fs6aj0_rp22sh2s8i6epj4c1gecpj4shh2bs)

We don’t have armies of left-wing lawyers like the Democrats. [**Can you pitch in to help us secure the vote in this still-undecided critical race?**](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zcv8kkchjp14bac1toeq03ceutph5jlj671elu80_rp22sh2s8i6epj4c1gecpj4shh2bs)

This election isn’t over yet, and we don’t want to lose a single vote - it's that close.

Please continue supporting our victory!

**Kevin Lincoln**

[** Help us Protect the Vote ➞ **](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2z20b37m47d2m3iv81siqf90gp2o8fq38u8a7njg8_rp22sh2s8i6epj4c1gecpj4shh2bs)

| **Meet Kevin Lincoln
**
My name is Kevin Lincoln, and my story, is the Valley’s story. I am a 3rd generation Stocktonian, raising a 4th generation in the city I love. I’m the grandson of Mexican immigrants, and my stepfather is an Army veteran.

Like many Stocktonians, my family has overcome economic setbacks. My own father struggled to survive on these streets, and was at one time, homeless. But I never let adversity define me. I joined the Marines and served in the White House Military Office, assigned to Marine One. I graduated college while working full-time and raising a family. I’ve worked in corporate America and served our community as a pastor, and as a non-profit leader.

I’m blessed with a beautiful wife, Bonnie, and two amazing children. I’ve lived a life of service, but the greatest honor of my life has been giving back to my community, through serving as Mayor of my hometown, Stockton.
[** SEND A MARINE TO CONGRESS ➞ **](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zj35v0phpp0j3v6ceinepnljqghqer2ivs2dgt6g_rp22sh2s8i6epj4c1gecpj4shh2bs)
---

_Kevin Lincoln is a former member of the U.S. Marine Corps. Use of his military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Marine Corps or the Department of Defense._

# Friday, November 8, 2024
---|---
**Good morning!** On this day in 1917, the _[Ferguson Forum](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0zzFyhCAUgOHTQKfDQwUsKNLYbdqUDshDmSDsALvu3j6T9LnA_8_3dVtvJsSQ9k9zom4vn1fO-NgBdEytzoT4XkfqtGVuBooa5ABMiFlN9NB-9JYpMYGws1B8sB6NBeW2wW0Cp4EG_RsDYApgEiB7aZ2QUnppJUhjDBkZutBy6duBDV-m-vjOTyz9lk8a9dHavZLhg_CF8OW6rr7Vw-QUQ8I-l53w5TDJ2Zy_CV8wtRKwEr54LPuj5tT5XB4nLdrdrxBjqEKSke2nCfHv0P4lPzX_CQAA___Z3Fp7)_ , a weekly political newspaper spearheaded by Governor James E. Ferguson, began publication in Temple. It remained in circulation until 1935. Move over, Starbucks! For the first time ever, Whataburger is [offering holiday-themed treats](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kL2OqzAQhZ_GdEaeicNA4eI27nLbLdGAh2DFgQicOHn7Fem3Pkff-fm59BeOKS7X_3wXl9_T2qNBqwG0afvAMX16WwU3mNBBJQ7oBKZpuvZczU6GYDscbXcarEzMiDJ153EyoW1GbKmK7oABmBbg3ADVNISGiCYaCIiZlTUSYl63Os-S5c37lD7rS7Z6XO9VcnPOj12d_in0Cn0ppb7tnA9RoV-k7Ap9WkdOCv0RpdADKPSGDvvMmYfndpVNx10_l5d8p-pFip7XFAN_dNjicjsw1ebCo8SU4t6QsuZ655i-NfKfv7wc_gYAAP___clk3A), with white chocolate peppermint-flavored shakes and coffee available now through Dec. 30. Let us know what you think if you try them out! We're sponsored today by [GACW](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx80L2uozAQhuGrMV2QxwYbChcrRemy7ZZowAOMZAOynb-7X5H-nP6Zkd7v3324Iwfelr8YyZX3vA9KquYCcJHd4JHDZ2gq70bpe6jIgdUgjem7tlqd0Rq6flKkey1n2ZserR6tbyct1Wjmit35DEB2AK0BW9vRG2vtbEcLFhFFI8lz2VNdVir0xjyHz_6kVE97rIJbSzmE_iPUTagbb0_KpV7CPmJATtMn8OYpvVaikM-L0-nbo8Qh7480kdBXH9OC0yvg5nlbDlxIKHOKSJ4fUejrgalslPLKB3Ty0kg7fOuFMmXDSPkXM0ALumta0_aqSs4fLw6Bs7GikUtEDt-O8uOwT6f-BwAA___QKHnC), offering a unique investment opportunity.

## Texas Latinos Back Trump, Cruz
Donald Trump and Ted Cruz both [performed better in Texas](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx00LGysyAUBOCnwU6HgwpaUPwNXf72ls5BIDKD4sBJTN7-TtLffufb2f25LTeMKR73_7h7Ta-QF8HF0AK0fFocxvRehsZpy90MjdegeuBSztPYbDpMIwxOzlJYcMDHYLkKXgzSTr1ce2yi_mAAfAIYJahOWSeVUkFZBQoR2cC9i5RLR5sn_8Ia0js_fenWvDdJb0RnZf0_JgwT5rqubn9XPPCgfMT8CTFhDn9VJkzKKyYmDBaKa_JMmJoftLVftn1m8qW2VB772cI8zZ8h3bmdTdHuvGJKsUrFBn7fMaZvPf35x1OL3wAAAP__E1djEw) than the last time they were on the ballot, largely due to increased support from the state’s growing Latino population. In 2020, Donald Trump won Texas with 52.1% of the vote but [trailed Biden by 17 points](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kLGOwyAQRL8Gd1iACSQFxTXucu2V1tq7xEjYRGYdJ_f1p6S__s1o5v1chyuknNbbNywU-BnLYJSxUmupzgNCyq_BNhhGhRfdUNC-08q5y_nUzAF8NDbiiEbF2KG2duxIxYikRocWmhTeZVqrs9Ynp33rR3Te--hHrz0ACKsIE5et5ZmYnlBjfpUHbe1UliaHmfleRfclTC9MfxxHG8vTuLnslcv6hoTpVzqqMP0nLjNwWkuVSJEmlrzty11O2_4r407voxJpSjU9SCItZdqAPyxwswW8HynnVJ0XVt0WSPmzg_8V8wjmLwAA__9-42mq) among Latino voters. In 2024, Trump enjoyed a 10-point advantage among Texas Latinos, helping secure a victory in the state with 56.3% of the vote. Ted Cruz narrowly defeated Beto O’Rourke by 2.6 points in 2018, receiving 35% of the Latino vote. But he beat Colin Allred by more than 8 points, with [support from 52%](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kDHPnDAMQH9N2ILiwEfCkKEL23XtiAxxwGogiERw9-8rbu_2ZFnPT_7zGl_IkfflN27kyjukUSvdSgCp7OiR42dsK-8m5XuoyIFpQHVdb3-q1UFjNFmLDYVpNnPT6dlCH0If7GTboCt2jwxAWYCfDkxtJt8ZY4KZDBhEFK0izyWddVmp0BtziJ900VnPaauiW0s5smh-CT0IPdz3XXuMEfNOd35WhB4eFHo4UuTC84PPTaEHAKEHZYQeVs4H7jzLKxU6s8wrhyJLkmUlefKyFhkJL94X-W2QnrY0n1iyzER_nznu-abzsVen88fNMXLujGjVsiHHb2757_8up_8FAAD__42MdEE) of Latino voters. The shifting voting patterns in the state [mirror national trends](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx00L2OpDAQBOCnMZmRbX4MgYNLnM2lF6IGN0PrjD3CvTDz9is23_iTqkr17zE9gCKl51_Y0fF7zZNRppVaSzVMASh-prYKblZh1BU6bRut-n4cumpzqgnrOHSAbRdCA9CqgL1e7DKu3dyCrcjdYVqrQeuu17a2c-ittaudrbYAIFqFgTgfNW_I-Iayxk8-8aiXvFfRbcyvIpo_wnhh_HVd9f8CfKMwPuFVhPExLxCF8XeVMF5rYbyyNwBTyvLMjEeRZcuXLPRMtNICiWXZaGXJ-YIjyJATxCD5-NpfkpJM-cR9xkN2EiMuTDkJ46vDhddFMVLprWjVcweKP1P51-9OZ74DAAD__55Xcmg). In 2020, 32% of self-identified Hispanics supported Trump nationwide, while 45% voted for him in 2024.
## School Bond Elections Overview
While Houston ISD’s failed [$4.4 billion bond proposal](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kDGP3CAQRn8N7mbFYDB2QZHG3aVNaWEzPpNgsGDO2f330aa_-vv09PR-fSwfPqaYP3_6kxw_97IoqTQgghyX4GN6LboLbpVhwo4c2h7lMEyj6Q637cqgpEHTJOVuNI1GqTAEUmhlmPYuujcMUY6IZkD7sGsYrLW7XS1a773QkkLkUh98ENPTtz29yk31sZWzS-5gvprofwg1CzX7dcP-vQg1X6WxUPNRvhqXDLEFaF8X1ZiZcqDMcMY_BGdM1CDExjVuDJX8xuB3pgp3YaoNKv2mjUFrWGNKsWRYSw6Qyw1GqBmNwam3k1BzV124_r5PbbBCy8_Tx_RflL8tdzv1LwAA___eIHFN) was the largest in state history, voters in other parts of the state weighed in on several bond packages with impressive price tags of their own. ## Texas-Oklahoma Border Redrawn
A pump station built on the Texas-Oklahoma border at Lake Texoma [created a legal quandary](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx00DGPozAQBeBfYzojezAeU7i4hi7XXonGeADrTIjACcn--lUibbnVK5706en9uwwXSjld57-0si_PaRtAgZFaS-WGSCm_BlNFH1TsdMVeY6OVtZ1rq8VHxGhbbiej3BSjDa7RLWKElpRukKvk35jWymndWo01hmgRccKAGolIGMUxlW2vy8KFn3RM-bU9eK_Hba2yX0q5HaL5I6AX0J_nWf9ftvu7FNDTXtKYWUB_5fMQ0OdtpCyg_0A_Kb847CTX-zFmPmRI88y7gB5cK4NFUq4h2TUOpJlCkJ2GIMEwdjZ2EY2rdh9vZ8o5HRaFUfNKKX_mlV__enj4DgAA__8Njmoz) that was resolved by redrawing the boundary between the two states. The pump station, which previously straddled the state line, supplies water to North Texas. Officials discovered that the station was inadvertently transporting invasive zebra mussels across state lines, a violation of federal law. To resolve the issue, the Red River Boundary Commission [moved the border](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kbGy4yAMRb_G7uQB4hincLGNu7ftlhlhZJt5gDJAwsvf75Ctt9W954w0-vN1_0LnXTx-Y6Cl_Ox8V0KNICWI-W7R-fd97O1ihL3JnhapL1JM022-9ueC132b5TibG-5K7UaJbdvMqNRNGD1eVO-WJpNSzFJeJ6kHbeyktd610VIjYjcKsq5wGspJhX4w7_7NL0rDxqH3y1nKI3eXX51aO7XWWofD8_ApDge_OrWWk-Dw3Kk1Us2dWh-JcoZEnjBTG7QVWswvCoZSYxoPHqOFjUNwOTuOlMA8t28XjxMDJLIJa4Z_Vf72eHJAyAULgeFntJjeQDE_k4sHZNwJmi_T9kwEFQsl2DlBqQzBee84QuRUzo8y5uEswfdpsY_a0jzpbhRHQOc_p5f__uK1qL8BAAD__3X2kt0) so that the pump station is entirely within Texas. _“This redrawn boundary line will ensure that millions of north Texans' water comes from a secure source in Texas,”_ said Texas General Land Office Commissioner Dr. Dawn Buckingham.

In 2020, Donald Trump won Texas with 52.1% of the vote but among Latino voters. In 2024, Trump enjoyed a 10-point advantage among Texas Latinos, helping secure a victory in the state with 56.3% of the vote. Ted Cruz narrowly defeated Beto O’Rourke by 2.6 points in 2018, receiving 35% of the Latino vote. But he beat Colin Allred by more than 8 points, with of Latino voters. The shifting voting patterns in the state . In 2020, 32% of self-identified Hispanics supported Trump nationwide, while 45% voted for him in 2024. School Bond Elections Overview While Houston ISD’s failed was the largest in state history, voters in other parts of the state weighed in on several bond packages with impressive price tags of their own. In nearby Waller ISD, voters to build four new schools and purchase buses, while they rejected an $11 million bond for technology improvements. Round Rock ISD voters approved to fund a new Career and Technical Education facility, technology improvements, and fine arts programs. In Allen ISD, voters approved to build a controversial multipurpose athletic complex and fund technology upgrades. In Bexar County’s East Central ISD, voters rejected a tax rate increase and a , while a tax rate increase and were voted down in Frisco ISD. Texas-Oklahoma Border Redrawn A pump station built on the Texas-Oklahoma border at Lake Texoma that was resolved by redrawing the boundary between the two states. The pump station, which previously straddled the state line, supplies water to North Texas. Officials discovered that the station was inadvertently transporting invasive zebra mussels across state lines, a violation of federal law. To resolve the issue, the Red River Boundary Commission so that the pump station is entirely within Texas. “This redrawn boundary line will ensure that millions of north Texans' water comes from a secure source in Texas,” said Texas General Land Office Commissioner Dr. Dawn Buckingham. body:
The Federal Reserve cut [interest rates by 0.25 percentage points](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kLGO4yAYBp8GOiL4jY1TUFzjLtdeaXHwOeaETQR_csrbr7L91iPNSPPntt5CLvm8_w4HPG91JU1WGaP0vKaQy3u1MvkNIAwS3rjB6MkNbpC7T5hG7fQUxznhGkIImG2wJkaLqGmS2X9sxujZkKbRXEjTMMJhBll9jVpYjZS5tsu_mk_esZV3faFdYj1k8Tvzo4vhl6BF0MI79lzKhwla_j57PtG7oMVe3ezIqg0JLRTV0NFeUPHJXeWT0dBZtcDoaqtN8Q7VEeuZFOcDghbZfHr8z6XkPjlh9f0I-Tsk-acpL09fAQAA__-kmWSE) Thursday to a range of 4.5 to 4.75 percent, marking its second consecutive cut after a two-year campaign of rate hikes to combat inflation.

| | |

# Friday, November 8, 2024
---|---
**Good Morning**! On this day in 1887, [John Henry “Doc” Holliday](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx00LFugzAQxvGnMZvR-Qw4DB66sKVrRwT2Ob7KiSPsBPH2FVXXzj_p-6T_13W-Lpz4cftc7mRryDMCdlIpCZfZL5yOuWu8DURIuiGrjFYwGG10E-24jgYC9j0OYxjBrSMpcn4Y9EpGedOwPdeUgotCwF61CKh7MnQh7GB0IDogzzVv7XfmR40U0pHftLUu35tkY63PIvSHwEngtO97G7nUvB2nC5xq5CL9ckh-yD8ROPnsZMwp8SmeqcgcZH2ttLlXyoVLs1n_3DklLoMRHdzuC6ffy_pfgrfFnwAAAP__yBdfLA), a well-known gunslinger and occasional dentist, died of tuberculosis at a sanitarium in Glenwood Springs, Colorado. He's famous for his role in the shootout at the O.K. Corral in Tombstone, Arizona, and his lasting friendship with Wyatt Earp. This afternoon, be on the lookout for our second edition of _The Feel Good Flyover,_ filled with positive news meant to inspire and lift your day. Today's edition includes tales of a life-saving puppy, a caring grandson, and a big doughnut tipper—don't miss it! Today, we have a new sponsor, [Cru Inner City](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kc2u0zAQRp9msmtkz-THXWQBN3jFZQMIsbLceJIa2XGVOIW-PUoVEEK6C0ueM9IZf55vr-bV-uDn6ZON3OUxGRRYnaQ8CWWc9eFhqsJ1IzMyFdzJlqRoWmqpuHaSqpaoJWUvth7UWDWiVogVjVhL61Thu90mpVASBdayRIFUc8uKsRLnQUAl2PmclvJH8nO-8hge6c5LOaRYhO6a820FegeoAfXk71wOy1amZQLUQ5ozz_loAOptBdQ87y0bb9ZP8w7wOApQnwF1vdeoznUr99vzfadL-pXGkO5cXnMMQPrlMLwkx0D9lw_v9cfvgE1Mzgagfh95mvyYARsH1P81NsNe_WcFbLYczZq2Zdh1R8oDR3Z-i0C985PPNhz4T4h_xz_5kZv6W1qz8fPwMF8_mzEFUyydu_30Ifi1aaESU7Q-PP8yv7Xbe4e_AwAA___cP5-u). Check out their Box of Love® Outreach program in today's edition.
|

## Fed Cuts Rates for Second Time
The Federal Reserve cut [interest rates by 0.25 percentage points](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kLGO4yAYBp8GOiL4jY1TUFzjLtdeaXHwOeaETQR_csrbr7L91iPNSPPntt5CLvm8_w4HPG91JU1WGaP0vKaQy3u1MvkNIAwS3rjB6MkNbpC7T5hG7fQUxznhGkIImG2wJkaLqGmS2X9sxujZkKbRXEjTMMJhBll9jVpYjZS5tsu_mk_esZV3faFdYj1k8Tvzo4vhl6BF0MI79lzKhwla_j57PtG7oMVe3ezIqg0JLRTV0NFeUPHJXeWT0dBZtcDoaqtN8Q7VEeuZFOcDghbZfHr8z6XkPjlh9f0I-Tsk-acpL09fAQAA__-kmWSE) Thursday to a range of 4.5 to 4.75 percent, marking its second consecutive cut after a two-year campaign of rate hikes to combat inflation. Fed Chair Jerome Powell deflected questions about [President-elect Trump's potential impact](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx00LGuozAQheGngW7QeDCYFC62ocu2WyID4-CNwZE9BOXtV9n-9p-Ojv4_9-nuQgzH47fb2YpPEyFpUApwmFYX4mfS9Wo9M3Fbs1WmVdib1rT1Zru5W9Cv1C-aVY-D1l13c-hvc6vNMMx1sN81pXBQhNSphpDajg0PTBpvC1YaeQ2ScvM3hUM29vGT3pybJe11tJvIq1Ttr4rGisbrupoiLksO83nw11Q0FknLE3aXnywgaXUfcCW4A8rmMhfILJmdgPPCGSSf-6vAOyyS8leCT8tZQM58FJAEsjF4Xisae1TKGByoznZ9XSHGUHpTaXzsLsT_D-WnYm9L_wIAAP__-vdvBA) on the economy, stating, _"We don't guess, we don't speculate, and we don't assume."_ He also [firmly rejected the possibility](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0z7GOhCAQgOGngU7DDOhoQXEN3V57pWFxWLmgGJdbs29_8fqr_-RL_q_bdPMpp-3x6Ve2NZYJFZoGoFHDNPuU35ORs43MyFqyBdKgetKk5WLJB-rNPNBIIeqh9x13kYnuiNBHGmWylwagBkCFHbSoUHdMPDAaNQYljOI51XK03yVtdeGY3-XFRxvKKrNdat2fQn8IdALdeZ5t2O7higLdRQt0AAKdIoFuLyfn3NTjZ93bpa5ZHnbez5RzevYkjHqsPuU_uv63-rL4GwAA___u2FLx) of stepping down if asked by President-elect Trump, responding with a simple "No" during Thursday's press conference and noting it is "not permitted under the law" for a president to fire or demote Fed leaders. The rate decision comes as the economy shows mixed signals, with October's jobs report showing only 12,000 new jobs added, the lowest in nearly four years, though the numbers were affected by hurricanes and the Boeing strike.
## GOP Expand Senate Majority, House Still Undecided
The AP has called Pennsylvania’s U.S. Senate race for Republican David McCormick, increasing the [GOP majority to 53 seats](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kL_uozAQhJ8GOkf24n8ULq5Jl2uvRIu9JHtnbAQWEW9_SvpfORppNN_35zE9kDOX529cKbSlTiBBC6WE9FNCztek-xQWIqChp6DcoKR1gxv6V1DS-DgmrzVYp6xHSMqOuOASwdJieg6fNaWkVyDBqBtIGAw58gRajlF2WlLiVvfb38qlvWjJVz1pv8W69jm8WtuObvjVwb2DO26F3sen-oS9cczUwX2jUo4rn1gYxUEFG4kvBGWKjWsRCU9OYo2x7ivHfyLiQZfw1qJ3ChKhnA3MJvlkZ-21Hq2fzdDvIW1vzpkP6zotnyty_h5rP4k6A_wPAAD__81laKw) with races in Arizona and Nevada still undecided. Democratic Sen. Bob Casey, Pennsylvania’s longest-serving Democrat in the Senate, has yet to concede, citing over 100,000 remaining ballots, including provisional, military, and overseas votes, still to be counted. In the House, [Republicans hold a narrow](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0jzFvgzAQRn-NvQWdzybQwUMXtnTtiC72AVcdOAIrVf59RfZu3_C-J73v23gjUdnmL1o51qmMCBguzl2gHzOJvsZgc5yYkb3l6Drv4Nr5ztslhmvfOgqhhdASTDndU485MxHwPWCyEk-bc9A7BGxdg4C-5Y57xgAfCUwAzlLL3vwU2erCk77Kk_cmldVqXGp9HMZ_GhwMDqycqpTtOKVNXXgR1ZM0OMy88U5qcNjoZN7T7jE_fkVVjmtnAswryftg63-pz4h_AQAA___iZlUf) 213-200 majority despite Democrats successfully defending three key seats in Nevada, with 22 races still yet to be called.
## 43 Lab Monkeys Escape Research Facility
[Forty-three young female monkeys](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kLtu6zAQBb9G6miQS_GhgsVt3Pm2KYU1tbQ2oURBZGz47wOlT3lwgMFgPm7TDTnz9viPK4WWygQSBqGUkH6akfN7Gvo5JCIg3VNQTitpnXa6X0LyVvo4oFX27kabohu9sWjRg3Xo557DSVNKegUSjLqABG3IkScY5BhlN0iauZXj8ll4awul_C5POi6xrH0OS2t77fS_Dq4dXHHf6FXP6xxH45ipg-tati96V0E14k4C876geNBGlauo5bstIuJRMm8orEXnwY3o8E7GRZN8muVotDajth77I8z7i3Pmal03yMeKnH9l2l9xngF-AgAA___oVmTT) escaped Wednesday night from Alpha Genesis, a biomedical research facility in Beaufort County, South Carolina after a new employee left an enclosure unsecured. Police launched a search using [thermal imaging cameras](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kLFuhDAQRL_GdEbrNRymcJGG7tKmRMYsxyYGI9s5xN9Hl9TpRjPS6Ol93Me748D7491tZMsSRwRspFISzDg7DtfYVLNdiJB0RVZ1WsGt052uVotqaj3109Lf5g6caSZtWgBcjMHWzH3F9vWmFBiFgK2qEVC31JEhbKD3IBqgmUtM9Wfkvay0hCs-KdU-blWwaylHFvpN4CBwOM-z9lPe6cyvXeDwigKHLe5fdGVJ2buDZI7fZZXepRh4dzJRJpf8KhfnOXC55BEDe5J_tcChSnY-Tg6B860TDTw2x-EXofyn5GnxJwAA___CzWSB) and food-baited traps while warning residents to secure their homes and avoid interacting with the monkeys, which have never been used for testing and are reportedly too young to carry disease. The incident continues a pattern of security issues at Alpha Genesis, which manages a 3,500-monkey colony on South Carolina's "Monkey Island." Federal officials fined Alpha Genesis in 2018 for previous escapes and violations, with [additional breakouts](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx00DGP4yAUBOBfgzsseGDjFBTXuMu1V1ov-BFzh8ECEsv__pTVttt90kij0fy5L3cMMaTnb9zJNp8XEKC5lFxMy4ohXovuVuuJgFRHVholxWiUUd1m1SRvIw0T-ZXIDyi1Hz2iUQ_zgFWrLthPm5RikiBgkD0IUAMZmgi0uDnBtKA1tFz6vzmktpGPV35T6V3eu2i31o7K1C8GM4P5PM8-PVyis35yBvOHDOZX5d_ac_pHV-VUHR7EMR4b8iclqqHyQpWwuI17dCGGdvGaX23jDkuOISEvLqE0N2FMV-x6nCHGUEfDtHjuGOLXqPbTSW8L_wMAAP__4ilrTQ) occurring in May 2024 and 2016.

He also of stepping down if asked by President-elect Trump, responding with a simple "No" during Thursday's press conference and noting it is "not permitted under the law" for a president to fire or demote Fed leaders. Police launched a search using and food-baited traps while warning residents to secure their homes and avoid interacting with the monkeys, which have never been used for testing and are reportedly too young to carry disease. The incident continues a pattern of security issues at Alpha Genesis, which manages a 3,500-monkey colony on South Carolina's "Monkey Island." name: History Facts
subject: The war that lasted 2,000 years
body:
From 264 to 241 BCE and again from 218 to 201 BCE, the city-states of Rome and Carthage engaged in a pair of epic wars for control of the western Mediterranean region. # [The Third Punic War didn’t officially end until 1985 — 2,000 years after it started.](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=smmsft&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
### [World History](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=smmsft&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
[](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=smmsft&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
| F rom 264 to 241 BCE and again from 218 to 201 BCE, the city-states of Rome and Carthage engaged in a pair of epic wars for control of the western Mediterranean region. Although Rome was victorious in both cases and emerged as the dominant power, Carthage's continued prosperity remained problematic to hawkish members of the Roman Senate. As a result, the Third Punic War was launched in 149 BCE to settle this zero-sum conflict between the two rivals, leading to the siege and razing of Carthage by 146 BCE. Considering that the city was utterly destroyed, and its survivors were sold into slavery, it's easy to see how the terms of a peace treaty failed to materialize between the conquerors and the conquered.
---
Two millennia later, that oversight represented a loose thread for representatives of Rome, the capital of the Republic of Italy, and Carthage, a suburb of the Tunisian capital of Tunis. So on February 5, 1985, two leaders, the mayor of Rome and the honorary mayor of Carthage (which was largely absorbed into Tunis except some ruins preserved as an archaeological site), [met](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=smmsft&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) at the historic city to sign the type of peace agreement that had been neglected by their ancient predecessors. The treaty, which pledged the "exchange of knowledge and the establishment of common information, cultural and artistic programs," was ostensibly part of a larger endeavor to strengthen ties between Italy and Tunisia, and was undoubtedly engineered to generate favorable publicity for the participants. Regardless of the motivation, this innocuous signing carried major historical ramifications, as it brought a formal conclusion to a war that had been frozen in time since the last of the fighting on the north African coast 2,131 years earlier.
[](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=smmsft&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) | [](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=smmsft&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)

## The seeding of Carthaginian soil with salt is likely a myth.
According to modern lore, the destruction of Carthage was accompanied by the salting of its soil to ensure nothing would ever grow again on its cursed grounds. While this would seemingly be a decisive punishment for one of the Roman Republic's most formidable foes, there's nothing in ancient Roman or Greek historical sources to indicate it actually happened. [According to](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=smmsft&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) modern scholars, this particular interpretation of events entered academic circles in the 19th century, and was repeated in historical texts until it became embedded in the realm of common knowledge. But while it fits in with other dramatic elements of the Third Punic War, including the narrative of the Carthaginian general's wife choosing a fiery death instead of surrendering to the Romans, it seems the oft-told tale of Carthage's salted furrows should be taken with a heavy grain of, well, salt.

clean_body: From 264 to 241 BCE and again from 218 to 201 BCE, the city-states of Rome and Carthage engaged in a pair of epic wars for control of the western Mediterranean region. | F rom 264 to 241 BCE and again from 218 to 201 BCE, the city-states of Rome and Carthage engaged in a pair of epic wars for control of the western Mediterranean region. Although Rome was victorious in both cases and emerged as the dominant power, Carthage's continued prosperity remained problematic to hawkish members of the Roman Senate. As a result, the Third Punic War was launched in 149 BCE to settle this zero-sum conflict between the two rivals, leading to the siege and razing of Carthage by 146 BCE. Considering that the city was utterly destroyed, and its survivors were sold into slavery, it's easy to see how the terms of a peace treaty failed to materialize between the conquerors and the conquered. Two millennia later, that oversight represented a loose thread for representatives of Rome, the capital of the Republic of Italy, and Carthage, a suburb of the Tunisian capital of Tunis. So on February 5, 1985, two leaders, the mayor of Rome and the honorary mayor of Carthage (which was largely absorbed into Tunis except some ruins preserved as an archaeological site), at the historic city to sign the type of peace agreement that had been neglected by their ancient predecessors. The treaty, which pledged the "exchange of knowledge and the establishment of common information, cultural and artistic programs," was ostensibly part of a larger endeavor to strengthen ties between Italy and Tunisia, and was undoubtedly engineered to generate favorable publicity for the participants. Regardless of the motivation, this innocuous signing carried major historical ramifications, as it brought a formal conclusion to a war that had been frozen in time since the last of the fighting on the north African coast 2,131 years earlier. | Thank you for supporting our advertisers! | By the Numbers | | Year Rome became an empire 27 BCE | | Total years of warfare waged over the three Punic Wars 43 | Total years of warfare waged over the three Punic Wars 43 | | Perimeter (in kilometers) of defensive walls surrounding Carthage 34 | | Year the town of Carthage, Missouri, was founded 1842 | Year the town of Carthage, Missouri, was founded 1842 | | | Did you know? ---|---|--- The seeding of Carthaginian soil with salt is likely a myth. According to modern lore, the destruction of Carthage was accompanied by the salting of its soil to ensure nothing would ever grow again on its cursed grounds. While this would seemingly be a decisive punishment for one of the Roman Republic's most formidable foes, there's nothing in ancient Roman or Greek historical sources to indicate it actually happened. modern scholars, this particular interpretation of events entered academic circles in the 19th century, and was repeated in historical texts until it became embedded in the realm of common knowledge. But while it fits in with other dramatic elements of the Third Punic War, including the narrative of the Carthaginian general's wife choosing a fiery death instead of surrendering to the Romans, it seems the oft-told tale of Carthage's salted furrows should be taken with a heavy grain of, well, salt. name: Kevin Lincoln
subject: Too close to call!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY KEVIN LINCOLN FOR CONGRESS
body:
Only half the votes for my battleground House race in CA-9 are in, and as you can see, it’s a dead heat.

| [](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2ztsvdjdmkrd6003b2jiaj4mu4p0c6lu72maejfrg_rp22sh2s8i6epj4c1ge6p32chh2bs)
Fellow American,

Across the nation, we’re celebrating huge wins for President Trump and a new Republican Senate Majority.

**But the battle for the House isn’t over yet.**

Only half the votes for my battleground House race in CA-9 are in, _**and as you can see, it’s a dead heat.**_

[](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2z4li9cojol14mcmt08vuo6tcjr8ks8sn3tdfb4o0_rp22sh2s8i6epj4c1ge6p32chh2bs)
This seat is needed to ensure we have a strong House Majority that can pass the Republican agenda, [**and we still need your help.**](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zfn1qhi2oshhf7ilpt3l1n918kdhtoj1lrpuo9j8_rp22sh2s8i6epj4c1ge6p32chh2bs)

We are dispatching field teams to cure mail ballots and make sure our votes are counted. And if necessary, we need to be able to take action. [**But we also need your help to pay for this.**](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zel8hbbhh2c5kbn048stucs20e2vejcrdklf4058_rp22sh2s8i6epj4c1ge6p32chh2bs)

We don’t have armies of left-wing lawyers like the Democrats. [**Can you pitch in to help us secure the vote in this still-undecided critical race?**](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2z338rfo8jfcjr106lv79dltnq4gj1t0lmno8gveg_rp22sh2s8i6epj4c1ge6p32chh2bs)

This election isn’t over yet, and we don’t want to lose a single vote - it's that close.

Please continue supporting our victory!

**Kevin Lincoln**

[** Help us Protect the Vote ➞ **](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2za0s793hv3ip05ihnqbakkgho7o4bgjmgco702fg_rp22sh2s8i6epj4c1ge6p32chh2bs)

| **Meet Kevin Lincoln
**
My name is Kevin Lincoln, and my story, is the Valley’s story. I am a 3rd generation Stocktonian, raising a 4th generation in the city I love. I’m the grandson of Mexican immigrants, and my stepfather is an Army veteran.

Like many Stocktonians, my family has overcome economic setbacks. My own father struggled to survive on these streets, and was at one time, homeless. But I never let adversity define me. I joined the Marines and served in the White House Military Office, assigned to Marine One. I graduated college while working full-time and raising a family. I’ve worked in corporate America and served our community as a pastor, and as a non-profit leader.

I’m blessed with a beautiful wife, Bonnie, and two amazing children. I’ve lived a life of service, but the greatest honor of my life has been giving back to my community, through serving as Mayor of my hometown, Stockton.
[** SEND A MARINE TO CONGRESS ➞ **](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zskanm66rthn9l4h8002lrg96uai1fo3o2d0nua8_rp22sh2s8i6epj4c1ge6p32chh2bs)

name: Dominic Rapini
subject: Call to Action: Ranked Choice Voting Testimony
body:
Cut, past, send, be heard.

View this email in your browser (https://us16.campaign-archive.com/?e=8763c56857&u=ebcefd906ec01db66e79c886c&id=592fa88f50)
https://www.dominicforct.us

Permission granted to download and use all content in this email for print and digital

** The Next Attack on Connecticut Elections:
------------------------------------------------------------

** Ranked Choice Voting (RCV)
------------------------------------------------------------

Tomorrow morning, the Government Administration and Election Committee will host a public testimony session to consider Ranked Choice Voting. Governor Lamont made a campaign promise to promote this controversial voting system in partnership with the CT Independent Party.

I have compiled a comprehensive white paper on the subject to provide you with more information.

Here’s what you can do: Copy and paste my testimony into your own email and send it to the GAE Committee. Let’s overwhelm the system with our counterarguments and make our voices heard.
Download the Testimony PDF (https://drive.google.com/file/d/1u3ECAtZgyL1fsCVGbgMNWkASCKGLBqS4/view?usp=sharing)

** Send Email Tonight or Tomorrow Morning:
------------------------------------------------------------

** TO: Giovanni.Pinto@ct.gov
------------------------------------------------------------

** Testimony: Ranked Choice Voting is a Threat to the Integrity of Connecticut Elections
------------------------------------------------------------

(Cut and paste into a Word Document, or send a PDF)

One Voter, One Vote.

Connecticut is facing many challenges right now, but the way we vote should not be one of them. Proponents of Ranked Choice Voting (RCV) argue that it will make our elections fairer, more inclusive, and more democratic. But that claim falls apart under scrutiny. RCV is not just a different way to vote—it’s a radical departure from the principle that has been at the foundation of American elections for nearly two and a half centuries: One Voter, One Vote.

In Connecticut, we already have a robust electoral system. Six political parties can appear on the ballot, each with the opportunity to rally support, present their ideas, and make their case to the voters. In a traditional election, candidates compete for a simple, clear-cut victory: the candidate who receives the most votes wins. Every vote is counted with equal weight, and the outcome is decisive. But with RCV, this straightforward process becomes convoluted, diluting the principle of a single, direct vote for a single candidate.

Confusion and Complexity Harm Voters

RCV introduces unnecessary confusion into an already complex electoral process. Instead of casting a single vote, RCV asks voters to rank candidates in order of preference. This sounds simple in theory, but in practice, it burdens voters with the responsibility of evaluating every candidate on the ballot. Most people need help to form strong opinions about more than one or two candidates; asking them to rank a dozen in order of preference is unrealistic and even unfair. Studies show that many voters, unsure how to rank candidates they know little about, will leave these choices blank.

When voters don’t rank every candidate, it leads to a phenomenon known as “ballot exhaustion.” If all the candidates a voter has ranked are eliminated in earlier rounds of counting, that ballot is thrown out—it no longer counts. This effectively disenfranchises voters, silencing their voice in the final outcome of the election. A New York City primary in 2021, conducted with RCV, saw over 140,000 ballots discarded due to ballot exhaustion. That’s 140,000 people who went to the polls expecting their vote to matter—only to be effectively erased from the results.

Delays and Uncertainty Undermine Trust

RCV doesn’t just confuse voters; it also introduces delays and uncertainty into the process. With RCV, elections can require multiple rounds of counting, stretching the process over days or even weeks. In New York City’s 2021 primary, it took three weeks to declare a winner after eight rounds of counting. In Alaska’s 2022 primary, two weeks passed before the final outcome was clear. In an era when trust in elections is already precarious, RCV’s drawn-out process only feeds public skepticism.

An election system that can’t deliver swift, decisive results is a system that risks losing the confidence of the people it serves.

RCV Does Not Reflect the Will of the People

One of the most troubling aspects of RCV is that it can produce winners who lack a clear mandate from the electorate. Under RCV, it’s entirely possible for a candidate who receives the most first-choice votes to lose the election after lower-ranked votes are reallocated. This process allows candidates to win based on second, third, or even fourth-choice rankings rather than on direct support from most voters. The winner of an RCV election might only have a lukewarm plurality of support rather than the solid majority that a traditional election requires.

This undermines the legitimacy of the election. How can a candidate claim a mandate when most voters didn’t choose them as their first choice? RCV ultimately diminishes the power of each vote, muddying the results and diminishing public trust in the winner’s legitimacy.

RCV Is a Solution in Search of a Problem

RCV advocates often frame it as a form of “election reform,” but it’s a complicated fix for a problem that doesn’t exist. Connecticut’s current electoral system isn’t broken. We have a healthy array of candidates, competitive races, and a transparent, efficient voting process. RCV proponents argue that it will make elections “fairer” by giving lesser-known candidates a better shot at winning. But is that really what we want? RCV effectively lowers the bar for winning, allowing candidates with fringe ideas or minimal public support to succeed by siphoning off lower-ranked votes rather than earning genuine, first-choice support from most voters.

Elections are about making hard choices between candidates with competing visions. RCV blurs these choices, making it harder for voters to distinguish between severe contenders and marginal candidates. By allowing candidates to win based on second or third-choice rankings, RCV encourages mediocrity and muddles the distinction between frontrunners and those who don’t have a realistic chance of leading.

The Cost of Complexity Is Voter Disenfranchisement

It’s not just hypothetical: RCV has a proven record of increased ballot spoilage due to voter mistakes. The instructions for ranking candidates are more complicated than traditional voting, leading to more spoiled ballots—ballots that are tossed out because voters made mistakes while navigating this convoluted system. In practical terms, this means more Connecticut voters could see their voices go unheard.

The evidence is clear: RCV is a misguided, convoluted system that disenfranchises voters rather than empowers them. It violates the longstanding principle of One Voter, One Vote, which has guided American elections since the founding of this country. Connecticut’s leaders should focus on the real issues facing our state—not on experimenting with an election system that will make it harder for citizens to understand the process, trust the results, and feel confident that their vote truly counts.

Let’s not trade clarity for confusion, decisiveness for delay, or genuine representation for a false sense of choice. Ranked Choice Voting is not the way forward for Connecticut. It’s time to put this misguided experiment to rest and recommit to our traditional voting system's simplicity, integrity, and fairness.

Dominic Rapini, Branford, CT

name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: The Mountain Minute: 11/8/2024
body:
Long after ‘the call’ there’s work being done in election offices across Colorado
| | | | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
---
**PRESENTED BY[ROCKYMOUNTAINVOICE.COM](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
| | Hi folks,
What a week! While we're celebrating many victories across the country, there's still so much post-election news to cover.
At the suggestion from a reader, here’s a quick preview of what we’re covering today that you won’t want to miss. _Like this preview at the beginning? Don't like it? Hit reply and let us know your thoughts!_
🇺🇸 **Trump’s Major Comeback** – We’re diving into [Trump’s inspiring return](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), securing a historic second term with [Susie Wiles as his new Chief of Staff](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.). This win has fueled fresh momentum among MAGA supporters, moving from an anti-establishment stance to a full [counter-establishment movement](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.).
**❌ Ranked-Choice Voting Rejected** – Voters nationwide largely [rejected ranked-choice voting](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) proposals this Election Day. We break down the implications for future elections and what this means for the movement’s future in Colorado and beyond.
**🗳️ Colorado’s Close Races** – With races still [too close to call](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), Colorado’s political landscape is shifting, especially in [competitive districts](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) where the impact of local issues has never been clearer. Join us as we analyze where things stand.
We’re here to bring you the latest, every step of the way! This is the Mountain Minute for Friday, November 8, 2024.
– Heidi Ganahl
---
|
---
**TODAY'S TOP STORIES**
**[Long after ‘the call’ there’s work being done in election offices across Colorado](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY BRIAN PORTER, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
When the polls close and enough ballots are counted on Election Night, voters are familiar with news agencies “calling” a winner in a race.
“The call” is generally an estimation based on many factors, such as the percentage of votes counted against the anticipated total ballots cast, whether the margin is moving with each data release, if outstanding votes in a certain area might be a significant anomaly from the returned votes, and possibly a mathematical formula of what it may take to flip the race with the remaining outstanding votes. In other words, would a candidate with 35% of ballots remaining and only 20% of the vote need 90% of remaining ballots to favor him, a statistical improbability?
Once all of those considerations and more are satisfied, a “call” is made on the race, but it is important, some county clerks say, for voters to recognize the difference between the call of a race and the days before all ballots are counted.
**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
The Rocky Mountain Voice is a 501(c)(3) that exists to promote informed civic engagement and the principles of self-governance through enterprise reporting on public policy, politics, government, and culture throughout the state of Colorado. The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support!
[DONATE TO ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
|
---
**[Caraveo’s lead has declined by 0.83%, as Evans now behind by 2,096 votes](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* Democrat Yadira Caraveo’s lead over Republican Gabe Evans in Colorado’s 8th District has decreased to 0.76%, with Caraveo now ahead by just 2,096 votes as of two days after Election Day.
* Adams County, a Democratic stronghold, has counted 81.7% of its ballots, while Republican-leaning Weld County has counted 86.5%, leaving room for further shifts as remaining votes are counted.
* The race holds significance for Colorado’s congressional representation, with an Evans victory potentially resulting in a 4-4 split for the state in the U.S. House, impacting the overall House balance as Republicans seek a majority.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[By regaining seat held by party-flipper Priola, GOP appears to have staved off Dems’ supermajority in Senate](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [THE SUM & SUBSTANCE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* Colorado Democrats are on track to maintain a supermajority in the House but fall one seat short of a supermajority in the Senate, ensuring continued significant influence but with some limitations.
* Key races in both chambers, especially in El Paso and Weld Counties, remain close, with Republicans making gains in certain districts and flipping a seat previously held by a Democrat who switched parties.
* The Colorado Chamber of Commerce, which endorsed moderate candidates across both parties, believes that a balanced legislative approach may result from the election, focusing on pro-business policies and regulatory reforms.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[Across America on Election Night, voters mostly rejected ranked-choice voting measures](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [COLORADO POLITICS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* Ranked-choice voting (RCV) faced major setbacks in the 2024 election, with voters in states like Colorado, Idaho, Oregon, and Nevada rejecting state-level RCV initiatives and Missouri passing a ban on the system altogether.
* Despite these losses, RCV saw some local wins, including a strong endorsement in Washington, D.C., reflecting a divide where urban centers are more open to adopting the voting method compared to state-level or rural areas.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[MAGA vision: anti-establishment becomes counter-establishment](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [MIG REPORTS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* Trump supporters, galvanized by his recent popular vote win, are pushing for sweeping changes to dismantle what they view as a corrupt and elitist establishment.
* Many Americans see Trump’s success as a clear rebuke to the ruling class, with his supporters framing his leadership as a mission to restore traditional American values, patriotism, and economic opportunity.
* Sentiments online reflect a blend of hope and defiance, with language often invoking religious and battle metaphors, as supporters rally around a "MAGA" vision of reviving America’s cultural and political landscape.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[Trump announces Susie Wiles as Chief of Staff](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [THE DAILY CALLER](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* President-elect Donald Trump has appointed Susie Wiles, his 2024 campaign manager, as his Chief of Staff, marking her as the first woman to hold this position in U.S. history.
* Wiles played a crucial role in Trump's campaign victories in 2016, 2020, and 2024, earning her respect within the team for her strategic skill and leadership.
* Trump praised Wiles as “tough, smart, and innovative,” highlighting her commitment to the "Make America Great Again" agenda, while other potential candidates, such as Kevin McCarthy, confirmed they were not interested in the role.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[Justice: Trump’s re-election is the most inspiring political comeback of all time](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [THE FEDERALIST](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
_Trump’s landslide reflects a triumph over the lockdowns, inflation, and division that defined the Biden era._
_The Covid years were tough, and now they’re finally ending. In two weeks, I’ll have a health book coming out that in part explains the story of why the lockdowns were so devastating. At the same time, Trump’s election gives hope to aggressively address the issues outlined in the pages._
_Former President Donald Trump hit a grand slam Tuesday. Republicans finally reclaimed the Senate and the White House four years after America’s deadly lockdowns coincided with a year of political turmoil and ended in a riot at the Capitol. Trump’s legacy was almost diminished as an ex-president whose tenure was defined by cultural unrest and whose exit interrupted the smooth transfer of power._
**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**ELECTION MUST-READS 🗳️**
**[Western Slope SPEAR task force takes 28,500 illegal pills off the streets](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** **BY LINDY BROWNING | LOCAL | NOVEMBER 7** _Members of a multi-jurisdictional task force, known as SPEAR (Special Problem Enforcement and Response Task Force), have intercepted 28,500 hydrocodone pills, with a street value of $285,000._
**[Two arrested Mesa County women, including a postal worker, allegedly conspired in ballot theft case to test election](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** **BY LINDY BROWNING | STATE | NOVEMBER 7** _Two women have been arrested in connection with the theft of multiple ballots in Mesa County, District Attorney Dan Rubinstein announced Wednesday._ __
[**With win over Frisch, Jeff Hurd has held Colorado’s 3rd District for Republicans**](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) **BY LINDY BROWNING | STATE |****NOVEMBER 6** _Jeff Hurd’s telephone rang at about 3:30 p.m. Wednesday, a little more than 24 hours after polls closed on Election Day._ _On the other end of the phone was his opponent, Democrat Adam Frisch._ __
**Feel like you have a unique story or commentary to share?** We'd love to talk with you about becoming an RMV Contributor. Reply to this email (or reach out to [brian@rockymountainvoice.com](mailto:brian@rockymountainvoice.com?subject=Contributor%20Interest%20\(via%20Mountain%20Minute\))) or send us a message on social media to chat!
|
---
|
---
**🤔 DID YOU KNOW?** One might say that George Washington deserves the record for the **greatest margin of victory** by any American president. In the first presidential election in 1789, Washington claimed 100 percent of the electoral college—all 69 votes cast. But Washington had a unique advantage: he ran unopposed.

Here are [seven of the most decisive American presidential elections](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), ranked by their margins of victory from smallest to largest.
|
---
**CARTOON OF THE DAY**
|
---
**QUOTE OF THE DAY**
“One of the most horrifying and surprising evolutions we have witnessed among our widespread campus network is the rapid movement away from tolerating opposing ideas and respectful debate to the deployment of obscene bully tactics from the left.****” – Charlie Kirk
|

Once all of those considerations and more are satisfied, a “call” is made on the race, but it is important, some county clerks say, for voters to recognize the difference between the call of a race and the days before all ballots are counted. | The Rocky Mountain Voice is a 501(c)(3) that exists to promote informed civic engagement and the principles of self-governance through enterprise reporting on public policy, politics, government, and culture throughout the state of Colorado. The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support! | Democrat Yadira Caraveo’s lead over Republican Gabe Evans in Colorado’s 8th District has decreased to 0.76%, with Caraveo now ahead by just 2,096 votes as of two days after Election Day. Adams County, a Democratic stronghold, has counted 81.7% of its ballots, while Republican-leaning Weld County has counted 86.5%, leaving room for further shifts as remaining votes are counted. The race holds significance for Colorado’s congressional representation, with an Evans victory potentially resulting in a 4-4 split for the state in the U.S. House, impacting the overall House balance as Republicans seek a majority. | Colorado Democrats are on track to maintain a supermajority in the House but fall one seat short of a supermajority in the Senate, ensuring continued significant influence but with some limitations. Key races in both chambers, especially in El Paso and Weld Counties, remain close, with Republicans making gains in certain districts and flipping a seat previously held by a Democrat who switched parties. The Colorado Chamber of Commerce, which endorsed moderate candidates across both parties, believes that a balanced legislative approach may result from the election, focusing on pro-business policies and regulatory reforms. | Ranked-choice voting (RCV) faced major setbacks in the 2024 election, with voters in states like Colorado, Idaho, Oregon, and Nevada rejecting state-level RCV initiatives and Missouri passing a ban on the system altogether. Despite these losses, RCV saw some local wins, including a strong endorsement in Washington, D.C., reflecting a divide where urban centers are more open to adopting the voting method compared to state-level or rural areas. | Trump supporters, galvanized by his recent popular vote win, are pushing for sweeping changes to dismantle what they view as a corrupt and elitist establishment. Many Americans see Trump’s success as a clear rebuke to the ruling class, with his supporters framing his leadership as a mission to restore traditional American values, patriotism, and economic opportunity. Sentiments online reflect a blend of hope and defiance, with language often invoking religious and battle metaphors, as supporters rally around a "MAGA" vision of reviving America’s cultural and political landscape. | President-elect Donald Trump has appointed Susie Wiles, his 2024 campaign manager, as his Chief of Staff, marking her as the first woman to hold this position in U.S. history. Wiles played a crucial role in Trump's campaign victories in 2016, 2020, and 2024, earning her respect within the team for her strategic skill and leadership. Trump praised Wiles as “tough, smart, and innovative,” highlighting her commitment to the "Make America Great Again" agenda, while other potential candidates, such as Kevin McCarthy, confirmed they were not interested in the role. | Trump’s landslide reflects a triumph over the lockdowns, inflation, and division that defined the Biden era. The Covid years were tough, and now they’re finally ending. In two weeks, I’ll have a health book coming out that in part explains the story of why the lockdowns were so devastating. At the same time, Trump’s election gives hope to aggressively address the issues outlined in the pages. Former President Donald Trump hit a grand slam Tuesday. Republicans finally reclaimed the Senate and the White House four years after America’s deadly lockdowns coincided with a year of political turmoil and ended in a riot at the Capitol. Trump’s legacy was almost diminished as an ex-president whose tenure was defined by cultural unrest and whose exit interrupted the smooth transfer of power. | ELECTION MUST-READS 🗳️ BY LINDY BROWNING | LOCAL | NOVEMBER 7 Members of a multi-jurisdictional task force, known as SPEAR (Special Problem Enforcement and Response Task Force), have intercepted 28,500 hydrocodone pills, with a street value of $285,000. BY LINDY BROWNING | STATE | NOVEMBER 7 Two women have been arrested in connection with the theft of multiple ballots in Mesa County, District Attorney Dan Rubinstein announced Wednesday. BY LINDY BROWNING | STATE |NOVEMBER 6 Jeff Hurd’s telephone rang at about 3:30 p.m. Wednesday, a little more than 24 hours after polls closed on Election Day. On the other end of the phone was his opponent, Democrat Adam Frisch. Feel like you have a unique story or commentary to share? We'd love to talk with you about becoming an RMV Contributor. Reply to this email (or reach out to mailto:brian@rockymountainvoice.com?subject=Contributor%20Interest%20\(via%20Mountain%20Minute\)) or send us a message on social media to chat! | | 🤔 DID YOU KNOW? One might say that George Washington deserves the record for the greatest margin of victory by any American president. In the first presidential election in 1789, Washington claimed 100 percent of the electoral college—all 69 votes cast. But Washington had a unique advantage: he ran unopposed. Here are , ranked by their margins of victory from smallest to largest. | CARTOON OF THE DAY | QUOTE OF THE DAY “One of the most horrifying and surprising evolutions we have witnessed among our widespread campus network is the rapid movement away from tolerating opposing ideas and respectful debate to the deployment of obscene bully tactics from the left.****” – Charlie Kirk | | | | ---|---|--- Did a friend forward you this email? | A product of Rocky Mountain Voice, Inc. 8200 S Quebec St, STE A3601 Centennial, CO 80112 This message was originally sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com Be sure to put “RockyMountainVoice.com” on your safe-senders whitelist. name: Save the Children
subject: Ukraine Update: Children still need your support
body:
There couldn’t be a more impactful moment to give.
|
---
| | | | | | | | | [](https://click.alerts.savethechildren.org/?qs=a7650826de341757a3fd0092df0d449c8b460ba72be15bceca3a0d5a767e8518d2d1d543e14d5b677bda63b4b37a4a143401739ce7bebf4236649da88f08f962)
---
| | | | [ ](https://click.alerts.savethechildren.org/?qs=47dbed774da209d1af43b3aa7a073acfdf7c248d67c1f07503220e20a77fa1eccf78b2c1736f887f9aa4848a422dcc16fa4bf9bc1caff2f9ce1919f38ae10692)
---
Friend, at just 3 years old, Yaroslav* has already faced horrors no child ever should. He's heard mines exploding, been forced to take shelter in a basement for weeks and seen his family's home in Bucha, Ukraine, destroyed by war.

**But during his darkest moments, supporters like you reached out to help.** Thanks to you, we've provided Yaroslav's family - and many more around the world - with cash assistance so they can afford basic necessities like gas, heat, food and hygiene supplies.

**[Today, we're asking you to join thousands of generous donors and make a gift that can help support children across the globe who are facing unimaginable hardships like conflict, poverty and disaster.](https://click.alerts.savethechildren.org/?qs=47dbed774da209d1c35fb3b1ad274a2f4078f5139f8a3d4f76e7dbd88c550fee39a96ec277306b588ee3df47ba4b3504c1797991505a326a96232b41453156a1)**
---
| [](https://click.alerts.savethechildren.org/?qs=a7650826de341757cc75ecdb4e6cf4acaa63dc2ed13a1aa89e0a082cdd99df43bd8adfad86d37fd5b70e8db86b57e2ca756d95d27942914eb92988af41acedd8)
---
In addition to helping support children in conflict zones like Yaroslav, your gift can help provide warm winter clothing to children like 7-year-old Mirwais,* whose family is struggling amidst political and economic instability in Afghanistan. It can help provide lifesaving medical treatment to severely malnourished children like 2-year-old Sophia,* who is facing the hunger crisis across the Horn of Africa.

**Every dollar makes a world of difference to children facing unimaginable hardships like conflict, poverty and hunger - and right now, we need your support to make sure we hit our $100,000 goal before the end of the month.**

**[So please, don't wait: donate now and help children like Yaroslav, Mirwais and Sophia access the resources and care they need to grow up healthy, educated and safe.](https://click.alerts.savethechildren.org/?qs=47dbed774da209d1c35fb3b1ad274a2f4078f5139f8a3d4f76e7dbd88c550fee39a96ec277306b588ee3df47ba4b3504c1797991505a326a96232b41453156a1)**
---
| [ ](https://click.alerts.savethechildren.org/?qs=a7650826de341757cc75ecdb4e6cf4acaa63dc2ed13a1aa89e0a082cdd99df43bd8adfad86d37fd5b70e8db86b57e2ca756d95d27942914eb92988af41acedd8)
---
| | | Thank you for everything you do to create lasting change for the children who need it most. Together, we can help millions more have brighter futures.

Save the Children

name: Kevin Lincoln
subject: Not over yet!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY KEVIN LINCOLN FOR CONGRESS
body:
Only half the votes for my battleground House race in CA-9 are in, and as you can see, it’s a dead heat.

| [](https://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2zbugec3c3lad3pn1l4luetap5kq74e7uop8dmld8_rp22sh2s8i6epj4c1ge1o72c1h2bs)
Fellow American,

Across the nation, we’re celebrating huge wins for President Trump and a new Republican Senate Majority.

**But the battle for the House isn’t over yet.**

Only half the votes for my battleground House race in CA-9 are in, _**and as you can see, it’s a dead heat.**_

[](https://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2zhkeh00j5mq6unlfhnauaj29fi9nq7bkq32sucpg_rp22sh2s8i6epj4c1ge1o72c1h2bs)
This seat is needed to ensure we have a strong House Majority that can pass the Republican agenda, [**and we still need your help.**](https://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2zlg26guchebmv8mrs15b14tj5q0hkn1i5h3lorjg_rp22sh2s8i6epj4c1ge1o72c1h2bs)

We are dispatching field teams to cure mail ballots and make sure our votes are counted. And if necessary, we need to be able to take action. [**But we also need your help to pay for this.**](https://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2zrfl2d4hke12bmjrqoqnlru7t7cf8jslrh4o6q5o_rp22sh2s8i6epj4c1ge1o72c1h2bs)

We don’t have armies of left-wing lawyers like the Democrats. [**Can you pitch in to help us secure the vote in this still-undecided critical race?**](https://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2z1f9h9unhv5n9qkeponm49euf4sg1sjf58k8maf0_rp22sh2s8i6epj4c1ge1o72c1h2bs)

This election isn’t over yet, and we don’t want to lose a single vote - it's that close.

Please continue supporting our victory!

**Kevin Lincoln**

[** Help us Protect the Vote ➞ **](https://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2z73jobvg0n83csk05o3k7m6a7ji0osqphd96oveg_rp22sh2s8i6epj4c1ge1o72c1h2bs)

| **Meet Kevin Lincoln
**
My name is Kevin Lincoln, and my story, is the Valley’s story. I am a 3rd generation Stocktonian, raising a 4th generation in the city I love. I’m the grandson of Mexican immigrants, and my stepfather is an Army veteran.

Like many Stocktonians, my family has overcome economic setbacks. My own father struggled to survive on these streets, and was at one time, homeless. But I never let adversity define me. I joined the Marines and served in the White House Military Office, assigned to Marine One. I graduated college while working full-time and raising a family. I’ve worked in corporate America and served our community as a pastor, and as a non-profit leader.

I’m blessed with a beautiful wife, Bonnie, and two amazing children. I’ve lived a life of service, but the greatest honor of my life has been giving back to my community, through serving as Mayor of my hometown, Stockton.
[** SEND A MARINE TO CONGRESS ➞ **](https://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2zr04vemv5n00lq6vu5jvsgh0p9fdflovrsksjm3o_rp22sh2s8i6epj4c1ge1o72c1h2bs)
---

But the battle for the House isn’t over yet. Only half the votes for my battleground House race in CA-9 are in, and as you can see, it’s a dead heat. This seat is needed to ensure we have a strong House Majority that can pass the Republican agenda, We are dispatching field teams to cure mail ballots and make sure our votes are counted. And if necessary, we need to be able to take action. We don’t have armies of left-wing lawyers like the Democrats. This election isn’t over yet, and we don’t want to lose a single vote - it's that close. Please continue supporting our victory! Kevin Lincoln | Meet Kevin Lincoln My name is Kevin Lincoln, and my story, is the Valley’s story. I am a 3rd generation Stocktonian, raising a 4th generation in the city I love. I’m the grandson of Mexican immigrants, and my stepfather is an Army veteran. Like many Stocktonians, my family has overcome economic setbacks. My own father struggled to survive on these streets, and was at one time, homeless. But I never let adversity define me. I joined the Marines and served in the White House Military Office, assigned to Marine One. I graduated college while working full-time and raising a family. I’ve worked in corporate America and served our community as a pastor, and as a non-profit leader. I’m blessed with a beautiful wife, Bonnie, and two amazing children. I’ve lived a life of service, but the greatest honor of my life has been giving back to my community, through serving as Mayor of my hometown, Stockton. Kevin Lincoln is a former member of the U.S. Marine Corps. Use of his military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Marine Corps or the Department of Defense. | PAID FOR BY KEVIN LINCOLN FOR CONGRESS Trump Train News PO Box 461 Fort Collins, CO 80522 |

subject: Urgent update on my race
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ted Cruz for Senate
body:

And the lead continues GROWING as the final votes are tallied. We're ahead, and make no mistake, we WON this race. But while votes continue to be counted, Bob Casey and his allies are organizing a potential recount, and we won't be caught flat-footed. name: Neil Parrott
subject: Too close to call...
body:
I wanted to give you a brief update regarding the election results. We had volunteers and paid staff in each of the 5 counties monitoring the count | | | [View this email in your browser](https://us2.campaign-archive.com/?e=5a45ee1154&u=fac7efbc0801875f99034b204&id=aeb6f8882e)
---
|
---
| | Peter,

I wanted to give you a brief update regarding the election results. We had volunteers and paid staff in each of the 5 counties monitoring the count to make sure every vote is counted properly.

We are going to continue to monitor the counting that will continue in Frederick and Montgomery Counties tomorrow and into Saturday.

UPDATE: After today, 4029 votes out of 330,637 total counted votes separate my opponent from me. We are one of the few House races that remains too close to call.

**Please continue to pray for the results and that God would be with our entire team through this time. ****Also, I ask for your immediate help to make sure every ballot is counted. **
---
