Friends,
While we grieve the end of Kamala Harris’ historic and inspiring race for President, we continue the campaign to win back the House.
Right now, the margins in several House races remain too close to call and our Democratic candidates have a chance to win — with OUR help.
Winning the House can literally be the bulwark for our Democracy — and for the cause of our lives: health care.
Since we passed the Affordable Care Act, our ever-expanding coalition has delivered, saved and strengthened health care — and now we must save it again for all Americans, particularly our most vulnerable.
Join me for an EMERGENCY MEETUP to discuss how we will organize to Save Our Health Care — now, by curing ballots in close House races, and going forward, with the outside mobilization we need to counter extreme MAGA Republicans’ assaults on the ACA.

subject: Our work isn’t done in Nevada
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alsobrooks for Senate
body:
[ https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/33460?t=1009&amount=25&akid=3215%2E52803%2E-7XIpj ][IMG]

Peter: Our work in Nevada isn’t done. Jacky Rosen’s race is
still ongoing and tens of thousands of ballots are still being counted.

I’m reaching out now because Jacky needs to see a major surge of resources
to finish this race strong and keep her seat blue. [ https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/33460?t=1010&amount=25&akid=3215%2E52803%2E-7XIpj ]Please, split a
donation between Jacky’s campaign and Angela Alsobrooks to help Jacky
reach her emergency goal:

[ https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/33460?t=1011&amount=25&akid=3215%2E52803%2E-7XIpj ]Donate

I know we didn’t get all of the results we wanted on Election Night, but
that‘s why it’s even more important to ensure that we defend Jacky Rosen’s
critical Senate seat.

Jacky’s dangerous MAGA opponent, Sam Brown, will be a rubber stamp for
President-elect Donald Trump’s dangerous agenda, including a national
abortion ban and pushing through the extreme, far-right Project 2025
agenda. Securing Jacky’s seat means that Democratic Senators will have a
check on Donald Trump and the new Republican Senate Majority.

Just like my race in 2022, this race is coming down to the wire. And
believe me when I say that there is still lots of work to be done to
guarantee that every vote is counted – and that they’re counted fairly.

The truth is, that work is expensive. And Jacky urgently needs our help so
she can keep her campaign running. Republicans spent nearly $80 million
against Jacky to defeat her this year, so her campaign drained their
resources to get her to Election Day. Now, she’s relying on us to step up
so she can keep her ground game going strong and count every last vote.

Time is running out, and the stakes are higher than ever. [ https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/33460?t=1012&amount=25&akid=3215%2E52803%2E-7XIpj ]So I’m humbly
asking you to split a donation to Jacky Rosen’s absolutely CRITICAL Senate
race and Angela Alsobrooks so we can get ballots counted and secure her
seat.

If you’ve saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your
donation will go through immediately.

Catherine Cortez Masto

clean_body: Our work is not done — | = | The election did not go the way that we hoped. We are extremely proud of Vice President Kamala Harris and how hard she fought in this election. Unfortunately, Donald Trump will return to the White House in January 2025. = = = = = = We know Tuesday’s results are devastating. We feel it too. Once again, California stands ready to be the keeper of America’s values. We have two strong U.S. Senators who will stand up to Donald Trump in the U.S. Senate, and there is still an opportunity for us to flip the U.S. House and provide a strong check on a Trump Administration. But that requires us working overtime to ensure every single eligible vote is counted in our critical purple seats. All the Republican-held seats that were ranked as “tossup” are still uncalled, and we’re going to fight tooth-and-nail for every single one. Our work is not done until every single vote is counted. But we can’t do it alone. = = No matter what, thank you for being here with us. We will keep you updated every step of the way. Onward, \- CADEM | | = Our Democratic leaders are working hard to make California a better place to live. From fighting for good-paying jobs, combating climate change, to expanding healthcare access so all Californians can lead safe, healthy, prosperous lives — California’s Democratic leaders turn progressive ideas into meaningful action. name: COURIER
subject: Humbly asking you to read this message of resilience, Peter
body:
“Amid the cowardice of legacy media, let’s find brave journalists and support them.”

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96795403/511213358/2130526953?refcode=emwrapper&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDc4NzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNGEyMTc0ZGEtZWI5ZC1lZjExLTg4Y2UtMDAwZDNhOThmYTZiIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=1RvIc-37ZBYP5rjv_E9bqs8i3HLYZzlrUX3tPmxsJOk=&emci=472174da-eb9d-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=4a2174da-eb9d-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=14016711)
Peter,
Anger. Sadness. Fear. Frustration.
**If Donald Trump’s victory this week left you feeling any of these emotions toward our politics or the legacy media that cover it, we’re right there with you.**
We know that many of you are feeling worried right now. We encourage everyone to take a moment to process what his victory means for our country and the values we hold dear.
In the meantime, we wanted to share COURIER’s Mark Jacob’s powerful Substack post, written the day after Election Day.
**Mark’s message is one of defiance and resilience, and we hope it offers you some inspiration for the fight ahead.**
[**If you have a moment, please read Mark’s message below. And if you’re able, please consider donating $25 or whatever you can to help us continue our mission in this critical time.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96795408/511213363/-593472309?refcode=20241108-EM-MarkJacob&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDc4NzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNGEyMTc0ZGEtZWI5ZC1lZjExLTg4Y2UtMDAwZDNhOThmYTZiIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=1RvIc-37ZBYP5rjv_E9bqs8i3HLYZzlrUX3tPmxsJOk=)
In solidarity,
_The COURIER Team_
* * *
**_The fascists want us to shut up. Don’t._**
Amid the cowardice of legacy media, let’s find brave journalists and support them.
The Guardian’s headline read: _“Donald Trump defeats Kamala Harris to become the first convicted criminal to be elected U.S. president.”_
**We’ll have to see how long the Guardian keeps its press credentials after Trump takes power.**
We’ll also have to see if any other mainstream outlets will be as bold as the Guardian in telling the truth about Trump. The TV networks and newspapers know that they may face government harassment if they criticize him. Retribution is his brand, and they know it.
**The coming months and years will be a roll call for fearless journalists, and we don’t know how many will answer. But as we enter an era of deepening national madness, there will be essential stories to tell.**
Trump plans to appoint corrupt figures like Robert F. Kennedy Jr. to key positions and establish a Putin-style oligarchy. He vows to round up immigrants, put them in camps, and deport them—creating widespread suffering. His election endorses the second-class status of women, people of color, and trans people.
In Trump’s dictatorship, there will be many victims to interview. But will there be journalists willing to do so?
Or will we see a journalism of avoidance and distraction?
This election highlighted the power of mass media. But it was the power to misinform. Right-wing propaganda from Trump and Fox News convinced Americans that our economy was failing, that their kids were at risk of being “indoctrinated” in school, and that immigrants posed a violent threat.
**The truth was drowned out. The truth-tellers failed us.**
The responsible media neglected their duty to help the public understand facts. Instead of challenging the liars, they often treated lies as just another viewpoint. Major outlets normalized fascist misconduct and minimized the threat to our democracy. On Sunday shows, the same Republican liars were given platforms to deceive again and again.
**Legacy media allowed right-wing propagandists to create an alternative reality. They did nothing to reach those trapped in a bubble of disinformation, choosing profit over public interest._The Washington Post_ , _Los Angeles Times_ , and _USA Today_ took the cowardly route, avoiding endorsements that might anger Trump.**
**[Support Independent Journalism](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96795412/511213397/-593472309?refcode=20241108-EM-MarkJacob&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDc4NzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNGEyMTc0ZGEtZWI5ZC1lZjExLTg4Y2UtMDAwZDNhOThmYTZiIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=1RvIc-37ZBYP5rjv_E9bqs8i3HLYZzlrUX3tPmxsJOk=)**
Now that Trump has his mandate, there’s little reason to believe those outlets will suddenly find their courage.
Look for Trump to welcome media moguls to Mar-a-Lago to bend the knee. He already has Elon Musk as a disinformation ally, and Mark Zuckerberg has retreated to the safe ground of downplaying political content on his platforms.
**_The New York Times_ faces a choice. It can oppose Trump and become a punching bag for his administration, or remain detached from the fight for democracy, burying internment camps under euphemisms like “involuntary gathering sites.”**
Our hope lies with the independent press, especially nonprofit outlets like _ProPublica_. In an era when legacy media have failed us, independent media have offered a lifeline. We need to support these outlets with our money and our passion. Read _The New Republic_. Read _Meidas Touch_. Read _COURIER_ —and more.
**We must keep posting on social media, speaking out about politics and injustice. We cannot ignore the disaster of these times. We must address it, or risk losing everything.**
Let’s stay connected through digital startups, newsletters, and independent platforms, beyond the interference of people like Elon Musk.
The fascists want us to accept the loss of our rights and the persecution of our fellow human beings.
Which is why we must keep speaking up.
And we won’t stop.
**[Donate ➡️](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96795416/511213409/-593472309?refcode=20241108-EM-MarkJacob&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDc4NzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNGEyMTc0ZGEtZWI5ZC1lZjExLTg4Y2UtMDAwZDNhOThmYTZiIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=1RvIc-37ZBYP5rjv_E9bqs8i3HLYZzlrUX3tPmxsJOk=)**

clean_body: “Amid the cowardice of legacy media, let’s find brave journalists and support them.” Peter, Anger. Sadness. Fear. Frustration. If Donald Trump’s victory this week left you feeling any of these emotions toward our politics or the legacy media that cover it, we’re right there with you. We know that many of you are feeling worried right now. We encourage everyone to take a moment to process what his victory means for our country and the values we hold dear. In the meantime, we wanted to share COURIER’s Mark Jacob’s powerful Substack post, written the day after Election Day. Mark’s message is one of defiance and resilience, and we hope it offers you some inspiration for the fight ahead. = In solidarity, The COURIER Team The fascists want us to shut up. Don’t. Amid the cowardice of legacy media, let’s find brave journalists and support them. The Guardian’s headline read: “Donald Trump defeats Kamala Harris to become the first convicted criminal to be elected U.S. president.” We’ll have to see how long the Guardian keeps its press credentials after Trump takes power. We’ll also have to see if any other mainstream outlets will be as bold as the Guardian in telling the truth about Trump. The TV networks and newspapers know that they may face government harassment if they criticize him. Retribution is his brand, and they know it. The coming months and years will be a roll call for fearless journalists, and we don’t know how many will answer. But as we enter an era of deepening national madness, there will be essential stories to tell. Trump plans to appoint corrupt figures like Robert F. Kennedy Jr. to key positions and establish a Putin-style oligarchy. He vows to round up immigrants, put them in camps, and deport them—creating widespread suffering. His election endorses the second-class status of women, people of color, and trans people. In Trump’s dictatorship, there will be many victims to interview. But will there be journalists willing to do so? Or will we see a journalism of avoidance and distraction? This election highlighted the power of mass media. But it was the power to misinform. Right-wing propaganda from Trump and Fox News convinced Americans that our economy was failing, that their kids were at risk of being “indoctrinated” in school, and that immigrants posed a violent threat. The truth was drowned out. The truth-tellers failed us. The responsible media neglected their duty to help the public understand facts. Instead of challenging the liars, they often treated lies as just another viewpoint. Major outlets normalized fascist misconduct and minimized the threat to our democracy. On Sunday shows, the same Republican liars were given platforms to deceive again and again. Legacy media allowed right-wing propagandists to create an alternative reality. They did nothing to reach those trapped in a bubble of disinformation, choosing profit over public interest.The Washington Post , Los Angeles Times , and USA Today took the cowardly route, avoiding endorsements that might anger Trump. = Now that Trump has his mandate, there’s little reason to believe those outlets will suddenly find their courage. Look for Trump to welcome media moguls to Mar-a-Lago to bend the knee. He already has Elon Musk as a disinformation ally, and Mark Zuckerberg has retreated to the safe ground of downplaying political content on his platforms. The New York Times faces a choice. It can oppose Trump and become a punching bag for his administration, or remain detached from the fight for democracy, burying internment camps under euphemisms like “involuntary gathering sites.” Our hope lies with the independent press, especially nonprofit outlets like ProPublica. In an era when legacy media have failed us, independent media have offered a lifeline. We need to support these outlets with our money and our passion. Read The New Republic. Read Meidas Touch. Read COURIER —and more. We must keep posting on social media, speaking out about politics and injustice. We cannot ignore the disaster of these times. We must address it, or risk losing everything. Let’s stay connected through digital startups, newsletters, and independent platforms, beyond the interference of people like Elon Musk. The fascists want us to accept the loss of our rights and the persecution of our fellow human beings. Which is why we must keep speaking up. And we won’t stop. = Email us: mailto:info@couriernewsroom.com

Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.

disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rosen for Nevada Rosen for Nevada
body:
Our work isn’t done
| | |
---
Friends – I’ve been spending all day talking to folks about Nevada’s Senate race, and I’ve all but lost my voice. But I’m gonna have to yell it one more time:
**IS THERE ANYTHING I CAN SAY TO CONVINCE YOU TO CHIP IN TO JACKY ROSEN’S CAMPAIGN???**
| | [Donate](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96795523/511213689/-107723448?amounts=250,100,50,25,5&refcode=jtk5412-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzZGEzYWRlNy1lYjlkLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=quMooaWqCVOc6VINh2jHYplOi3Sw7-AiYyVguw2DvmA=)
---
Jacky Rosen’s race was _always_ going to come down to a narrow margin. After Democrats squeaked by with a 0.77% margin of victory in 2022, Republicans were dead set on flipping Nevada red – and they spent nearly $80 million to do it.
With the help of this grassroots team, Jacky has run one of the best campaigns I’ve ever seen. But our work isn’t done yet.**This race is still going - and there are THOUSANDS of ballots that need to be counted.**
If we let up now, Jacky’s Trump-endorsed opponent will flip this seat and be another vote to secure the worst of Trump’s agenda – including gutting the Affordable Care Act and passing a nationwide abortion ban.
Jacky Rosen has a path to victory, but we can’t make a single mistake. And lemme tell ya – letting her fall short of her emergency fundraising goal would be a HUGE MISTAKE RIGHT NOW!!
I’m pleading with you. _**[PLEASE chip in $5 to Jacky’s campaign before it’s too late to make sure every vote is fairly counted.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96795524/511213690/173338096?amounts=250,100,50,25,5&refcode=jtk5412-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzZGEzYWRlNy1lYjlkLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=quMooaWqCVOc6VINh2jHYplOi3Sw7-AiYyVguw2DvmA=)**_

name: Team Rosen
subject: Ballot curing is an integral part of close races like this
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Defeat Boebert PAC
body:
| | | _Are you feeling as anxious as we are, Peter?_ Jacky Rosen’s U.S. Senate race is **still** too close to call in Nevada. That’s why we’re doing everything we can to knock doors, make phone calls, and help with the ballot curing process to ensure that every last vote is counted in this race that is going to come down to the margins. **The problem is that we spent all the resources we had to get through Election Day, so we need to raise emergency funds to get us through this final stretch. name: Anna Cheng Kramer
subject: I Am Grateful for Your Support
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Anna Cheng Kramer for Congress_
body:
I want to express my sincere thanks for everything you have done to encourage me, donate, and volunteer for my campaign. | | | | | |
---
| | | | | | | | | |
---
|
---
| | | | | To My Friends and Supporters,
---
| I want to express my sincere thanks for everything you have done to encourage me, donate, and volunteer for my campaign. While I did not win, I’m glad there is hope for our nation as President Trump returns to the White House. I am also proud of our efforts as we gave hope to many in our blue county and broke a few records.
In San Francisco County, we beat Arnold Schwarzenegger as the top Republican vote-getter in my district reaching 32% of the voters so far. In San Mateo County, our current 28% exceeded the 19% and 21% prior Republican candidates obtained for this congressional seat during Presidential Election years. Finally, with your help, we outraised prior Republican candidates as well. These achievements are a testament to our collective efforts and your unwavering support.
It has been an incredible experience to run as a candidate, and I appreciate all of you who joined me on this journey. The valuable knowledge gained will translate to future wins for San Mateo County as California continues to wake up to the destructive policies of the progressive Democrats. I hope to continue this effort as Chairwoman of the San Mateo County Republican Party. Our Central Committee members have dedicated countless volunteer hours to move us forward. If you want to join us or get our newsletters, please click on the buttons below. We will need everyone to participate in this great movement to flip California red.
---
| | | | | | | | | |
[Join SMGOP](https://ackramerforcongress.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0681592302c29e74ce410ea82&id=63e36bcf5d&e=d9ccafcb33)
|
[Subscribe to SMGOP Newsletter](https://ackramerforcongress.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0681592302c29e74ce410ea82&id=a98f157358&e=d9ccafcb33)
| With deepest gratitude,
---

name: NEXTGENPAC.ORG
subject: This is an all hands on deck moment
disclaimer_text: Paid for by NextGen Climate Action Committee; [http://nextgenpac
body:
| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/96796377/511216375/-1995327540?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNTkcvMS81NjE1OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjMjg4MzcwMC1lZTlkLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=MUhBnChxPuaoKewxh1DbS1kJvC0oUzbYSPrOfSwnhX8=&emci=53e5f61d-dd9d-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=c2883700-ee9d-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=6787172)
---
Team, you may still be processing the results of the election -- we certainly are. But now, we have to channel our energy into action.
**It is abundantly clear that, in the next few years, we are going to face extraordinary challenges. **The threats of a second Trump administration are very real and extremely distressing.
Project 2025 clearly lays out the far-right's plans to overhaul the government, from dismantling the Department of Education to taking away abortion access to even getting rid of no-fault divorce.
But let's get one thing clear: They WANT us to feel powerless. They want us to roll over when they take away our rights. They want us to sit back and cry while they destroy any hope of progress. You know what we say to that? **HELL NO.**
[These threats to our rights and future are exactly why each and every one of us needs to recommit ourselves to fighting back. If you're with us and you're ready to stand up against Trump's dangerous agenda, add your name to this petition TODAY.](https://click.everyaction.com/k/96796378/511216376/2092786572?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNTkcvMS81NjE1OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjMjg4MzcwMC1lZTlkLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=MUhBnChxPuaoKewxh1DbS1kJvC0oUzbYSPrOfSwnhX8=&emci=53e5f61d-dd9d-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=c2883700-ee9d-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=6787172)
| [FIGHT BACK →](https://click.everyaction.com/k/96796379/511216377/2092786572?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNTkcvMS81NjE1OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjMjg4MzcwMC1lZTlkLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=MUhBnChxPuaoKewxh1DbS1kJvC0oUzbYSPrOfSwnhX8=&emci=53e5f61d-dd9d-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=c2883700-ee9d-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=6787172)
---
When Trump threatens vulnerable communities, we'll be right there to support them. When he tries to give tax breaks to billionaires and corporations while neglecting the working class, we won't keep silent. When environmental regulations are slashed on a whim and abortion access is undercut, we will keep fighting.
It's not enough to just hope things won't go wrong. We have to act. If you're ready to act, sign the petition today and pledge to resist Trump's agenda.

clean_body: | Team, you may still be processing the results of the election -- we certainly are. But now, we have to channel our energy into action. It is abundantly clear that, in the next few years, we are going to face extraordinary challenges. The threats of a second Trump administration are very real and extremely distressing. Project 2025 clearly lays out the far-right's plans to overhaul the government, from dismantling the Department of Education to taking away abortion access to even getting rid of no-fault divorce. But let's get one thing clear: They WANT us to feel powerless. They want us to roll over when they take away our rights. They want us to sit back and cry while they destroy any hope of progress. You know what we say to that? HELL NO. | When Trump threatens vulnerable communities, we'll be right there to support them. When he tries to give tax breaks to billionaires and corporations while neglecting the working class, we won't keep silent. When environmental regulations are slashed on a whim and abortion access is undercut, we will keep fighting. It's not enough to just hope things won't go wrong. We have to act. If you're ready to act, sign the petition today and pledge to resist Trump's agenda. | Together, NextGen PAC | Paid for by NextGen Climate Action Committee; ; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. | = |

name: Jacky Rosen
subject: I'm personally asking for your help.
body:
Folks, I wouldn't reach out again if it weren't important.
Our race in Nevada isn’t over. This race will be one of the tightest in the country, and unless we raise another $115,762 before midnight, we could fall short of the critical resources we need to win.
The hard truth is that Nevada is won by the margins, so please, will you chip in right now to help tip the scales in our favor to keep Nevada blue?
Here's the best link to split a donation between my campaign and The Real Texas PAC: secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-rosen [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-rosen?refcode=110724_2]
Best,
Jacky

name: Tucker Carlson
subject: Helpful video on the Electoral College
body:
Citizens are free to say what they think. Subjects aren’t.

| | _Please see a special message from our sponsor. If you would like to unsubscribe, visit this[link](http://links.liberty-pulse.com/e/encryptedUnsubscribe?_r=29bc9815846f49728b1d374165de7bf2&_s=4ed5fbd2f45f4381876cf26ebb7d8f69&_t=QIFgLZ_Zr3PEK_NZ1KwkEz9RnKuyiFH3JHkLRk6cgBe9GivuKMnSfAk07CACa9A76FcZbddixaJmCEipTp5nYbyG-QMmgrPPbyhNQl6gxCSrWxVwLEt3wxyYlam25SWZ)_
---
| | [](http://links.liberty-pulse.com/u/click?_t=29bc9815846f49728b1d374165de7bf2&_m=4ed5fbd2f45f4381876cf26ebb7d8f69&_e=dLuAX8fC4hsiXXs6Vo52SLHTfmWAJRMXWGVzk1AIMH6rZmBM9RuYfsQs6R1GKPjbmFRlbcZV8eicD2mGnRUsaD4vlKDUVKPC160OtepeoyDedp_8IPOrlkwvFaVJnED-F1lqnf2DBVFIJakqtcFupEY64kFSRk5woAGXt0Xpbi9Vnccs50MLbUKWXn8k01eHkWy5qfzWx2YjDCAWlwbbJv3s4iMx4EsX1wBi17Tzkl1UNxv5J1jxYU7-1Uyaqjc9q17nsZHG3oksg3skjjkRGkkt15nMwI0xkNtWhOR9KUdk2BGvpgKq3Vr7F0Ymt7Hr62JXiyiDfACfHtaZHVWfK91n41wjP_tOpfjCEqYisgo4Dq7QmBduaG85notDivLsV8P_lrX1t6i58dzNgR6dgA%3D%3D)
---
---
| | Dear Friend,

Citizens are free to say what they think. Subjects aren't.

Sadly, the question of whether we are citizens or subjects has become politically controversial in our country. A clear sign of this is the attempt to suppress the expression of obvious truths, such as the fact that boys cannot become girls.

Another sign that many of our leaders consider us subjects is their denial of our right to decide who is allowed to enter our country--indeed, they tell us that if we complain about mass illegal immigration, we are racists.

**This is a pivotal time in our nation 's history--a time when we must stand up and forthrightly demand to be treated as citizens, not subjects. Because freedom hangs in the balance.**

A poisonous ideology has infected almost every major American institution. Only a few still stand in defense of freedom and the right of self-government.

One of the most important of these is **Hillsdale College**. And today, my friends at Hillsdale need your partnership and support in their mission to preserve the blessings of civil and religious liberty through education.

For 180 years, Hillsdale has offered its students the kind of education America's founders thought necessary to sustain free government. And in recent decades, Hillsdale has extended its teaching mission nationwide with a vast national outreach effort.

This outreach effort includes its free publication _Imprimis_ , its free online courses, and its tireless work to return excellence--including excellent civics instruction--to American K-12 education.

And Hillsdale College does all of this while refusing to accept one penny of government funding--not even indirectly through federal or state student grants and loans.

That's why I ask you to take the time to view their video **"[The Electoral College Explained](http://links.liberty-pulse.com/u/click?_t=29bc9815846f49728b1d374165de7bf2&_m=4ed5fbd2f45f4381876cf26ebb7d8f69&_e=dLuAX8fC4hsiXXs6Vo52SLHTfmWAJRMXWGVzk1AIMH6rZmBM9RuYfsQs6R1GKPjbmFRlbcZV8eicD2mGnRUsaD4vlKDUVKPC160OtepeoyDedp_8IPOrlkwvFaVJnED-F1lqnf2DBVFIJakqtcFupEY64kFSRk5woAGXt0Xpbi9Vnccs50MLbUKWXn8k01eHkWy5qfzWx2YjDCAWlwbbJv3s4iMx4EsX1wBi17Tzkl1UNxv5J1jxYU7-1Uyaqjc9X3OOsAppGSrsRAgYvunZ2rVfGCI8aGvXh8D6a4bgNPr30lPsMukkV6VWUVKXpGLPdrdp2rvY2HhngOZj3xkOzgiP5bTV4qFe-ACr37irPeONgnur6wvW0Cm2zFZy9_Wbcp4A9-vyMaw1ZZv1iAvjSQ%3D%3D)"** below and join Hillsdale in defending freedom and the American way of life.

clean_body: Citizens are free to say what they think. Subjects aren’t. | | Please see a special message from our sponsor. If you would like to unsubscribe, visit this | | | | Dear Friend, Citizens are free to say what they think. Subjects aren't. Sadly, the question of whether we are citizens or subjects has become politically controversial in our country. A clear sign of this is the attempt to suppress the expression of obvious truths, such as the fact that boys cannot become girls. Another sign that many of our leaders consider us subjects is their denial of our right to decide who is allowed to enter our country--indeed, they tell us that if we complain about mass illegal immigration, we are racists. This is a pivotal time in our nation 's history--a time when we must stand up and forthrightly demand to be treated as citizens, not subjects. Because freedom hangs in the balance. A poisonous ideology has infected almost every major American institution. Only a few still stand in defense of freedom and the right of self-government. One of the most important of these is Hillsdale College. And today, my friends at Hillsdale need your partnership and support in their mission to preserve the blessings of civil and religious liberty through education. For 180 years, Hillsdale has offered its students the kind of education America's founders thought necessary to sustain free government. And in recent decades, Hillsdale has extended its teaching mission nationwide with a vast national outreach effort. This outreach effort includes its free publication Imprimis , its free online courses, and its tireless work to return excellence--including excellent civics instruction--to American K-12 education. And Hillsdale College does all of this while refusing to accept one penny of government funding--not even indirectly through federal or state student grants and loans. That's why I ask you to take the time to view their video "" below and join Hillsdale in defending freedom and the American way of life. Sincerely, Tucker Carlson | | | | | .

# A Post-Election Note
---
Like many of you, we’re reeling from this week’s election results. It’s a hard pill to swallow and many of the races signal an enormous amount of work ahead to fight for a future where we are all valued and able to live without fear. This newly elected administration and congress have been loud and clear about the fascist future they intend to implement through their path of [Project 2025](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.lmpSTrE-G07FKUPTdpC9IbOKOc33ehZvf_TrTPCl0dMMbi9t3AyLvwRTfzTbjH0-NBwSdkydlez6p6yUkMt8MopDOTgCIuo0jR4ncEYV6N45YBdeUnEiy6S4SU97lGdisCzPRTNYMOtNYK875ybn92LXOHAfLZpwFc7_EoJCzKBxZt6RBt9eynP8tNuooA5r7ZPyBYadHJfqyurUZPind0dN5O2AYta21jRHaYjQNAEs34Io_Gy5D5tDUEuu2zMb1eYcyMsUvy3O-bgmIvxfYo63sj0Wbyu7CodLIEHN2nE/4ba/XTOAnapQTOaqknBxszMN2g/h1/h001.dgs8sFM4DdiTrzjzJ1_pyfoIBMNtcwzGw3V683Fgg8s). But, our progressive movement is just that — movement! It means that we join together to regroup, shift, and continue forward with a more impactful, cohesive strategy.
---
|
---
This election made it resoundingly clear that we not only have to fight these policies, but also must focus on public education, creating better tools, and framing information to provide concrete ways for people to take action. We at Keystone Progress Education Fund are committed to deepening and expanding this work to move our country in a progressive direction that recognizes the strength in our diverse communities and improves our quality of life. We hope that you will join us in the fight ahead — because, make no mistake, it will be a fight. An important note: not all of the Pennsylvania elections have been called! Stay up to date on all of Pennsylvania's election results, including the yet to be determined Senate seat **[here](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.ey81QDo6Jmsd8k7EQQaVfDnK_wqQpmX889kK7z8UBs6kP8A_hbAbI_F2LHRJHM-bST1rXMZYxkcs18WCVLFoJXqpFbNQfrN2V1YYwCAZ3MqVn0od3gwA47ea5KmomRHDlJp2PIaHGxizLrkOmlmcZyDDdWO66ESu78X-UHrcyuYwUvkdfJZ9X8ZXEJU95yOAspqGGzMwAHvjdh0wBUeXk5Di7_Dy-dFm8bOTIgbe66WiAOb8-XO_oRTENn8WD8Leb4aAVbgKAUrUP8PmVojAlTOC6Di-jxjpK8Ms5BMPm6juhLXynBhs9UG0wo4EcQ-C/4ba/XTOAnapQTOaqknBxszMN2g/h2/h001.1tOYK-AElZR0v1QtcLc5fXHRMcnjD_ANoomly2Pqvgg)**.

# Repro Roundup
---
This week’s election also caused major changes in the country's reproductive rights landscape. Learn more about the measures passed that protect abortion access **[in seven different states](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMTHpY68KeBL-tuPFtNUwAZSEQKHHBYRNw7FRfv4oOeNXqgpQuygU2sn29ilHzUKTXVku_yWUqEnyJqZdjYJMntE2RtTnXdWl9mvb5tDeFLAG9QvTHa5VHHY7ZloV6tGwNVfMwMcsE9Vfexs-XyZ3SEu07-3rl7z4vQgkhuxfmr8q66g7qRKbD5u5moBwbfQyU0Y4MW5kjHxQ7tGWrz3S4RURITrZIgAP3FipQT69eqqcUwDADUIwklWRbKJPtnIfG6keUfA1RfRhmMV4ntJ-YRwRtBB9Q2ii6zExEIguXXTTcA1Gy7BpYfbGA9O5qsoxJ-TLJ71uwWPwMf7Pp5bJmq_aliFQQSAX_VUXOGOShJV2/4ba/XTOAnapQTOaqknBxszMN2g/h3/h001.-EUrpuN7mxe_jbIssVgs_E1DJN7HBMKgrSBbHZYuAWE)**. Additionally, while the measure to codify abortion in Florida’s state constitution did not reach the 60% needed to pass, it did get over 57% of the vote, making Floridians desire for abortion access clear.

It was a great event facilitated by KPEF’s Rebecca Susman with Tyler Gillett, Staff Attorney at the Women’s Law Project, Sydney Etheredge, President and CEO of Planned Parenthood of Western PA, and Alecia Ott, Founder of The Auto(nomous) Body Shop! Take a listen here: **[(In) Accessible podcast](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.mohcMWigGtDpp7Zj47amQrW1ppR1ZTWuh-aLNstthId5reXCf8oXAqV-ZIiQMv7VIe3TPcThcLuxFEUPqFvKbJdM-dQ5Ui8wxdMuSjqSeZQuHuYXQ6do0PkPWJmp1thkHFiLNOoJGGhVO4Ec9NUN9VIzU-nl9GJrsUMR9dDlX_RzKyb6WGNKZ7XqNGRp7oc8ij31_qVl6n1lCw7aXrKC-McjZrwR6RcipHATo6Gre_qyY4f4ehANo-49kRO0ozlNtltsTlGUvSt_bLun6luSfCUVFCg6qPUohc4rrzSWWH0fKIHGG5Pj8fIEs0nk-srpFUE1QQ0CxDmQ-KW2g1eP_w/4ba/XTOAnapQTOaqknBxszMN2g/h5/h001.p2okWl-arnuysRZHbAOsU3l4qfP5snIS7--BJo2yRuM)**

clean_body: Reeling from the results? Take a breath and we'll move forward together | | A Post-Election Note Like many of you, we’re reeling from this week’s election results. It’s a hard pill to swallow and many of the races signal an enormous amount of work ahead to fight for a future where we are all valued and able to live without fear. This newly elected administration and congress have been loud and clear about the fascist future they intend to implement through their path of . But, our progressive movement is just that — movement! It means that we join together to regroup, shift, and continue forward with a more impactful, cohesive strategy. | This election made it resoundingly clear that we not only have to fight these policies, but also must focus on public education, creating better tools, and framing information to provide concrete ways for people to take action. We at Keystone Progress Education Fund are committed to deepening and expanding this work to move our country in a progressive direction that recognizes the strength in our diverse communities and improves our quality of life. We hope that you will join us in the fight ahead — because, make no mistake, it will be a fight. An important note: not all of the Pennsylvania elections have been called! Stay up to date on all of Pennsylvania's election results, including the yet to be determined Senate seat . Repro Roundup This week’s election also caused major changes in the country's reproductive rights landscape. Learn more about the measures passed that protect abortion access . Additionally, while the measure to codify abortion in Florida’s state constitution did not reach the 60% needed to pass, it did get over 57% of the vote, making Floridians desire for abortion access clear. (In)Accessible Podcast: Bonus Episode! The Art of Democracy: Whose Choice? Panel Discussion Did you miss the October 8th panel discussion at our The Art of Democracy: Whose Choice? Don't worry, we made it into a for you! It was a great event facilitated by KPEF’s Rebecca Susman with Tyler Gillett, Staff Attorney at the Women’s Law Project, Sydney Etheredge, President and CEO of Planned Parenthood of Western PA, and Alecia Ott, Founder of The Auto(nomous) Body Shop! Take a listen here: | Check us out! Stay up-to-date on the latest progressive events, news, and resources. Don’t forget to give us a ! In Solidarity, The Team at Keystone Progress Education Fund + Keystone Progress With your support we’re breaking down barriers to voter participation across Western PA. Educational materials and programs like the Art of Democracy, our repro rights podcast (In)Accessible, and our new Disability Justice program.Together, we’re fighting for a progressive future at the polls and beyond! | | | | | | | Sent via . name: Nancy Pelosi
subject: 🚨 EMERGENCY TEAM PELOSI MEETUP TO HELP WIN THE HOUSE + SAVE OUR HEALTH CARE
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Nancy Pelosi for Congress
body:
---
| | | |
---
Friends,
While we grieve the end of Kamala Harris’ historic and inspiring race for President, we continue the campaign to win back the House.
Right now, the margins in several House races remain too close to call and our Democratic candidates have a chance to win — with OUR help.
Winning the House can literally be the bulwark for our Democracy — and for the cause of our lives: health care.
Since we passed the Affordable Care Act, our ever-expanding coalition has delivered, saved and strengthened health care — and now we must save it again for all Americans, particularly our most vulnerable.
Join me for an EMERGENCY MEETUP to discuss how we will organize to Save Our Health Care — now, by curing ballots in close House races, and going forward, with the outside mobilization we need to counter extreme MAGA Republicans’ assaults on the ACA.
TODAY Friday, November 8th at 4pm ET / 1pm PT
Sign Up HERE: **[_https://streamyard.com/watch/WgBiPa9qfdfX_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96796810/511218037/-942289773?refcode=20241108_em_ex_b1_np4c_grassroots&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDIvMS84ODQ2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwYzk2MTJiNC1lZTlkLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=YQq6iFD5bOVsSnhxL6kc0cKg2E1knVcSRcUISIWouW4=&emci=47a3ade7-eb9d-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=0c9612b4-ee9d-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=16320219)**

name: Jacky Rosen
subject: Nevada’s Senate race is not over
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ELISSA SLOTKIN FOR MICHIGAN
body:
We can’t afford to lose another Senate seat
# [](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/188?t=1&akid=2196%2E135344%2E7cVS65)

Here's where things stand: **Nevada’s Senate race is not over – which means our work isn’t done yet. And we’re expecting my race to come down to a handful of votes.**
We cannot afford to lose a single additional Senate seat. If Republicans flip Nevada red, they’ll have even more power to plow through with their destructive agenda – including passing a nationwide abortion ban.
Winning is going to require ensuring that every last vote gets properly counted. So today, I need to make the most important ask I've ever made of you: [**Please, will you split $24 now – or whatever you can spare – between my campaign and Elissa Slotkin to show the huge wave of support we need to keep Nevada blue?**](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/9?c0=ebs-em-rosen-nov24.Q1FQsH&refcode=em241108-1-900&t=2&akid=2196%2E135344%2E7cVS65)
**[CONTRIBUTE $24](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/9?c0=ebs-em-rosen-nov24.Q1FQsH&refcode=em241108-1-900&amount=%2424&t=3&akid=2196%2E135344%2E7cVS65)**
We laid it all on the line to counter their attacks and turn Nevada blue. In order to do that, we had to spend every last dollar we had just to get through Election Day – because our race was one of the most expensive in the whole country.
Our work isn’t done until every vote in Nevada is counted. But now, we’re starting all over again from scratch, which is why we’ve set an emergency goal to raise $500,000. And now, the stakes are higher than ever – we absolutely need to reach this goal to win.
**So please, I'm counting on you to come through in the most pivotal moment:** [**Split $24 or more right now to help my campaign get across the finish line.**](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/9?c0=ebs-em-rosen-nov24.Q1FQsH&refcode=em241108-1-900&t=4&akid=2196%2E135344%2E7cVS65)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly between Elissa Slotkin and Jacky Rosen:_
| | **[Donate $24](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/127?c0=ebs-em-rosen-nov24.Epqg1T&amount=24&refcode=em241108-1-900&t=23&akid=2196%2E135344%2E7cVS65)**
---
****
| **[Donate $35](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/2881?c0=ebs-em-rosen-nov24.QOTlij&refcode=em241108-1-900&t=24&akid=2196%2E135344%2E7cVS65)**
---
****
| | **[Donate $50](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/122?c0=ebs-em-rosen-nov24.IUtufg&refcode=em241108-1-900&t=25&akid=2196%2E135344%2E7cVS65)**
---
****
| **[Donate $100](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/123?c0=ebs-em-rosen-nov24.0Pdf--&refcode=em241108-1-900&t=26&akid=2196%2E135344%2E7cVS65)**
---
****
| | **[Donate $250](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/124?c0=ebs-em-rosen-nov24.z07ZOx&refcode=em241108-1-900&t=27&akid=2196%2E135344%2E7cVS65)**
---
****
| **[Other Amount](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/127?c0=ebs-em-rosen-nov24.Epqg1T&refcode=em241108-1-900&t=28&akid=2196%2E135344%2E7cVS65)**
---
****
Thank you so much.
Jacky

PAID FOR BY ELISSA SLOTKIN FOR MICHIGAN
P.O. Box 4145
East Lansing, MI 48826
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a very important way we communicate with supporters like you, but if you want to, you can [ unsubscribe here](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=29&akid=2196%2E135344%2E7cVS65).

name: Ruben Gallego
subject: You have an important role to play right now, Peter.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TAMMY BALDWIN FOR SENATE
body:
| I’m confident that — after all the votes are counted — we’re going to win. But it’s going to take a little while until that happens. And I can tell you this, for sure: Whatever the result is, it’s going to be close. _Tammy Baldwin here — I wanted to make sure you saw this message from Ruben Gallego. Ruben's Senate race in Arizona is still too close to call, so I hope you'll read his message and[chip in if you can.](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/2155?c0=tbe-em-gallego.sustuI&refcode=AU_STB_FR_TAN_GEN_AC_20241108_AM1_V1_S1&t=3&akid=4516%2E35263%2EMW-166)_
* * *
[](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/2155?c0=tbe-em-gallego.sustuI&refcode=AU_STB_FR_TAN_GEN_AC_20241108_AM1_V1_S1&t=4&akid=4516%2E35263%2EMW-166) Peter — When I first launched this campaign, I never would’ve dreamed that this many people would have my back. It’s unbelievable, and I’ll always be grateful for everything we built together. Here’s where we’re at in Arizona: I’m confident that — after all the votes are counted — we’re going to win. But it’s going to take a little while until that happens. And I can tell you this, for sure: Whatever the result is, it’s going to be close. Unfortunately, my opponent has a history of fanning the flames of conspiracy and disinformation. She’s already done a ton of damage to my state and to our country, but I’m not about to back down. Maybe it’s the Marine in me, but I’ll always fight for our democracy. And you have a critical role in that fight, if you’re up for it. [I know you’ve already given so much, but I’ve got to ask you one more time, Peter.] [**If you’re looking for some way to have an impact and help us fight back against Kari Lake as we work to ensure the votes are counted, please make a split donation to my campaign and Tammy Baldwin.**](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/2533?c0=tbe-em-gallego.JRYRxA&refcode=AU_STB_FR_TAN_GEN_AC_20241108_AM1_V1_S1&t=5&akid=4516%2E35263%2EMW-166) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation to Ruben Gallego and Tammy Baldwin will go through immediately:_ | | [SPLIT $10 NOW](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/3664?c0=tbe-em-gallego.ABg2Fw&refcode=AU_STB_FR_TAN_GEN_AC_20241108_AM1_V1_S1&t=6&akid=4516%2E35263%2EMW-166)

| [SPLIT $50 NOW](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/3666?c0=tbe-em-gallego.2cMIE0&refcode=AU_STB_FR_TAN_GEN_AC_20241108_AM1_V1_S1&t=8&akid=4516%2E35263%2EMW-166)

[ANOTHER AMOUNT](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/2591?c0=tbe-em-gallego.Mdq-Q1&refcode=AU_STB_FR_TAN_GEN_AC_20241108_AM1_V1_S1&t=11&akid=4516%2E35263%2EMW-166)

Thanks again, and Semper Fi.
Ruben
P.O. BOX 696 MADISON, WI 53701

name: Team Baker
subject: Next Steps Meeting Postponed
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Karen Baker for the 9th District VA[View email in browser](https://mailchi
body:
| | | | | [](https://bakerforva9th.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=50da08ea7a2ab9a8c73259dcd&id=0d8afe9373&e=8cac9e7f7d)
---
| | | | | Dear Dedicated Supporters,
As many of you know, we planned to have a Next Steps meeting on Sunday, November 10th in Dublin at the New River Community College. After thinking further about this and the result of elections across the country, we have decided to postpone this meeting. Everyone needs time to breath, to process, and to spend time with friends and family. We have so much to be proud of and we will revisit this after the holidays.
Thank you,Team Baker
---
| | | | | | | | Paid for by Karen Baker for the 9th District VA[View email in browser](https://mailchi.mp/bakerforva9th/make-a-difference-in-the-9th-district-6415611?e=8cac9e7f7d)
Karen Baker for Congress * PO Box 239 * Floyd, VA 24091-0239 * USA

name: Ruben Gallego (via DLCC.org)
subject: We’re going to fight our way to the finish→
disclaimer_text: Paid for by DLCC PAC
body:
I’ve been through some tough times in my life. Times when it seemed foolish to hope, when the outcome was uncertain. I got through those tough times by fighting my way to the other side. Today, tomorrow, and the next day, we have to fight. | | |
---
| |
---|---
[](https://an.dlcc.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqvLlvOtFHcHSW1T-Hwi1Or_STOtPW0JKKFHiXz6TXsPhh6VRk3tcs3IxBE4XfjK55bMLsG_NVfo_syMDEnReuvJSHVSPZLSm2hmmvHWRBIylYpxje3WZWYa1-F0qWty7MWbPibIzPuvxvK4_jva8CohIAZ4FlxkDTV-jzJvEiEYoadQgknb8QJVeDJXGwWutyBbeQP9QyxMng-lbTbMHOZ-HmYHTMWFPUQ_h9VUSTlLBE32ysI7xvf4s3FuuXc07JzkTeN78BhgsGEox7ASYBu1tx_VrSCezVrThX8S85gKEHj3f8CNd7p7flrSXzSpcz4YYZpN6tFGEM22D9hi0X7d5WWuMnQPWX4h_pl2QTbyHLQ3eNxxyofEjuduxe83FACqnxqnLlBkfJ9Vhn8CLqXV/4ba/CJdN2Hd4QNyE-iENQmv0_w/h0/h001.m2DNIeRCOxAicPxWclQ4QVD-qVq-k1Q_xSCUJSwhhGQ) Team, I know the last few days haven’t gone how we’d hoped. I’ve been through some tough times in my life. Times when it seemed foolish to hope, when the outcome was uncertain. I got through those tough times by fighting my way to the other side. Today, tomorrow, and the next day, we have to fight. With Republican control of the Senate assured, every seat that we win is a check on their control. I won’t shy away from working with anyone if it’s in the best interest of Arizona, but whatever power we retain is a mechanism for fighting the worst that might come our way. My race is still too close to call. There are hundreds of thousands of votes yet to be counted. We need to run through the tape and win this seat in Arizona. [ Please, make a split donation to my campaign and the DLCC. One last push to victory in Arizona, and an investment in building the next generation of Democratic leaders in the states. This matters. And I would not be asking you for help if it didn’t. ](https://an.dlcc.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqvLlvOtFHcHSW1T-Hwi1Or_STOtPW0JKKFHiXz6TXsPhh6VRk3tcs3IxBE4XfjK55bMLsG_NVfo_syMDEnReuvJSHVSPZLSm2hmmvHWRBIylYpxje3WZWYa1-F0qWty7MWbPibIzPuvxvK4_jva8CohIAZ4FlxkDTV-jzJvEiEYoatAR01MM8i5DGUIH4Qlp-2xOvUZg8YUnba1qsyk2XYdIwb9Cl1TaXM5RO9LW-yuMSpplGumf3pHX80cij4gN4fDNdYtnxSuQpE3Q08X85EDKp0rODwBZzRMR5u64GRoIZ5Z8pOr0Oy-o1sRYrall2JaXplrKWc7MQnp1g_ns51K7wYEcUqxK5dUS-TW33NvXndFQ7Tr8jFezVnVo8LMbbAyQuPwRF7GkfvuwRQgve4Bdy_VubX6Z0Q3YJHtSqYqhw/4ba/CJdN2Hd4QNyE-iENQmv0_w/h1/h001.-Zyk7pMRCuz075qGXn-UOF0ag1sqasqwgyLz_zXnX1w) | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately and be split between Ruben Gallego and DLCC PAC.

clean_body: I’ve been through some tough times in my life. Times when it seemed foolish to hope, when the outcome was uncertain. I got through those tough times by fighting my way to the other side. Today, tomorrow, and the next day, we have to fight. | | | | | ---|--- Team, I know the last few days haven’t gone how we’d hoped. I’ve been through some tough times in my life. Times when it seemed foolish to hope, when the outcome was uncertain. I got through those tough times by fighting my way to the other side. Today, tomorrow, and the next day, we have to fight. With Republican control of the Senate assured, every seat that we win is a check on their control. I won’t shy away from working with anyone if it’s in the best interest of Arizona, but whatever power we retain is a mechanism for fighting the worst that might come our way. My race is still too close to call. There are hundreds of thousands of votes yet to be counted. We need to run through the tape and win this seat in Arizona. | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately and be split between Ruben Gallego and DLCC PAC. | | | | | | Thank you, Ruben | Democratic Legislative Campaign Committee 1225 Eye Street NW Suite 1250, Washington, DC 20005 Paid for by DLCC PAC. Gabe Vasquez won the most competitive Democratic seat in Congress in 2022. He's running to be a champion for working families and build an economy that benefits everyone. The race for CA-27 is too close to call, while control of the U.S. House is still up for grabs. Team — we have an urgent update from a key California House race where the election is still too close to call. Former NASA chief of staff George Whitesides, whose tossup race in CA-27 has yet to be called, is only down by a few thousand votes. George could be headed for a recount, so we're mobilizing our supporters to ensure he has the resources to count every vote fairly and accurately. [The House is still up for grabs, and we need all hands on deck to keep our hopes of a House majority alive. Can you split $10 between my campaign and George Whitesides' recount fund today? We know the presidential and senate outcomes were disappointing, but in California, thousands of ballots are still being counted and George's race could go either way.
We need to make sure every vote gets counted — because everyone deserves to have their voices heard. Our House majority could come down to just a handful of votes in California.
[George Whitesides needs our help to keep fighting for our values. Will you split $10 between my campaign and George's recount fund as the final ballots in this key toss up race are counted?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96797608/511220549/1005217641?amount=10&source=SBD_GV_EM_FR_110824_1_1_WHITESIDES&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzAxMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmNjFjM2FkMi1mMDlkLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=wpY3McKIPlK_0uyCqVHbUGUlmYTTWtiw9W8_M8a01IY=)

| | Peter, I know many of us are still processing the results of Tuesday's election and what it means for our district and our nation. Donald Trump's victory presents challenges we must now confront together, and we'll need a strong presence in the People's House of Representatives to find the Big Middle, breaking down partisan divides. Results are still coming in, and it looks like our race may result in a recount despite our small lead. That's why we must do everything we can to win the House or limit the GOP majority - that means holding the line in OH-09. Peter, I need your support to fund our recount efforts and help us get over the finish line. Can I count on you right now? **Here's the link:**[**https://secure.actblue.com/donate/recount_em**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96797783/511220981/653402453?refcode=2411_mk_recount_partners&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS85MzczOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjMDMyNDI0OS1mMTlkLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=6tY-8BaaUdGLfdS-jZUm4yCvsb28u5m2_NDNaYSfLsA=) Thank you so much. — Marcy

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: Read my op-ed on where we go from here
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for Senate
body:
Warren for Senate: https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/370?source=em-header&t=1001&akid=2412%2E4032634%2ECUqAF_

There’s a big question that many people are asking at their
dinner tables, in their group chats, and quietly within themselves: “Where
do we go from here?”

I wrote down my thoughts in TIME to start to answer this question. You can
read it below, [ https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/2840?t=1006&akid=2412%2E4032634%2ECUqAF_ ]or visit the link here to share with the people you care
about.

We have two tasks ahead. First, learn from what happened. And then, make a
plan.

What we do next is important. And I need you in this fight with me.

Thanks for being a part of this,

Elizabeth

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

Sen. Elizabeth Warren: Here's the Plan to Fight Back

To everyone who feels like their heart has been ripped out of their chest,
I feel the same. To everyone who is afraid of what happens next, I share
your fears. But what we do next is important, and I need you in this fight
with me.

As we confront a second Donald Trump presidency, we have two tasks ahead.
First, try to learn from what happened. And then, make a plan.

Many political experts and D.C. insiders are already blaming President Joe
Biden’s economic agenda for Vice President Kamala Harris’ loss. This does
not stand up to scrutiny. Even though the Biden economy produced strong
economic growth while reining in inflation, incumbent parties across the
globe have been tossed out by voters after the pandemic. American voters
also showed support for Democratic economic policies, for example,
approving ballot initiatives to raise the minimum wage in Alaska and to
guarantee paid sick leave in Missouri.

But good economic policies do not erase painful underlying truths about
our country. For my entire career, I’ve studied how the system is rigged
against working-class families. On paper, the U.S. economy is the
strongest in the world. But working families are struggling with big
expenses like the cost of housing, health care, and childcare. Giant
corporations get tax breaks and favorable rules while workers are gouged
by higher prices. Billionaires pay paltry taxes on their wealth while
families can’t afford to buy their first homes.

Americans do not want a country where political parties each field their
own team of billionaires who then squabble over how to divvy up the spoils
of government. Vice President Harris deserves credit for running an
inspiring campaign under unprecedented circumstances. But if Democrats
want to earn back the trust of working people and govern again, we need to
convince voters we can—and will—unrig the economy.

What comes next? Trump won the election, but more than 67 million people
voted for Democrats and they don’t expect us to roll over and play dead.
We will have a peaceful transition of power, followed by a vigorous
challenge from the party out of power, because that’s how democracy works.
Here’s a path forward.

First, fight every fight in Congress.

We won’t always win, but we can slow or sometimes limit Trump’s
destruction. With every fight, we can build political power to put more
checks on his administration and build the foundation for future wins.
Remember that during the first Trump term, mass mobilization—including
some of the largest peaceful protests in world history—was the battery
that charged the resistance. There is power in solidarity, and we can’t
win if we don’t get in the fight.

During the Trump years, Congress stepped up its oversight of his
unprecedented corruption and abuses of power. In the Senate, Democrats
gave no quarter to radical Trump nominees; we asked tough questions and
held the Senate floor for hours to slow down confirmation and expose
Republican extremism. These tactics doomed some nominations entirely, laid
the groundwork for other cabinet officials to later resign in disgrace,
and brought scrutiny that somewhat constrained Trump’s efforts.

When all this work came together, we won some of the toughest fights.
Remember Republicans’ attempts to repeal the Affordable Care Act?
Democrats did not have the votes to stop the repeal. Nevertheless, we
fought on. Patients kept up a relentless rotation of meetings in Congress,
activists in wheelchairs performed civil disobedience, and lawmakers used
every tactic possible—late night speeches, forums highlighting patient
stories, committee reports, and procedural tactics—to draw attention to
the Republican repeal effort. This sustained resistance ultimately shifted
the politics of health care repeal. The final vote was a squeaker, but
Republicans lost and the ACA survived.

Democrats should also acknowledge that seeking a middle ground with a man
who calls immigrants “animals” and says he will “protect” women “whether
the women like it or not” is unlikely to land in a good place. Uniting
against Trump’s legislative agenda is good politics because it is good
policy. It was Democratic opposition to Trump’s tax bill that drove
Trump’s approval ratings to what was then the lowest levels of his
administration, forcing Republicans to scrap all mention of the law ahead
of the 2018 midterm election and helping spark one of the largest blue
waves in recent history.

Second, fight Trump in the courts.

Yes, extremist courts, including a Supreme Court stocked with MAGA
loyalists, are poised to rubber-stamp Trump’s lawlessness. But litigation
can slow Trump down, give us time to prepare and help the vulnerable, and
deliver some victories.

Third, focus on what each of us can do.

I understand my assignment in the Senate, but we all have a part to play.
During the first Trump administration, Democrats vigorously contested
every special election and laid the groundwork to take back the House in
the 2018 midterms, creating a powerful check on Trump and breaking the
Republican trifecta. Whether it’s stepping up to run for office,
supporting a neighbor’s campaign, or getting involved in an organization
taking action, we all have to continue to make investments in our
democracy—including in states that are passed over as “too red.” The
political position we’re in is not permanent, and we have the power to
make change if we fight for it.

Finally, Democrats currently in office must work with urgency.

While still in charge of the Senate and the White House, we must do all we
can to safeguard our democracy. To resist Trump’s threats to abuse state
power against what he calls “the enemy within,” Pentagon leaders should
issue a directive now reiterating that the military’s oath is to the
Constitution. Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer must use every minute
of the end-of-year legislative session to confirm federal judges and key
regulators—none of whom can be removed by the next President.

To those feeling despair: I understand. But remember, every step toward
progress in American history came after the darkness of defeat.
Abolitionists, suffragettes, Dreamers, and marchers for civil rights and
marriage equality all faced impossible odds, but they persisted. Now it is
our turn to pull up our socks and get back in the fight.

clean_body: Warren for Senate: There’s a big question that many people are asking at their dinner tables, in their group chats, and quietly within themselves: “Where do we go from here?” I wrote down my thoughts in TIME to start to answer this question. You can read it below, [ ]or visit the link here to share with the people you care about. We have two tasks ahead. First, learn from what happened. And then, make a plan. What we do next is important. And I need you in this fight with me. Thanks for being a part of this, Elizabeth Sen. Elizabeth Warren: Here's the Plan to Fight Back To everyone who feels like their heart has been ripped out of their chest, I feel the same. To everyone who is afraid of what happens next, I share your fears. But what we do next is important, and I need you in this fight with me. As we confront a second Donald Trump presidency, we have two tasks ahead. First, try to learn from what happened. And then, make a plan. Many political experts and D.C. insiders are already blaming President Joe Biden’s economic agenda for Vice President Kamala Harris’ loss. This does not stand up to scrutiny. Even though the Biden economy produced strong economic growth while reining in inflation, incumbent parties across the globe have been tossed out by voters after the pandemic. American voters also showed support for Democratic economic policies, for example, approving ballot initiatives to raise the minimum wage in Alaska and to guarantee paid sick leave in Missouri. But good economic policies do not erase painful underlying truths about our country. For my entire career, I’ve studied how the system is rigged against working-class families. On paper, the U.S. economy is the strongest in the world. But working families are struggling with big expenses like the cost of housing, health care, and childcare. Giant corporations get tax breaks and favorable rules while workers are gouged by higher prices. Billionaires pay paltry taxes on their wealth while families can’t afford to buy their first homes. Americans do not want a country where political parties each field their own team of billionaires who then squabble over how to divvy up the spoils of government. Vice President Harris deserves credit for running an inspiring campaign under unprecedented circumstances. But if Democrats want to earn back the trust of working people and govern again, we need to convince voters we can—and will—unrig the economy. What comes next? Trump won the election, but more than 67 million people voted for Democrats and they don’t expect us to roll over and play dead. We will have a peaceful transition of power, followed by a vigorous challenge from the party out of power, because that’s how democracy works. Here’s a path forward. First, fight every fight in Congress. We won’t always win, but we can slow or sometimes limit Trump’s destruction. With every fight, we can build political power to put more checks on his administration and build the foundation for future wins. Remember that during the first Trump term, mass mobilization—including some of the largest peaceful protests in world history—was the battery that charged the resistance. There is power in solidarity, and we can’t win if we don’t get in the fight. During the Trump years, Congress stepped up its oversight of his unprecedented corruption and abuses of power. In the Senate, Democrats gave no quarter to radical Trump nominees; we asked tough questions and held the Senate floor for hours to slow down confirmation and expose Republican extremism. These tactics doomed some nominations entirely, laid the groundwork for other cabinet officials to later resign in disgrace, and brought scrutiny that somewhat constrained Trump’s efforts. When all this work came together, we won some of the toughest fights. Remember Republicans’ attempts to repeal the Affordable Care Act? Democrats did not have the votes to stop the repeal. Nevertheless, we fought on. Patients kept up a relentless rotation of meetings in Congress, activists in wheelchairs performed civil disobedience, and lawmakers used every tactic possible—late night speeches, forums highlighting patient stories, committee reports, and procedural tactics—to draw attention to the Republican repeal effort. This sustained resistance ultimately shifted the politics of health care repeal. The final vote was a squeaker, but Republicans lost and the ACA survived. Democrats should also acknowledge that seeking a middle ground with a man who calls immigrants “animals” and says he will “protect” women “whether the women like it or not” is unlikely to land in a good place. Uniting against Trump’s legislative agenda is good politics because it is good policy. It was Democratic opposition to Trump’s tax bill that drove Trump’s approval ratings to what was then the lowest levels of his administration, forcing Republicans to scrap all mention of the law ahead of the 2018 midterm election and helping spark one of the largest blue waves in recent history. Second, fight Trump in the courts. Yes, extremist courts, including a Supreme Court stocked with MAGA loyalists, are poised to rubber-stamp Trump’s lawlessness. But litigation can slow Trump down, give us time to prepare and help the vulnerable, and deliver some victories. Third, focus on what each of us can do. I understand my assignment in the Senate, but we all have a part to play. During the first Trump administration, Democrats vigorously contested every special election and laid the groundwork to take back the House in the 2018 midterms, creating a powerful check on Trump and breaking the Republican trifecta. Whether it’s stepping up to run for office, supporting a neighbor’s campaign, or getting involved in an organization taking action, we all have to continue to make investments in our democracy—including in states that are passed over as “too red.” The political position we’re in is not permanent, and we have the power to make change if we fight for it. Finally, Democrats currently in office must work with urgency. While still in charge of the Senate and the White House, we must do all we can to safeguard our democracy. To resist Trump’s threats to abuse state power against what he calls “the enemy within,” Pentagon leaders should issue a directive now reiterating that the military’s oath is to the Constitution. Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer must use every minute of the end-of-year legislative session to confirm federal judges and key regulators—none of whom can be removed by the next President. To those feeling despair: I understand. But remember, every step toward progress in American history came after the darkness of defeat. Abolitionists, suffragettes, Dreamers, and marchers for civil rights and marriage equality all faced impossible odds, but they persisted. Now it is our turn to pull up our socks and get back in the fight. But I did want to let you know that amidst some terrible losses, Educated We Stand stood firm against the tide of extremism in our schools. As results continue to come in from Florida and Michigan, we’re seeing victories up and down our endorsement list—and that gives me hope, Peter. Since we began just a few short months ago, Educated We Stand has endorsed and supported 26 races in Florida and Michigan. **We won 20 of them!** **Our children are our future, and our future can only be better than the darkness we’re facing right now if we invest in it.** I can promise you Educated We Stand will fight against whatever the Trump Administration tries to do to the Department of Education. We will continue to work to elect commonsense School Board Members and erase Moms for Liberty’s influence in this country. name: Stephen Miller
subject: Maricopa County, AZ Election Lawsuit >>
body:
| | [](http://links.conservativeum.com/u/click?_t=26c2e4aa9bbe40418f61e26972138f21&_m=7adc355ebd6e468ca7c4517da9a1efb5&_e=-8czCr8TuJEl7xPA463sRh2yn6HITFNI6HG1l4xAsYgfJ-LqvlTmcf1ih7GtgF4eare0pQCjs9SHEwdQ2vNOZlqWqNTXrPmqQ0vdb-fgVlUeGpWrVY9qjaNORh5vOKrW-xNj3_WRpKu_YkMZBU3OC7V2P6ZABBw7ssnckYwVCa3GDdb7F9HyTfyTTXG8jveTOVIPXLNpgOyJNvmZuf_-fk9sU25rr5j8yw_cN-GJH39oVwpUspDbD_XVFPCnLIXRHNBJEH2xqsS-mPJtKMRQBPTVOajgCLF4XgRT2Ngv2KSCxdqV6Z48-mC5ID3WvjR04WThXMWo_A-aQIiOn1XYaODN0_IQ17r4Dj811dibKU6ydD9LzbpeRYfVcLu9z8mD9MBotypL1gEt5p80EcLf8RWdySlEr1M8foNH_fazbb0wdNQWzAhFnkMqeuoO2Jaf7596WgZXYcF8jKKbnxGs3Srzw5BloTxOQvxhCf6CuCkDqWM8QcgNK92Oum_L7veM)
---
Peter, I have breaking news to share with you... We at America First Legal have just filed an **absolutely momentous** lawsuit in Maricopa County, Arizona against their elections officials for repeatedly and egregiously violating the State of Arizona’s election administration laws. The election interference and systematic election law violations we allege in Arizona are appalling, bone-chilling, and have the most serious consequences for every American. We all know what’s going on. _That’s why we are taking action to expose and punish those responsible!_[_**DONATE NOW TO SUPPORT OUR EFFORTS >>** _](http://links.conservativeum.com/u/click?_t=26c2e4aa9bbe40418f61e26972138f21&_m=7adc355ebd6e468ca7c4517da9a1efb5&_e=-8czCr8TuJEl7xPA463sRh2yn6HITFNI6HG1l4xAsYgfJ-LqvlTmcf1ih7GtgF4eare0pQCjs9SHEwdQ2vNOZlqWqNTXrPmqQ0vdb-fgVlUeGpWrVY9qjaNORh5vOKrW-xNj3_WRpKu_YkMZBU3OC7V2P6ZABBw7ssnckYwVCa3GDdb7F9HyTfyTTXG8jveTOVIPXLNpgOyJNvmZuf_-fk9sU25rr5j8yw_cN-GJH39oVwpUspDbD_XVFPCnLIXRHNBJEH2xqsS-mPJtKMRQBPTVOajgCLF4XgRT2Ngv2KSCxdqV6Z48-mC5ID3WvjR04WThXMWo_A-aQIiOn1XYaI4K1iW4-gWKjPCAL20Eqh_lDROOtXHowg-7W1wbc9329_uRa1re5xuBANltpI2-DGIl0YkNPbetdDIFXxAcOI199Kv2Heh8rgaBC04FSBHwxt_U3epaUsoaC60pvn1D19OPkC46gPO7YQcULxN_Wcdk3SQxEGuqx6-rS3vGVMkS)**We strongly allege numerous serious violations and criminal activity, including:****#1 -** _Maricopa County refuses to maintain the mandatory chain of custody for ballots.**In 2022, these failures resulted in a discrepancy of over 25,000 votes – larger than the margin of victory in the state governor’s race**_**#2 -** _**Maricopa County ignores mandatory reconciliation procedures to track each ballot printed or issued to a voter.** The law requires tracking and reconciling ballots cast and voters checked in to stop fraud, but the Defendants do not perform any reconciliation procedures at all_**#3 -** _Arizona law requires ballot drop boxes to be staffed by at least two election officials positioned close enough to view each person who deposits ballots into it.**Maintaining an unstaffed drop box is a felony. However, Maricopa County maintains unstaffed, unsupervised ballot drop boxes, facilitating illegal ballot harvesting**_ For many years now, the Maricopa County Board of Supervisors and the County Recorder have consistently failed to lawfully administer elections. We believe these individuals are fully aware of their election law violations.**Make no mistake:** Our lawsuit has been filed to prevent the November 5, 2024, election from being tainted with the same mistakes and maladministration that occurred during the 2020 and 2022 elections and to prevent election interference from occurring._Thank you for your support of our mission and historic legal actions here!_[_**_PLEASE RUSH SUPPORT HERE_ >>**_](http://links.conservativeum.com/u/click?_t=26c2e4aa9bbe40418f61e26972138f21&_m=7adc355ebd6e468ca7c4517da9a1efb5&_e=-8czCr8TuJEl7xPA463sRh2yn6HITFNI6HG1l4xAsYgfJ-LqvlTmcf1ih7GtgF4eare0pQCjs9SHEwdQ2vNOZlqWqNTXrPmqQ0vdb-fgVlUeGpWrVY9qjaNORh5vOKrW-xNj3_WRpKu_YkMZBU3OC7V2P6ZABBw7ssnckYwVCa3GDdb7F9HyTfyTTXG8jveTOVIPXLNpgOyJNvmZuf_-fk9sU25rr5j8yw_cN-GJH39oVwpUspDbD_XVFPCnLIXRHNBJEH2xqsS-mPJtKMRQBPTVOajgCLF4XgRT2Ngv2KSCxdqV6Z48-mC5ID3WvjR04WThXMWo_A-aQIiOn1XYaLJCS7mVOoNQgEDQTc3kEoqc4rO5M3b6fVteqh2lqe_Uq0MG6p1t30QyxH4zEFiwcQElv7ePxbx0SxoMKDNECPkU5PMMCHDZZPlMP-dPGOgdNzjpIlTPJjmGNEJHywQB-a7x0dQwyU_I8yFxYy9sc2yNjULstASmJ7uUXb0Jqo7k)_** **_ Stephen Miller _President, America First Legal _

We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T8da489a0-bdb6-4978-be6a-dbf556c25234/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

| Peter, they're still tallying the votes in Nevada’s must-win Senate race, and we just finished an emergency meeting at Team Rosen headquarters. Here’s the latest: FIRST, experts everywhere – from CNN to The Washington Post to Cook Political Report – predicted that our race would be one of the closest in the country. THEN, national Republicans and their dark money allies poured $75 million into Nevada to smear Jacky's record and try to buy this seat. NOW, the final results are too close to call, votes are still being counted, and we have to be prepared for whatever the next few days have in store. We've set an urgent $500,000 goal to help us make sure every single vote is counted in Nevada, but we need your support to get there. [So please, before it's too late, split $10 or more right now to Jacky Rosen’s campaign and Blue Amp Action and it will go immediately toward ensuring every vote is counted in Nevada’s must-win battleground Senate race!](https://default.salsalabs.org/T3b9d44b9-feb8-41ca-84ef-97a201be8116/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

| | | About BlueAmp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! Please follow Cliff Schecter on Twitter @cliffschecter, or subscribe to The Takedown with Cliff Schecter on YouTube, to keep up to date with this battle for the heart and soul of our country. — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T76155208-53c6-4f34-a62b-2eb9e42e8d80/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._
