name: Dan Crenshaw
subject: How We Won and What It Means
body:
Breaking down what led to this historic win...
| | | | | | [ ](https://ctrk.klclick2.com/l/01JC6FDCGFS2TCNDVRGD1PTATK_0)
---
| Peter- Tuesday’s elections were a victory for the Republicans up and down the ballot. It was also a victory for common sense and America’s interests at home and abroad. I break down what led to this historic win for Republicans and what policies we can expect moving forward. **Thank you to everyone who donated to my campaign. Your support helped me win a resounding re-election to the House and allowed me to lift up my fellow conservatives up and down the ballot. It is an honor to represent Texas’ Second Congressional District and fight for you on the issues that matter most in Congress. It is going to be a busy two years, and I look forward to getting to work. ** Now, here’s your brief.
---
| | | |
---
| A HISTORIC REALIGNMENT
---
| |
---
| [**WATCH MY TAKE ON ELECTION NIGHT HERE**](https://ctrk.klclick2.com/l/01JC6FDCGFS2TCNDVRGD1PTATK_1) On Election Night, we witnessed a political realignment in real time. Just at the top of the ticket, President Trump made gains with virtually every single demographic group and in virtually every state and city in America, including traditional Democrat strongholds. If Republicans can maintain this strong performance (as we should), we have built a powerful coalition made up of a broad set of voters that will be very difficult for Democrats to overcome. Three charts from the Financial Times illustrate just how stark this shift was.
---
| |
---
| President Trump improved his standing with virtually every single demographic group in America. White non-college men, white non-college women, white voters, male voters, voters between the ages of 45 and 64, white college men, females, voters between the ages of 30 and 44, voters between the ages of 18 and 29, Hispanic voters, Asian voters, and Black voters all increased their support for President Trump. The *only* groups President Trump lost support with were voters over the age of 65 and white college women.
---
| |
---
| The second chart illustrates the localities that swung toward President Trump. Pay attention to the Northeast in New York and New Jersey, South Texas, and Southern California. These are traditionally blue areas, and President Trump improved his standing in every one of them. Just in South Texas, [Donald Trump won Starr County](https://ctrk.klclick2.com/l/01JC6FDCGFS2TCNDVRGD1PTATK_2) \- which Democrats have won since 1892 - with nearly 60% of the vote. That county is 97 percent Hispanic and has the longest Democratic voting streak of any county in the United States.
---
| |
---
| Now look at the state-by-state breakdown. Donald Trump improved his margins in every single state except for Washington and Utah, which saw minimal swings towards Democrats. This is a historic shift, and there are a couple of factors. The primary factor is one that has been apparent to any objective observer: the entire country was fed up with the party in charge of the White House. Every single election comes down to a simple calculus for voters - do they want change or more of the same? This was a change election fundamentally. Donald Trump was the change candidate (73% of voters according to exit polls said that he would bring needed change to the country). That’s why he won. But it is also deeper than that. President Trump’s message was straightforward and common sense: he promised to secure the border, tame inflation, keep taxes low, and restore American strength on the world stage. These are the primary functions voters expect in a President, and it turns out this common sense message resonated across the board. I provided a longer analysis on Wednesday, which you can watch in the video above. The entire debate is worth watching, as it includes an epic emotional breakdown from a far-left commentator that illustrates exactly why Democrats lost this election.
---
|
---
| WHAT TO EXPECT FROM A SECOND TRUMP TERM
---
| |
---
| [**IF YOU HAVE ANYONE SCARED OF WHAT A SECOND TRUMP TERM WILL LOOK LIKE, SHOW THEM THIS**](https://ctrk.klclick2.com/l/01JC6FDCGFS2TCNDVRGD1PTATK_3)
---
| The byproduct of a massive electoral shift that President Trump ushered in on Tuesday night was a down ballot romp for Republicans as well. We took back the United States Senate and are likely to maintain our majority in the House of Representatives. That will give President Trump a Congress that will work to pass his agenda. So, what will that agenda look like? Spoiler alert: It’s not Project 2025. A big reason Democrats lost in such a historic fashion is that they focused on taking down a platform (Project 2025) that every single Republican explicitly denounced, including and especially Donald Trump. Perhaps the reason they did that is because they could not sensationalize the actual Republican agenda, which is based primarily on commonsense conservative policies. If you want clues as to what a second Trump term will look like, you don’t have to look far. Just look at what he did during his first term. We reduced taxes and regulations. We implemented the most basic border security policies. We projected strength around the world. That’s the agenda he campaigned on in 2024 as well. Here’s the bottom line: we all lived through President Trump’s first term, and life was pretty great. Record-low unemployment, a booming economy, real focus on energy independence, securing our borders, and cutting waste. The scare tactics fall flat when you remember what actually happened. The lies don’t hold up against reality.
---
|
---
| IMPORTANT READS
---
| In the aftermath of the election, there are several pieces that provide sharp analysis of what we saw and what it means for both political parties and the country. For a self-reflection from a Kamala Harris voter on how Democrats lost the plot with voters in this election, read this op-ed from Bret Stephens: [A Party of Prigs and Pontificators Suffers a Humiliating Defeat](https://ctrk.klclick2.com/l/01JC6FDCGFS2TCNDVRGD1PTATK_4) For a breakdown on the demographic shifts evident in the election (and how those shifts debunk popular assumptions about what changing demographics in America mean for political power), read [Rich Lowry’s piece: Trump Shows Demography Isn’t Destiny.](https://ctrk.klclick2.com/l/01JC6FDCGFS2TCNDVRGD1PTATK_5) And to understand how Donald Trump took advantage of a balkanized media landscape and the decline in legacy media to own the new media environment, read this breakdown from the [Wall Street Journal: Trump’s Win Cemented It: New Media Is Leaving the Old Guard Behind](https://ctrk.klclick2.com/l/01JC6FDCGFS2TCNDVRGD1PTATK_6)
---
|
---
| | [ ](https://ctrk.klclick2.com/l/01JC6FDCGFS2TCNDVRGD1PTATK_7)
---
| | [ SHOP NOW ](https://ctrk.klclick2.com/l/01JC6FDCGFS2TCNDVRGD1PTATK_8)
---
| | | That's it for this week's campaign brief. Thank you for reading.
---
| In service,
---
| |
---
| Dan Crenshaw

clean_body: Breaking down what led to this historic win... | | | | | | | Peter- Tuesday’s elections were a victory for the Republicans up and down the ballot. It was also a victory for common sense and America’s interests at home and abroad. I break down what led to this historic win for Republicans and what policies we can expect moving forward. Thank you to everyone who donated to my campaign. Your support helped me win a resounding re-election to the House and allowed me to lift up my fellow conservatives up and down the ballot. It is an honor to represent Texas’ Second Congressional District and fight for you on the issues that matter most in Congress. It is going to be a busy two years, and I look forward to getting to work. Now, here’s your brief. | | | | | A HISTORIC REALIGNMENT | | | On Election Night, we witnessed a political realignment in real time. Just at the top of the ticket, President Trump made gains with virtually every single demographic group and in virtually every state and city in America, including traditional Democrat strongholds. If Republicans can maintain this strong performance (as we should), we have built a powerful coalition made up of a broad set of voters that will be very difficult for Democrats to overcome. Three charts from the Financial Times illustrate just how stark this shift was. | | | President Trump improved his standing with virtually every single demographic group in America. White non-college men, white non-college women, white voters, male voters, voters between the ages of 45 and 64, white college men, females, voters between the ages of 30 and 44, voters between the ages of 18 and 29, Hispanic voters, Asian voters, and Black voters all increased their support for President Trump. The only groups President Trump lost support with were voters over the age of 65 and white college women. | | | The second chart illustrates the localities that swung toward President Trump. Pay attention to the Northeast in New York and New Jersey, South Texas, and Southern California. These are traditionally blue areas, and President Trump improved his standing in every one of them. Just in South Texas, \- which Democrats have won since 1892 - with nearly 60% of the vote. That county is 97 percent Hispanic and has the longest Democratic voting streak of any county in the United States. | | | Now look at the state-by-state breakdown. Donald Trump improved his margins in every single state except for Washington and Utah, which saw minimal swings towards Democrats. This is a historic shift, and there are a couple of factors. The primary factor is one that has been apparent to any objective observer: the entire country was fed up with the party in charge of the White House. Every single election comes down to a simple calculus for voters - do they want change or more of the same? This was a change election fundamentally. name: The Voter Protection Project
subject: URGENT: The U.S. House is still up for grabs.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
| **The race for CA-27 is too close to call, while control of the U.S. House is still up for grabs.** Friend — we have an urgent update from a key California House race where the election is still too close to call.

Former NASA chief of staff George Whitesides, whose tossup race in CA-27 has yet to be called, is only down by a few thousand votes.

George could be headed for a recount, so we're mobilizing our supporters to ensure he has the resources to count every vote fairly and accurately.

[_The House is still up for grabs, and we need all hands on deck to keep our hopes of a House majority alive. Can you split $10 between the Voter Protection Project and George Whitesides' recount fund today? Help make sure every vote in CA-27 gets counted._](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/84865?t=6&akid=17991%2E878179%2ExRiCJh) If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation between George Whitesides - Recount Fund and VPP will go through immediately: | [CONTRIBUTE $10 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/84866?t=7&akid=17991%2E878179%2ExRiCJh)
---
[CONTRIBUTE $25 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/84867?t=8&akid=17991%2E878179%2ExRiCJh)
[CONTRIBUTE $50 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/84871?t=9&akid=17991%2E878179%2ExRiCJh)
[CONTRIBUTE $100 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/84869?t=10&akid=17991%2E878179%2ExRiCJh)
[Or a custom donation »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/84870?t=11&akid=17991%2E878179%2ExRiCJh)
We know the presidential and Senate outcomes were disappointing, but in California, thousands of ballots are still being counted and George's race could go either way.

We need to make sure every vote gets counted — because everyone deserves to have their voices heard. Our House majority could come down to just a handful of votes in California.

[_George Whitesides needs our help to keep fighting for our values. Will you split $10 between the Voter Protection Project and George's recount fund as the final ballots in this key toss up race are counted?_](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/84865?t=12&akid=17991%2E878179%2ExRiCJh)

Thanks for stepping up,

The Voter Protection Project
[DONATE $10 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/84865?t=13&akid=17991%2E878179%2ExRiCJh)
---
| | Paid for by VPP
(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=17991%2E878179%2ExRiCJh). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=17991%2E878179%2ExRiCJh).**

[Click here](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=17991%2E878179%2ExRiCJh) to request fewer emails. Click here to [unsubscribe](https://act.protectvoting.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=14&akid=17991%2E878179%2ExRiCJh). | | [Please: Pitch In Once A Month To Protect Voting Rights »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/123?t=4&akid=17991%2E878179%2ExRiCJh)

name: Sean Feucht
subject: 😅My wife roped me into running a half-marathon
body:
| | | [](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=dcac8242330e4c15a55d3b915d5debd3&_m=6dec5d158c8046e28ac0d9ba0888df2f&_e=ouFXh0YNuq2F27vYjyikoMjbfTR-5BEmWq6-uP5-biCsDkTOpI3tLJvApJWDVLypoUCAgu_B8sgIAI76YyVJiUm0qaQMwkZN-8lPPrSZUfzPJ0DPQoeOLBiEV0sIS2EtnUu0VIKS96GgoIF_WR-ImJKZiJx3wPT-H4odi6vlWeq-Hl_GDF9iId33aUzpbR1oZge3rBSPHAWKGgWDH192eaVg2PPRM52D6Pj593hYou5-CVwWkl3NeXsr7rdznvU-hdQ3-iMcyqm9C9awauwRum727cwJsl3TzcJ57s2KSMeYMD_jhjXzXHNTFq10y0Pb)
---
Dear Friend,
---
After spending the last two years traveling across America, holding over FIFTY praise and worship revivals, I planned on catching my breath. Nope! My wife roped me into running a half-marathon TOMORROW in Monterey, California! It's crazy, but it's happening!

I'm stepping up and RUNNING THE RACE, and I'm calling on Let Us Worship to have my back! I'm turning this into a run for revival in 2025, and raising money to help launch the next move of God in 2025.

Will you be a part of this crazy undertaking by sponsoring me? $5, $3—even just $1 per mile will help lay the groundwork for what God has next for the Let Us Worship movement! Watch the video and donate below!
---
[](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=dcac8242330e4c15a55d3b915d5debd3&_m=6dec5d158c8046e28ac0d9ba0888df2f&_e=ouFXh0YNuq2F27vYjyikoMjbfTR-5BEmWq6-uP5-biCsDkTOpI3tLJvApJWDVLypoUCAgu_B8sgIAI76YyVJiUm0qaQMwkZN-8lPPrSZUfzPJ0DPQoeOLBiEV0sIS2EtnUu0VIKS96GgoIF_WR-ImJKZiJx3wPT-H4odi6vlWeq8I7KpHLZCQVBboWZqWIdd6Yw58NpwdoX2Bh4_GGFLGxxvmUwXR1FA2Ad3V8i7-eK9c68kUNBoPY0LLxDnj4jBIfuiJzzPdaRoPYMLMLd8qR1oEZ_OSrt8qz0lgikvjH8d67hZypvlWDkx-0Wdd8X2)
---
All year, we've been on the Kingdom to the Capitol Tour, so I haven't been training, but I'm running the race anyways! Would you like to sponsor us as we run a half-marathon?
---

clean_body: | | | Dear Friend, After spending the last two years traveling across America, holding over FIFTY praise and worship revivals, I planned on catching my breath. Nope! My wife roped me into running a half-marathon TOMORROW in Monterey, California! It's crazy, but it's happening! I'm stepping up and RUNNING THE RACE, and I'm calling on Let Us Worship to have my back! I'm turning this into a run for revival in 2025, and raising money to help launch the next move of God in 2025. Will you be a part of this crazy undertaking by sponsoring me? $5, $3—even just $1 per mile will help lay the groundwork for what God has next for the Let Us Worship movement! Watch the video and donate below! All year, we've been on the Kingdom to the Capitol Tour, so I haven't been training, but I'm running the race anyways! Would you like to sponsor us as we run a half-marathon? Till all hear, Sean | | | INTERNATIONAL DONORS— DONATE- Donate by Check or Money Order Sean Feucht Ministries310 Third Street NEWashington, DC 20002 Contributions are tax-deductible. | | Email sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com by Let Us Worship

| | Dear Fellow American,

I'm glad you opened my email today! I have something urgent to ask you that will shape the future of our families, communities, and country.

[**Will you take our National Opinion Survey on Critical Race Theory (CRT)?**](http://links.redconservatives.com/u/click?_t=66ce1250519441959ebe70cf27686494&_m=7924a226d6c5434ea9126ffc4c2a3e2a&_e=bYznXCxnBWnkGx85GofvQjTaf7IEpNV15n1YnGtt3-yF0ts7Hs0pQQFM-F1KPpNG0rrikO-vmARustQNh2FAkv4TXS-KyB_o4iu6EYqAqHrPn-dku7vloj0d_631BaX16Gfmxj8haN0JufU4rUrQdhWPPSn2_kUuvvcr9cMrkqzkdQDev-aNmGZlZ8Gmo4z3rbe9YPDqYn0DVf-z1PzpKJwqVHwohWhZcByQVIAiYPkQGpBEwCuOf-s-exwf2awX1KlcOxCzgcEpvvR3FECCyIkIig_cvAUoYt7tGpMS6rkUe4WOCnC8AbNlOsfn0Aavl6jDya-IipVEokrvx_bchPr6X7xXHhy27LHtbjCYQyshObmqIC6fP8NFBQGG3OcJ)

Your participation will help us understand and address many Americans' concerns about CRT's influence on our society.

This survey will only take just a few minutes.

But in those few minutes, you'll help us gather important insights to defend America's founding principles of liberty...

...and ensure a bright future for our children and grandchildren.

**You can share your thoughts on CRT in the National Opinion Survey on Critical Race Theory Survey here:**

[**https://info.hillsdale.edu/crt-survey/**](http://links.redconservatives.com/u/click?_t=66ce1250519441959ebe70cf27686494&_m=7924a226d6c5434ea9126ffc4c2a3e2a&_e=bYznXCxnBWnkGx85GofvQjTaf7IEpNV15n1YnGtt3-yF0ts7Hs0pQQFM-F1KPpNG0rrikO-vmARustQNh2FAkv4TXS-KyB_o4iu6EYqAqHrPn-dku7vloj0d_631BaX16Gfmxj8haN0JufU4rUrQdhWPPSn2_kUuvvcr9cMrkqzkdQDev-aNmGZlZ8Gmo4z3rbe9YPDqYn0DVf-z1PzpKBgJB4nhhQeq6zkSuSLTEkpq4tHsP5C7QXkeL2oacRhGc5339_EqundP5NheWgeLhsl_NQWjUNVjae4h8CFon_mjuRs0SsXyVSQCmeDw5HMTmOCXpd8Y5idUsH20MShzh8ttLYHywFDMfxfFUMTm_guMRHYlhWLmKJ-Hnu61wdvO)

Sharing your opinion is an important way you'll combat the spread of CRT in our schools and workplaces. Together, we can preserve the constitutional and free-enterprise principles that made our nation so great and so free.

Thanks in advance! I look forward to seeing your response.

Bill Gray, '01

Institutional Advancement
Hillsdale College

name: JusticeDemocrats.com
subject: AIPAC failed to take down the Squad
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

Though the Democratic establishment failed to stop a second Trump
presidency and lost control of the U.S. Senate, our progressive incumbents
saw big wins across the board in this week’s general election.

Every single Justice Democrats-endorsed member of Congress won their
re-election bids on Tuesday. Right-wing groups like AIPAC and their
billionaire donors promised to take out every member of the Squad this
cycle for standing up for the needs of everyday people — they failed.

But they dragged our democracy through the mud to do it and had to spend
more than $20 million just to defeat a former nurse and principal in their
primary races this summer.

Congress is less representative without the voices of Representatives Cori
Bush and Jamaal Bowman, but their fights — and the rest of the Squad’s
victories — prove just how powerful everyday people can be.

Just look at Dearborn, Michigan, where Rep. Rashida Tlaib stood by her
constituents and loudly demanded the Biden administration be held
accountable for its complicity in Israel’s genocide against Palestinians:
she not only won re-election but did nearly twice as well as presidential
candidate Kamala Harris.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20241108?refcode=em20241108-36135&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=36135_1203867_4_Tf2n&akid=36135%2E1203867%2E4_Tf2n ]The Intercept: In Dearborn, Rashida Tlaib did nearly twice as well as
Kamala Harris. Harris refused to distance herself from the Biden
administration’s support of Israel’s war on Gaza. Tlaib rallied against
it.

Our nation is divided, our movement is fractured, and right-wing forces
are desperate to keep it that way. That’s why now is the time to build a
new Democratic Party, led by working class people fighting for working
class people.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20241108?refcode=em20241108-36135&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36135_1203867_4_Tf2n&akid=36135%2E1203867%2E4_Tf2n ]Help us elect the leaders we deserve and rebuild our bloc in Congress:
chip in anything you can afford to our 2025-2026 Candidate Recruitment
Fund today.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20241108?refcode=em20241108-36135&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36135_1203867_4_Tf2n&akid=36135%2E1203867%2E4_Tf2n ]Contribute $5

Every dollar will help us find the next set of progressive leaders
dedicated to taking on the status quo and truly fighting for the people.

In solidarity,

Justice Democrats

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20241108-36135-footer&t=1002&refcode2=36135_1203867_4_Tf2n&akid=36135%2E1203867%2E4_Tf2n

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1003&akid=36135%2E1203867%2E4_Tf2n

name: Wes Moore
subject: My statement on the outcome of the 2024 Presidential Election:
body:
Friend,

The Maryland State House is the place where General George Washington resigned his military commission in 1783 – the first time in our nation's history where the peaceful transfer of power took place and set the precedent for how electoral transitions should occur in this country. And this Tuesday, tens of millions of Americans went to the polls and exercised their democratic right and elected Donald Trump to be the next President of the United States.

Our state is grateful to the poll workers, volunteers, and elections officials who took on the mantle as custodians of our democratic process. I am thankful to Vice President Harris for her moving concession address and doing her part to advance the successful transfer of power with integrity and patriotism.

I also want the people of Maryland to know that the Moore-Miller Administration’s promise to leave no one behind remains steadfast, and we will continue to move forward as we always have in our state — together.

Elevate,

Wes

Contribute » [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20241108-1?refcode=em20241108-1]

---

_Out-of-state Democrat elites tried to tear us down, but we fought back!_

Now that we've officially WON re-election, I'm preparing my agenda for 2025 and need your help. I promise to continue to focus on the issues that matter most to true American patriots like you in 2025.
_**[Defending your Constitutional rights>>

Combating skyrocketing inflation >>

Securing our Southern border >>

Excessive contributions will be returned, refunded, redesignated or reattributed as provided by law. PO Box 11 Bondurant, IA 50035 Conservative Liberty Wire PO Box 1188 Fort Collins, CO 80522 | _

name: Diane Sare
subject: Tonight's Symposium--FDR: Let's Take Responsibility for the American Revolution
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Sare for Senate
body:
| | [](https://www.sareforsenate.com/?e=bd1d687cdeff164927fc1e159dca3e71&utm_source=sareforsenate&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=20241108_symposium&n=1)
---
Friend -- What can we learn from the American revolt which installed FDR in 1933 and, most importantly, what can we learn from the way FDR followed through on the election which put him into power? Americans, true to our tradition, used the 2024 elections to let the world know there is a brewing national disgust with the arbitrary rule of the arrogant self-appointed oligarchy -- the "economic royalists" as FDR called them-- which for years has only promoted their own predatory financial interests and their own sadistic policies of perpetual war. Where do we go from here? Let's look at the installation of FDR 93 years ago to consider the dangers and opportunities before us today What can we all, as part of the Sare/Vega campaign, do for the future success of the American Revolution? Can we revive the idea of government which follows the "consent of the governed" and implements policies for the general welfare of ourselves and our posterity -- worldwide? Tonight I will discuss these questions and more with FDR expert and EIR editor, Gerald Rose. Please join us!
### [New York Symposium, November 8 , 2024, 8:00 PM](https://www.sareforsenate.com/r?u=2PNlHEbVYFsh-F_FzIY8nSV4QqfEAzoeg2TE8gbGwTc&e=bd1d687cdeff164927fc1e159dca3e71&utm_source=sareforsenate&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=20241108_symposium&n=2)

Friend,

_Out-of-state Democrat elites tried to tear us down, but we fought back!_

Now that we've officially WON re-election, I'm preparing my agenda for 2025 and need your help. I promise to continue to focus on the issues that matter most to true American patriots like you in 2025.
_**[Defending your Constitutional rights>>

Combating skyrocketing inflation >>

Securing our Southern border >>

name: United College Park
subject: Much Ado About a Doo-Doo: When the Park Restroom Gets Shut Down
body:
| |
---
After $750,000 spent on a ‘smart’ restroom at Barrett Park, our city plans to close it for winter and replace it with a porta-potty. This isn’t just about bathrooms; it’s a symbol of short-sighted decisions and limited community input. Let’s push for accountability and practical solutions that respect our investments. | | [View in browser](https://exeprx.clicks.mlsend.com/tb/c/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEwNTM2NTUsXCJsXCI6MTM3NDYwNTM3NDYxMDQ4Mzc0LFwiclwiOjEzNzQ2MDU0ODcwMTc4Mzc4Mn0iLCJzIjoiMTYxMGI3YTc0OGY2Nzk4MSJ9)
| | |
---
|
---
| |
---
|
---
## Much Ado About a Doo-Doo: When the Park Restroom Gets Shut Down
| | |
---
|
### Something smells....
---
|
---
| |
---
|
---
We’ve all been through a lot lately—fundraisers, rallies, texts asking for donations, and what felt like endless campaign commercials! Sometimes, after all that, you just want a moment to sit back, relax, and decompress on your personal throne. But imagine this: you come home to find your newly renovated bathroom roped off, replaced with an outhouse in the backyard. 🤢
Sound absurd? Well, that’s exactly what’s happening at College Park’s Barrett Park. And something about it just doesn’t smell right.
After investing $750,000 in a “smart, safe” restroom with keypad access, many of us assumed this facility would be open year-round. Now, however, the city plans to shut it down for winter and swap it for a porta-potty—a plan led by our city manager Adediran and backed by Councilman Carn and Councilwoman McKenzie. The suggested reason? Freezing pipes. But for those of us who manage to keep our own pipes working fine every winter, this explanation feels, well, a little backed up.
[At Monday’s council meeting, things were flowing along smoothly](https://exeprx.clicks.mlsend.com/tb/c/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEwNTM2NTUsXCJsXCI6MTM3NDYwNTM3NDY4Mzg4NDEwLFwiclwiOjEzNzQ2MDU0ODcwMTc4Mzc4Mn0iLCJzIjoiYjEyMGNmYmEwY2NlZDc4ZSJ9) until Mayor Bianca Motley Broom raised questions residents have been asking. She wondered how closing this costly facility could be cheaper than simply maintaining it. And instead of addressing her questions directly, council members responded with a mixed bag of excuses—staffing, vandalism, freezing pipes—none of which explained why a brand-new facility can’t stay open.
With the decision to close Barrett Park’s restroom until spring, some questions remain unanswered. Let’s be clear... this may seem trivial, and yes, our city has bigger issues than bathrooms. But this situation is a prime example of how our local government gets bogged down with short-sighted solutions and dismisses resident input.
While a some leaders seem focused on keeping our amenities accessible and working for everyone, others appear quick to shut them down at the first sign of inconvenience, pushing through decisions with minimal discussion or regard for the community’s needs. This isn’t about taking sides; it’s about ensuring our community gets the transparency, accountability, and practical solutions we deserve. When taxpayer money funds public spaces, we have a right to know how these investments will be managed—and to expect decisions that put the long-term benefit of our community first.
This situation may seem small, but it points to a bigger issue: the need for thoughtful planning and open dialogue on every matter affecting College Park. Without these, we’re left with quick fixes and limited communication—when what we truly need are leaders willing to prioritize real solutions and keep residents informed every step of the way. **
Stay strong. Stay united.**
| |
---
| [ ](https://exeprx.clicks.mlsend.com/tb/c/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEwNTM2NTUsXCJsXCI6MTM3NDYwNTM3NDcyNTgyNzE2LFwiclwiOjEzNzQ2MDU0ODcwMTc4Mzc4Mn0iLCJzIjoiNTQ3ZWRlNzU0NTZlMGMyYSJ9)
---
| |
---
### ******Ep. 4 VOICES of College Park: holding officials accountable on actions & transparency**[](https://exeprx.clicks.mlsend.com/tb/c/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEwNTM2NTUsXCJsXCI6MTM3NDYwNTM3NDc1NzI4NDQ1LFwiclwiOjEzNzQ2MDU0ODcwMTc4Mzc4Mn0iLCJzIjoiYzhmOWYyZTNkZDQ0YzBiYyJ9)
****
Dom Kelly, chair of the political action committee United College Park, joins the podcast to discuss why the organization is committed to holding city officials accountable for approving rezonings, lands sales and more without residents' knowledge and input, or full transparency.

Members of United College Park are active in an effort to recall Councilwoman Jamelle McKenzie. The councilwoman as repeatedly stated that accusations against her are untrue and an act of retaliation by opposing voters who did not support her election in 2023.

Separate from United College Park, Dom is the co-founder, president & CEO of the nonprofit New Disabled South, which is committed to building a progressive future for disabled people in his region. He said he began to brainstorm the nonprofit in 2019.
| | [ Click here to view Voices of College Park ](https://exeprx.clicks.mlsend.com/tb/c/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjEwNTM2NTUsXCJsXCI6MTM3NDYwNTM3NDc4ODc0MTc1LFwiclwiOjEzNzQ2MDU0ODcwMTc4Mzc4Mn0iLCJzIjoiZTFjZDRiOTUyODI4N2YxYyJ9)

body:

If you'd like to unsubscribe [click here.](https://an.collectivepac.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm3-gP0Xd1LL-Lub7Gk22PHy9biRkT0ZFLBZo_8f8EnE7Jowo6A0R8oop8Rrw_kitS3sMwrJn2ozruxmkvaG936_l1duoWABImS72zia5WVvFdRmFyvM4hF9ZFRjVqWPG751754ZmsIOhKCDEzMWitsoKfCsf2JrYrwCyErGXiq6WTxXdMV6QHeZHFkeWOo_B5qS43KN7l7KQO1pI-l-A6mMY-DIZKcx6ZLZoM0sVUsSNj5sEHjS8T8MTOurGxY-UibEYs03RuJbYKFY2BHHC2J__aSj3Vk3gOI4ZsbiecaPBrZQZN6VQ_KuV47A6dweJGAhj_ytYK3kdy_VyppLkgYNn3FkfU9yh2rViGQlgpdeBBzbwi5rzDV662IBt1pxnV4m0I7uA_geQ95q_VjMvthlg6ltmPjxpNU3QCKvTLOwafp6ylewqlFAF1ua9euHg5Oo3dOAhoomMeJkzzGR4BUWm/4ba/qikLDyrHStqn8na0PNVpGA/h0/h001.vf1eIHxLrhd2QzkN8nLgybOj9HtFTKtlya9Mpmf8Gxg)

Back in 2017, she became the first woman and first Black person to represent Delaware in Congress. Now, she’s breaking barriers again by becoming Delaware’s first Black woman Senator.

This victory has also **doubled** the number of Black women elected to the Senate _**AND**_ will be the first time two Black women will serve at the same time.

**[We’re proud to continue building Black political power across the country — join us in congratulating Senator-elect Lisa Blunt Rochester!](https://an.collectivepac.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm3_iXaEBE6RoEOjHEa7LfvrWME3XVIgE_5Y7zzzwd29Y7uj6508ftgmNl003pVN-tuxINj6vcIkhKnw50mQOoewmLh8uvAccCIFIFFiZT1Z2Qz_N15sUGOZZtHq_WSnYQYLGrLLHi-dCitiiBPRcTfHFsjECs_vc1vA9oKVbGLhecGOfgwl3jp9S_Z5vbJzc1gEbG757s8pJi_5tztrs_qA9fkDDJq6ortMNveQf8HP_ROr__Wy5xLYT4RRYWDtR8cq06TdUpD0XRLdxcYbUMlXYNL7H4o4ECakFZXGowU9HSl2V7JM8ihqMjP7g7soa3TLIkhYgRjN4uAQswSOKT_3WDYurPP5xUdXDyl7pOJD5toHaOi9XV5iFqqqOHvD4R6Brsa_YTJUrQf3VRsRydn8G/4ba/qikLDyrHStqn8na0PNVpGA/h4/h001.COomuHzVZLW3cHgwK38ZlyM602xS6Gzgr_ROeoaVJDg)**

name: Jimmy Panetta
subject: Thank you, Peter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jimmy Panetta for Congress 2024, FEC ID# C00592154
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96807100/511255691/-2113420158?refcode=EM_NBI_JP_20241108_TY_0_6MO_JimmyPanetta_NA_NA_Postelection&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTkwMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3YzM1NTQ1NC0wNDllLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DxPhqVbRlodp5WPay-tLaG2ccTH3iXx9urboJWmR_ok=&emci=9b6ae5f2-009e-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=7c355454-049e-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=6108787) | Thank you so much for doing your part in our democracy.

Elections are an essential part of who we are as a nation of, by, and for the people. I also want to express my appreciation for all of your support for my campaign.

I am honored and humbled that the people of California's 19th Congressional District have again elected me to represent them in the United States Congress. I have always been driven and guided by the principle that good governing is good politics. I have worked hard to fulfill that goal and improve the lives of my constituents. Despite the division and dysfunction that can pervade our politics, I am committed to helping all of my constituents, no matter who they voted for, and I will work with anyone, regardless of party, to get things done. At the same time, I will fight for our rights and values when threatened, and ensure our system of checks and balances is functioning and abiding.

**I want to reassure you of my unwavering commitment to representing the people and families of the 19th Congressional District, regardless of the results of other elections.** I am committed to stepping up for my constituents of California’s 19th congressional district. I will continue to fight for lower prescription drug prices, affordable housing, accessible healthcare, public safety, and all that improves our community.

Thank you, again, for your support and the honor of serving for the next two years as the U.S. Representative for the people of California’s 19th Congressional District.

Sincerely,

Jimmy Panetta
---

**[Click here to unsubscribe.](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/KkfECMdTvUi3Zaeb87WFHA2?unsubscribedata=fxaaj9Itz6%2Br0Rg2xaTKeSv5dV5dZYbP3e48RvIFLIIVyDBmKPChJA95EyPn649E7tzWBzahbSEGmy2LrJ%2B%2FJljOTtFkaV10qfwEaYJ2GAICbeh1sBRB3fR3PtT5IJCRngnfD0rDlsiScUrmwotZck5DaQPy8nOn%2BcJtJA59Fa4Z8ONu%2Bmq7rZWz3Bz2VToK83oy0gvj27mbSzjtR1Qj%2BYK2PXWl%2BR5l0vJHOgWIJOKWbo8np0Ai%2B%2BG9CUPehOAgfbGX1N3AIZc6tjUkDO8cDZZVS2%2BnK5Bp4JqVgDR1tO8tHSrHmNK3E0x1y0KFopWdOAETPhiFPercg6JPG4JTz%2FVqb%2BeaLowK8%2FlHW2GZen7u%2FW9joh7Z4FGqWpad4BFbqYmtho2WpgLC7ivOzTwA8jjdN4F%2F6AyajnYC5FT57ws%3D)** **If you'd like to receive only the most important updates from us,[click here!](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96807101/511255692/1166513253?refcode=EM_NBI_JP_20241108_TY_0_6MO_JimmyPanetta_NA_NA_Postelection&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTkwMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3YzM1NTQ1NC0wNDllLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DxPhqVbRlodp5WPay-tLaG2ccTH3iXx9urboJWmR_ok=&emci=9b6ae5f2-009e-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=7c355454-049e-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=6108787)** Authorized and Paid for by Jimmy Panetta for Congress 2024, FEC ID# C00592154

name: Katie Porter
subject: Keep the count going in CA-27:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Truth to Power PAC
body:
In the oncoming war on science, we need George in the House.
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/9?t=1&akid=39283%2E3422442%2EoQr5PR)
Derek --
We can unseat a Republican and inch us closer to regaining the House:
George Whitesides is running to unseat a California Republican in the Antelope Valley, and he could do it: Right now, he’s down by barely 1%, and he’s winning a majority of the ballots election officials are still counting.
**[We have to count every last vote and build the reserves for a recount fight: Will you split a donation of $5 or more right now to support George Whitesides and help Democrats in the fight to take back the House?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ttp-em-20241108?refcode=em20241108-39283&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=39283_3422442_oQr5PR&akid=39283%2E3422442%2EoQr5PR)**
[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ttp-em-20241108?refcode=em20241108-39283&refcode2=39283_3422442_oQr5PR&akid=39283%2E3422442%2EoQr5PR)
More than one quarter of votes still haven’t been counted, and this will come down to the wire.
We know that science will be under attack in a Trump administration, and we need George in the House to take this on. He’s a former NASA chief of staff and the first CEO of Virgin Galactic, so we know he’ll be ready on Day One to handle the inevitable war on science, facts, and truth that’s about to come.
**And, this might just be the race to decide control of the House** --so George might be all that’s standing between us and a Trump administration with full control of the White House and both houses of Congress.
**[If you want to fight back, here’s your chance: Split a donation of $5 or more to help George flip this seat for Democrats in California.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ttp-em-20241108?refcode=em20241108-39283&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=39283_3422442_oQr5PR&akid=39283%2E3422442%2EoQr5PR)**
Thanks,
Katie

**Katie Porter started Truth to Power to support Democrats who reject corporate PAC money** and are running to protect the environment, restore the nationwide right to an abortion, and create an economy that works for working people. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/truth-to-power-1?refcode=em20241108-39283&amounts=&t=2&refcode2=39283_3422442_oQr5PR&akid=39283%2E3422442%2EoQr5PR)**

Welcome to the Indiana Democratic Party's weekly newsletter, The Kernel of Truth. The State Party will provide the most important fact - a kernel of truth - from the week. _[If you were forwarded this newsletter, you can sign up to receive the Kernel every week by clicking here.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96807252/511256340/1416943042?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYzk1ZjkzOC0wNTllLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=wvJAYFdTN97BVW-GKAcrfIFfKi1epQ_c_2evB4FhkHs=&emci=a507a671-009e-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=3c95f938-059e-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=4925535)_ ****
_André Carson and Frank Mrvan Win Re-Election to Congress, Grow Their Majorities_ ****************
[ Share INDems](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96807253/511256341/-1169403077?ref_src=twsrc%5Egoogle%7Ctwcamp%5Eserp%7Ctwgr%5Etweet&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYzk1ZjkzOC0wNTllLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=wvJAYFdTN97BVW-GKAcrfIFfKi1epQ_c_2evB4FhkHs=&emci=a507a671-009e-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=3c95f938-059e-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=4925535) Democratic Reps. Andre Carson and Frank Mrvan both cruised to re-election on Tuesday, and increased their victory margins in their districts. Rep. Carson nearly received 70% of the vote due to the incredible work he has done to advocate for Indianapolis families and working Hoosiers. He is fighting everyday to make our state better for all people. Rep. Mrvan was one of the most targeted Democratic members in the country. Frank ended up winning by 27K votes, more than doubling the number of votes he won by two years ago. We are excited to see them continue their work for us in Congress and protect Hoosiers from attacks on their freedoms and on their economic security. ****_Indiana Democrats Defend All Seats in Indiana Legislature_ **[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96807254/511256342/327826553?s=46&t=Dj7-Y210GYDWXG21xFaxCQ&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYzk1ZjkzOC0wNTllLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=wvJAYFdTN97BVW-GKAcrfIFfKi1epQ_c_2evB4FhkHs=&emci=a507a671-009e-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=3c95f938-059e-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=4925535)**[ Share INDems](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96807255/511256343/327826553?s=46&t=Dj7-Y210GYDWXG21xFaxCQ&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYzk1ZjkzOC0wNTllLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=wvJAYFdTN97BVW-GKAcrfIFfKi1epQ_c_2evB4FhkHs=&emci=a507a671-009e-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=3c95f938-059e-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=4925535) As the final votes are counted across Indiana, we have seen that Republicans failed to gain any seats in the Indiana legislature despite Trump’s large victory in Indiana and the rightward shift of the country. While this means we also fell short of our goal to break the Republicans’ supermajority control of the Statehouse, we are in a strong position to keep growing our numbers in upcoming elections because we didn’t lose any ground. Now, our members will work hard to make sure tax breaks benefit working Hoosiers before the wealthy elite, and that we support our public schools. ****_Each week, the State Party will highlight key policy from Democrats at the Statehouse or across the state. Democrats deliver a better future for Hoosier families, and we urge you to share this information with your networks._ **[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96807256/511256344/-1316959021?ref_src=twsrc%5Egoogle%7Ctwcamp%5Eserp%7Ctwgr%5Etweet&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYzk1ZjkzOC0wNTllLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=wvJAYFdTN97BVW-GKAcrfIFfKi1epQ_c_2evB4FhkHs=&emci=a507a671-009e-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=3c95f938-059e-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=4925535)**[Share INDems](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96807257/511256345/-1316959021?ref_src=twsrc%5Egoogle%7Ctwcamp%5Eserp%7Ctwgr%5Etweet&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYzk1ZjkzOC0wNTllLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=wvJAYFdTN97BVW-GKAcrfIFfKi1epQ_c_2evB4FhkHs=&emci=a507a671-009e-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=3c95f938-059e-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=4925535) There is bipartisan agreement that Hoosiers need property tax reform, and Democrats are committed to seeing it through as well. However, we must make sure that any changes to the property tax system benefit regular, everyday hard-working Hoosiers as the priority, not millionaires and the wealthy like Mike Braun himself. The current Republican plan gives the wealthy a huge cut while leaving scraps for working families. clean_body: | Welcome to the Indiana Democratic Party's weekly newsletter, The Kernel of Truth. The State Party will provide the most important fact - a kernel of truth - from the week. ** André Carson and Frank Mrvan Win Re-Election to Congress, Grow Their Majorities ************ Democratic Reps. Andre Carson and Frank Mrvan both cruised to re-election on Tuesday, and increased their victory margins in their districts. Rep. Carson nearly received 70% of the vote due to the incredible work he has done to advocate for Indianapolis families and working Hoosiers. He is fighting everyday to make our state better for all people. Rep. Mrvan was one of the most targeted Democratic members in the country. Frank ended up winning by 27K votes, more than doubling the number of votes he won by two years ago. We are excited to see them continue their work for us in Congress and protect Hoosiers from attacks on their freedoms and on their economic security. Indiana Democrats Defend All Seats in Indiana Legislature As the final votes are counted across Indiana, we have seen that Republicans failed to gain any seats in the Indiana legislature despite Trump’s large victory in Indiana and the rightward shift of the country. While this means we also fell short of our goal to break the Republicans’ supermajority control of the Statehouse, we are in a strong position to keep growing our numbers in upcoming elections because we didn’t lose any ground. Now, our members will work hard to make sure tax breaks benefit working Hoosiers before the wealthy elite, and that we support our public schools. Each week, the State Party will highlight key policy from Democrats at the Statehouse or across the state. Democrats deliver a better future for Hoosier families, and we urge you to share this information with your networks. There is bipartisan agreement that Hoosiers need property tax reform, and Democrats are committed to seeing it through as well. However, we must make sure that any changes to the property tax system benefit regular, everyday hard-working Hoosiers as the priority, not millionaires and the wealthy like Mike Braun himself. The current Republican plan gives the wealthy a huge cut while leaving scraps for working families. It is just another way to package a tax cut for the rich while forcing rent to go up and raising rates on small businesses. ** Get Involved! Opportunities After the Election Now is the time to join Hoosiers For Harris if you haven't already! Head to to sign up for our weekly phonebanks and make an impact for Democrats right here in Indiana! As we move past the 2024 Election, it is imperative that we unite together to create a better future for our state. One of the best ways to do that is to help us rebuild our party and grow in the coming months and years. Upcoming Candidate Trainings Join the National Democratic Training Committee on Tuesday, November 12th from 1-2 pm ET for a free virtual live training on Running an Effective Campaign Debrief , designed to help you get across the finish line on Election Day. INDems IN the News Capital Chronicle: Ball State Daily News: NWI Times: Indiana Public Media: WFYI: IndyStar: Evansville Courier & Press: AP: NWI Times: IN House Dems: Mirror Indy: [content goes here] 101 W Washington St., #1110E Indianapolis, IN 46204 Paid for by the Indiana Democratic Party, www.indems.org. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.

name: Michael Bennet
subject: Thank you
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BENNET FOR COLORADO
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/embutton?refcode=X_110824_ElectionStatement_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/embutton?refcode=X_110824_ElectionStatement_act]
Dear friend,
I’m reaching out today to say thank you. So many of you supported the Harris/Walz ticket by knocking on doors, making calls, and urging your friends, family, and neighbors to get out and vote. This was an extraordinary campaign from the start. Vice President Harris stepped into this moment and ran a joyful campaign with the promise of a brighter future for all Americans.
I am holding onto that promise, and I hope you will too. We know we face many challenging times in the days and years ahead, but we must never give up fighting for a better future.
No matter who is in the White House, the promise of a better, more prosperous future for our children and grandchildren will continue to drive my work in the Senate. I remain more committed than ever to passing an expanded Child Tax Credit, protecting our public lands, and ensuring America’s rural communities thrive.
I am deeply grateful for your support of Democrats up and down the ticket across the entire country. Our work is not done.
In the coming weeks, I look forward to hearing from you about how we can best move forward. To paraphrase James Baldwin, we cannot falter in our duty.
Onward,
Michael Bennet

Bennet for Colorado
PO Box 3078
Denver, CO 80201
United States

subject: Election updates from key state-level races
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President
body:
News from key races across the country
| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/96807365/511256768/-3547583?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjhlY2ZlZTY0LTA1OWUtZWYxMS04OGNlLTAwMGQzYTk4ZmE2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=qTYKiVvxXKMdOFRC6P2QALGGfqOHKAnkwS2zyI4zR6I=&emci=765b164e-ea9d-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=8ecfee64-059e-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=8431045)
---

Hi Peter — Like most of us, you’re probably still processing what happened this week — searching for answers, supporting your friends and loved ones, and making plans to keep fighting and moving forward. **We are right there with you.** It’s incredibly difficult and painful to accept that Donald Trump has once again been elected as president of the United States, knowing what he and his extreme MAGA agenda will attempt to do to our fundamental freedoms. There are going to be difficult fights ahead, and we must not waver. **But know this, Peter, all is not lost.** Our movement for fair maps won some key victories this week — and more races are still yet to be called. We are continuing to lay the groundwork for fair representation and the election of leaders who will defend, not undermine, our democracy. **Here’s what we know so far about some critical races for state legislatures and state supreme courts:**
* **At least 82 of the NDRC’s Democracy Defenders have already won their elections —** and there are many more races that have not yet been decided as votes continue to be counted. These candidates have fought and won important victories that will help create and defend fair electoral maps for future elections. We congratulate them on their wins and thank all of the NDRC supporters like you who helped propel them to victory.

* **With new fair legislative maps in Wisconsin, Democracy Defenders broke a Republican supermajority in the state senate and flipped seats in the state assembly,** creating a path to win majorities in 2026.

* **In Alabama, a newly fair map helped enable the election of two Black Congress members, Shomari Figures and Terri Sewell,** for the first time in history, and they will truly reflect the political desires of Alabamians. As former Attorney General Eric Holder said, “It is because of the heroism of previous generations that a tenacious group of Black Alabama voters challenged the State of Alabama in court to enforce protections enshrined in the Voting Rights Act of 1965, and took that fight all the way to the U.S. Supreme Court — and won.”

* **In Michigan, two pro-democracy candidates defeated ideological extremists to win seats on the state supreme court —** Kyra Harris Bolden and Kimberly Ann Thomas will both play key roles in defending a fair electoral system in this critical battleground state. A.G. Holder celebrated this outcome, declaring that “Michigan will have independent and fair-minded state supreme court justices who will protect their most cherished freedoms.”

* **And in other battleground states, Democracy Defenders flipped key seats blue — like Floyd Griffin,** who was elected to Georgia’s state house, and **Patty Kim** , who won a state senate seat in Pennsylvania.

We’re just beginning to understand the results and implications of the elections, but no matter what, we know that we’ll remain ready for the next round of fights for fair maps and the candidates who support them. Thank you for making these state-level victories possible, and for the work we will do together in the months and years ahead. Much more to come, The National Democratic Redistricting Committee | [DONATE](https://click.everyaction.com/k/96807366/511256769/1464966451?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_WhatWeKnow_email_20241108-NDRC-Tier1-WhatWeKnow_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20241108-NDRC-Tier1-WhatWeKnow_&utm_campaign=WhatWeKnow&amount=30&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjhlY2ZlZTY0LTA1OWUtZWYxMS04OGNlLTAwMGQzYTk4ZmE2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=qTYKiVvxXKMdOFRC6P2QALGGfqOHKAnkwS2zyI4zR6I=)

Here’s what we know so far about some critical races for state legislatures and state supreme courts: At least 82 of the NDRC’s Democracy Defenders have already won their elections — and there are many more races that have not yet been decided as votes continue to be counted. These candidates have fought and won important victories that will help create and defend fair electoral maps for future elections. We congratulate them on their wins and thank all of the NDRC supporters like you who helped propel them to victory. With new fair legislative maps in Wisconsin, Democracy Defenders broke a Republican supermajority in the state senate and flipped seats in the state assembly, creating a path to win majorities in 2026. In Alabama, a newly fair map helped enable the election of two Black Congress members, Shomari Figures and Terri Sewell, for the first time in history, and they will truly reflect the political desires of Alabamians. As former Attorney General Eric Holder said, “It is because of the heroism of previous generations that a tenacious group of Black Alabama voters challenged the State of Alabama in court to enforce protections enshrined in the Voting Rights Act of 1965, and took that fight all the way to the U.S. Supreme Court — and won.” In Michigan, two pro-democracy candidates defeated ideological extremists to win seats on the state supreme court — Kyra Harris Bolden and Kimberly Ann Thomas will both play key roles in defending a fair electoral system in this critical battleground state. A.G. Holder celebrated this outcome, declaring that “Michigan will have independent and fair-minded state supreme court justices who will protect their most cherished freedoms.” And in other battleground states, Democracy Defenders flipped key seats blue — like Floyd Griffin, who was elected to Georgia’s state house, and Patty Kim , who won a state senate seat in Pennsylvania. We’re just beginning to understand the results and implications of the elections, but no matter what, we know that we’ll remain ready for the next round of fights for fair maps and the candidates who support them. Thank you for making these state-level victories possible, and for the work we will do together in the months and years ahead. Much more to come, The National Democratic Redistricting Committee | = = Since 2017, the NDRC has executed a comprehensive redistricting strategy that shifts the redistricting power, creating fair districts where Democrats can compete. Our victories have been made possible by our strong community of grassroots supporters. = If you want to receive periodic updates from the NDRC on our fight for fair maps, text MAPS to 36787. Text HELP for help, STOP to end. Msg & Data rates may apply. We know we send a lot of emails, but we think it's important to keep you updated with the latest information on Republican gerrymandering and voter suppression and how we're fighting back -- regardless of whether you choose to donate. = to make a donation. Every dollar you give will go toward our fight to end gerrymandering and voter suppression. | | Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President. | | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- National Democratic Redistricting Committee 712 H St NE, Suite #25 Washington, DC 20002 United States ** if you'd like to receive fewer emails from the NDRC.

body:
Friend,
We hope you are hanging in there heading into this long weekend.
We just wanted to reach out to express our gratitude for the support you extended to Secretary Benson heading into this year’s big election. It means the world to the secretary, and our whole team, to be in this work side by side with you.
The outcome was obviously not what we hoped at the presidential level. But we can be immensely proud of the way that Secretary Benson oversaw this election in Michigan — ensuring every voice was heard and every vote was counted.
The Washington Post just featured Secretary Benson in a profile that captures her dedication to our democracy. We believe it speaks to the hope and resilience we’ll need to tap into in the coming months. Please give it a read:

name: Andrew Yang
subject: The Path Ahead
body:
Not surprisingly, Forward has seen a surge of interest in the past number of days. People know we can’t do the same thing over and over again. | |
---
| | |
---
| [ ](https://yangspeaks.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7448319cdb745ea2fd1b1b8a8&id=2de6867a7d&e=0f227c2ae3)
---
|
---
| Hello, I hope that you and yours are doing well. I wrote a piece in [Newsweek](https://yangspeaks.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7448319cdb745ea2fd1b1b8a8&id=b2eda3209b&e=0f227c2ae3) about how the Dems lost and a [POLITICO](https://yangspeaks.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7448319cdb745ea2fd1b1b8a8&id=48534cc26f&e=0f227c2ae3) piece as to what the Democratic Party should do now. I don’t expect the latter to be heeded obviously. 25 Forward Party endorsed or affiliated candidates won their races, including John Curtis in Utah and Don Davis in North Carolina. The win rate of our candidates was 20%, which was about the best you can hope for.
---
| [ ](https://yangspeaks.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7448319cdb745ea2fd1b1b8a8&id=de190c97b4&e=0f227c2ae3)
---
| People are taking different messages and lessons from Election Day. Here’s my take: The institutions are dying. People are losing faith right and left. Mistrust is a winner. If you try to sell me on your virtue, you’re probably full of it. The Democratic Party has taken on the mantle of the institutions; things are working, nothing to see here, believe us and believe in us, the experts know best. The Republican Party has taken on the energy of Donald Trump; everyone is full of it, the institutions are lying to you, I may say things you disagree with but at least I talk like a human being, I don’t pretend to be good. Not believing has become a lot easier than believing. Here’s how I put it in [The War on Normal People](https://yangspeaks.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7448319cdb745ea2fd1b1b8a8&id=89ce2834ba&e=0f227c2ae3): “We have entered an age of transparency where we can see our institutions and leaders for all of their flaws. Trust is for the gullible. Everything now will be a fight. Appealing to common interests will be all the more difficult.” That seems about right. As the fabric frays and the institutions unravel, individuals rise to replace them. Trump is a movement leader who survived two assassination attempts. Elon Musk is the richest man in the world who owns a social media platform. Joe Rogan is a martial arts expert who is the most widely listened to media figure in America. They each represent avatars of individuality. At our base, we prefer human beings to faceless corporations, change agents to the status quo in a time of widespread discontent. So what now? Do I think the Democratic Party will reform itself in some way? I’m not optimistic, even as I lay out the steps I would take here. The Party reminds me of a big company that has lost touch with its constituents, but has a big regulatory moat keeping it in place. I’ve always thought that building new ways of doing things or companies or organizations is a lot more fun and positive way to spend time than hoping that an incumbent will all of a sudden have a culture change. Not surprisingly, Forward has seen a surge of interest in the past number of days. People know we can’t do the same thing over and over again and hope for a different result. I’ve feared Trump for a long time as an accelerant to the decline of institutions. He’ll be President again. It’s time to build, both a new political movement but also in our own lives closer to home. Families are institutions. So are schools and businesses and places of worship and even book clubs. We always need great people in our lives. Start there. And yes, believe in them. To see my piece in Newsweek click [here](https://yangspeaks.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7448319cdb745ea2fd1b1b8a8&id=5cb0a5fc03&e=0f227c2ae3) and for my POLITICO piece click [here](https://yangspeaks.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7448319cdb745ea2fd1b1b8a8&id=01122a9e18&e=0f227c2ae3). To join Forward to help build the new party click [here](https://yangspeaks.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7448319cdb745ea2fd1b1b8a8&id=cfc8987a38&e=0f227c2ae3) – some phenomenal people are coming our way.
---
| **
Andrew Yang**
Co-Chair, Forward Party
[forwardparty.com](https://yangspeaks.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7448319cdb745ea2fd1b1b8a8&id=ca18164450&e=0f227c2ae3)
[andrewyang.com](https://yangspeaks.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7448319cdb745ea2fd1b1b8a8&id=665d94180a&e=0f227c2ae3)
---
| | | Want to change how you receive these emails?
You can [update your preferences](https://yangspeaks.us5.list-manage.com/profile?u=7448319cdb745ea2fd1b1b8a8&id=de65787ec1&e=0f227c2ae3&c=825fb7ba88) or [unsubscribe](https://yangspeaks.us5.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=7448319cdb745ea2fd1b1b8a8&id=de65787ec1&t=b&e=0f227c2ae3&c=825fb7ba88).
---

disclaimer_text: Paid for by Nancy Pelosi for Congress
body:
---
| | Let me explain where things stand: [**1.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96807522/511257418/1694124557?amount=15&refcode=20241108_em_fr_b1_np4c_main&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDIvMS84ODQ2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjMWYwZTc5MS0wNjllLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=20AxzHxLoqUDAIQZ98gMRfbO0Q7ecUmSPXycuT6M6mI=) As of 2pm, dozens of House races in swing districts are too close to call. [**2.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96807523/511257419/1694124557?amount=15&refcode=20241108_em_fr_b1_np4c_main&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDIvMS84ODQ2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjMWYwZTc5MS0wNjllLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=20AxzHxLoqUDAIQZ98gMRfbO0Q7ecUmSPXycuT6M6mI=) If we want ANY chance of reclaiming the House, we need to ensure every last vote is counted fairly. [**3. However, these battles are costly, and many campaigns likely have no funds left. They need our immediate help.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96807524/511257420/1694124557?amount=15&refcode=20241108_em_fr_b1_np4c_main&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDIvMS84ODQ2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjMWYwZTc5MS0wNjllLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=20AxzHxLoqUDAIQZ98gMRfbO0Q7ecUmSPXycuT6M6mI=) I know many of us may be feeling helpless right now. But I am urging you to stay focused on the task at hand. ********[_A Democratic House majority is our LAST chance to ensure a check on Trump’s power. If we want to win, we MUST ensure every last vote is counted fairly. We MUST send emergency resources to any race that still needs it. We MUST not leave anything to chance in these final battles. However, many outstanding campaigns likely spent down to zero. Their bank accounts are completely empty and they need help. So I set an ambitious goal to raise 5,000 donations in the next 10 hours to replenish funds and help Democrats bring this vital win home. Is there any way I could count on your help and your $15 in this consequential moment for our country? >>_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96807525/511257421/1694124557?amount=15&refcode=20241108_em_fr_b1_np4c_main&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDIvMS84ODQ2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjMWYwZTc5MS0wNjllLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=20AxzHxLoqUDAIQZ98gMRfbO0Q7ecUmSPXycuT6M6mI=)******** It all depends on what we do at this moment. These outstanding races are the ones that will decide who holds the House Majority. Only half the votes for my battleground House race in CA-9 are in, and as you can see, it’s a dead heat. | [](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=cc270a06f2294e02b628f775ada51a19&_e=UwGXkLIwP6i7qZFCTy71CVceHdMlxefZkudTOp4-IFZRwW-0bBFmD1_QoxjAK2dwGCPRdb9ZXkG359Xl4PxKih8HG8vz9Kmy-DLwyzTP8q2T6lSE-VnPVvmRZB9odEOY0zMfM5RxuLVfRZdQtlLoC8qxNtI15fRqKq-IHmjx3r_tsv9IIkWqUjfOG-qDpKtZUFX2WrHzcAO0HLSGzgWDNaYsuJTiabknXs9A-22q-e0Ls-0A1Dn9FkbfnO2T7FwE_w-n1j5Rzlk_cdl5WkFJLi8k0K-ALGo6UQ4ImYbnDf4q2cDT3HKsBKAqq3riOV3GGiP1oPlLVUnlnC3DD8_DAHJP1KPagR8ivnl1yYd_S4SyNPVTNFqsNzyBRxhr0pmfSklStYhYAat5V8gj6JGkD4cz7re6uV4WrkNuA9XQ1oTCDXSnnif85NGRqFVFe8rw)
Fellow American,

Across the nation, we’re celebrating huge wins for President Trump and a new Republican Senate Majority.

**But the battle for the House isn’t over yet.**

Only half the votes for my battleground House race in CA-9 are in, _**and as you can see, it’s a dead heat.**_

| **Meet Kevin Lincoln
**
My name is Kevin Lincoln, and my story, is the Valley’s story. I am a 3rd generation Stocktonian, raising a 4th generation in the city I love. I’m the grandson of Mexican immigrants, and my stepfather is an Army veteran.

Like many Stocktonians, my family has overcome economic setbacks. My own father struggled to survive on these streets, and was at one time, homeless. But I never let adversity define me. I joined the Marines and served in the White House Military Office, assigned to Marine One. I graduated college while working full-time and raising a family. I’ve worked in corporate America and served our community as a pastor, and as a non-profit leader.

I’m blessed with a beautiful wife, Bonnie, and two amazing children. I’ve lived a life of service, but the greatest honor of my life has been giving back to my community, through serving as Mayor of my hometown, Stockton.

name: Salud Carbajal
subject: Thank you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Salud Carbajal for Congress
body:
First and foremost, I would like to thank you for joining my campaign team this cycle. Thanks to your dedication and support, I am honored to have the opportunity to continue my service in Congress
Like you, I am saddened to see the incredible and inspiring presidential campaign that I was proud to be a part of come up short. I am reminded, however, that we have been here before.
I was elected alongside Donald Trump eight years ago, and served on the front lines of fighting back against his extremist policies from my seat in Congress. We have stood up to his radical views before, and I am prepared to do so again in this coming term. I know how much is at stake if we do not fight every single day to deny the vision that Donald Trump and his Project 2025 have laid out for our country.
Although the results of the presidential race were not what we wanted, I hope you will not lose hope, get discouraged, or disengage. Our nation needs your dedication and involvement now more than ever.
Thank you again for standing with me, and I look forward to continuing our work together.
Sincerely,
Salud Carbajal
Paid for by Salud Carbajal for Congress
PO Box 1290, Santa Barbara, CA 93102
info@saludcarbajal.com; www.saludcarbajal.com

clean_body: | | | | Secure your tickets today Friend, The 2024 Advance is Set to Be the Most Attended in Recent History! As we prepare to gather in Charlottesville and celebrate the historic Republican victories across the Commonwealth and the nation, we’re thrilled by the overwhelming interest in this event. Important: If you've reserved a room, you must purchase your ticket by MIDNIGHT TONIGHT to secure your reservation. We’ve received hundreds of inquiries about waitlisting for our room blocks, and starting tomorrow, we’ll begin releasing rooms of those who have not purchased tickets. Don’t miss out—secure your ticket and your spot today! | Our room block at the Forum Hotel is officially full, but we still have a limited number of rooms available at our alternative locations. Click below to book your room now —and make sure to purchase your ticket at the same time to secure both your accommodation and event access! Reminder: A ticket purchase is required to confirm your hotel reservation. Don't wait—book today to guarantee your spot! | | Thank you for your continued support, and we hope to see you in December! The Republican Party of Virginia PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY THE REPUBLICAN PARTY OF VIRGINIA ==

name: Hillary Scholten
subject: we can find a way forward, together
disclaimer_text: Paid for by A Pleasant Peninsula PAC
body:
When you start to feel despair over what happened in the Presidential race, remember that really is only part of the story.

---
| Team, how are you? These past two days have been tough, but I’ve been thinking non-stop about what we accomplished here together despite a political environment that was against us at every turn.

When you start to feel despair over what happened in the Presidential race, remember that really is only part of the story. There are bright lights everywhere, including right here in MI-03. We overcame unimaginable odds in 2022 when we flipped this 50-year stronghold and elected the first pro-choice woman to represent West Michigan. Then, this year, despite a country trending FAR and FAST to the right, we sent the first Democrat since the Civil War BACK for a second term to Congress.

So, as I think about the dark and troubling road that lies ahead, I’m honored to be able to shine a light on it. I’m not agonizing; I’m organizing. I want to be able to help replicate what we’ve built here in West Michigan in other places across the country. Because the reality is, MOST Americans are with us on the most critical issues–protecting a woman’s right to choose, ensuring we have clean air to breathe and water to drink, and protecting our kids from gun violence, among others. But they ALSO need leaders who will listen to their concerns on the economy and DO SOMETHING about it.
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96808621/511261203/2042543991?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTY0MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNGVhZWYwNy0wYzllLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=O5khgY3Y6pMzGPQyszlTypENd-7QKK6eyrgDVEQPuhU=)
_...but it didn’t crumble for MI-03._

That’s exactly what I’m doing, and I want to help other candidates do the same. So that we can continue to grow and expand a NEW GENERATION of leaders. It’s up to us now–those of us who KNOW what works on the ground.

So today, I’m reaching out about my leadership fund, A Pleasant Peninsula PAC–a fund that will help me grow and expand our bench of leaders ready to face the challenges of not just the next 4 years but the next 40 years. If you’re feeling despair over where we are but unsure where to put your frustration, I am asking you to consider a small investment in the future through my leadership. I need you in my corner to face the road ahead. Would you consider a $25 or $50 contribution to help us expand what we’ve built here: [_**https://secure.actblue.com/donate/a-pleasant-peninsula-em**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96808623/511261205/2042543991?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTY0MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNGVhZWYwNy0wYzllLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=O5khgY3Y6pMzGPQyszlTypENd-7QKK6eyrgDVEQPuhU=) [_**If you’re able, we’d be honored to have you support the future we’re building. Your contribution today ensures we have the resources to build a foundation for the work to come.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96808625/511261207/2042543991?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTY0MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNGVhZWYwNy0wYzllLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=O5khgY3Y6pMzGPQyszlTypENd-7QKK6eyrgDVEQPuhU=)
|

Thank you for believing in our vision and being part of this journey with us.

Deeply grateful,

Hillary

clean_body: When you start to feel despair over what happened in the Presidential race, remember that really is only part of the story. | Team, how are you? These past two days have been tough, but I’ve been thinking non-stop about what we accomplished here together despite a political environment that was against us at every turn. When you start to feel despair over what happened in the Presidential race, remember that really is only part of the story. There are bright lights everywhere, including right here in MI-03. We overcame unimaginable odds in 2022 when we flipped this 50-year stronghold and elected the first pro-choice woman to represent West Michigan. Then, this year, despite a country trending FAR and FAST to the right, we sent the first Democrat since the Civil War BACK for a second term to Congress. So, as I think about the dark and troubling road that lies ahead, I’m honored to be able to shine a light on it. I’m not agonizing; I’m organizing. I want to be able to help replicate what we’ve built here in West Michigan in other places across the country. Because the reality is, MOST Americans are with us on the most critical issues–protecting a woman’s right to choose, ensuring we have clean air to breathe and water to drink, and protecting our kids from gun violence, among others. But they ALSO need leaders who will listen to their concerns on the economy and DO SOMETHING about it. = ...but it didn’t crumble for MI-03. That’s exactly what I’m doing, and I want to help other candidates do the same. So that we can continue to grow and expand a NEW GENERATION of leaders. It’s up to us now–those of us who KNOW what works on the ground. So today, I’m reaching out about my leadership fund, A Pleasant Peninsula PAC–a fund that will help me grow and expand our bench of leaders ready to face the challenges of not just the next 4 years but the next 40 years. If you’re feeling despair over where we are but unsure where to put your frustration, I am asking you to consider a small investment in the future through my leadership. I need you in my corner to face the road ahead. Would you consider a $25 or $50 contribution to help us expand what we’ve built here: = = | Thank you for believing in our vision and being part of this journey with us. Deeply grateful, Hillary * | | Paid for by A Pleasant Peninsula PAC A Pleasant Peninsula PAC 122 C Street NW, Suite 360 Washington, DC 20001 United States | = If you would like to receive fewer emails, . name: Dr. Kristin Hook
subject: A Silver Lining
body:
| | | | | [](https://drkristinhook.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fd888d2bb4a2d4cad917af4b9&id=edbc263ccc&e=395a33a3d2)
---
|
---
| | | | |
---
| I’m still processing extreme disappointment and grieffrom election night, as I’m sure many of you are too. But losing the White House and Senate doesn’t and shouldn’t diminish the work we did this past year.
Yesterday, I finally had a chance to pour over some of the [election results out of Texas](https://drkristinhook.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fd888d2bb4a2d4cad917af4b9&id=c3d9e202f1&e=395a33a3d2), and I want to share something BIG with you.
**152,900**
That’s how many folks voted for me in our district. That is how many people believe in a better future for our country.One that is fair and just, not extreme and morally bankrupt.
And you know what else?
**Among the U.S. House races here in Texas,****I got the highest number of votes****of any non-incumbent Democrat!**
For the races with challengers, I got outshined by Reps. Lloyd Doggett, Jasmine Crockett, and Greg Casar – but who wouldn’t!?
**To everyone who voted for me, for us, THANK YOU!** I do not take a single vote for granted.These votes are a testament to how much grassroots support we had and how hard work truly pays off.
I gave this campaign my everything.Without a full campaign staff or manager and with little to no institutional or organizational support, I had no other choice.
Despite every setback (and believe me there were many!), I am so proud of what we were able to accomplish and am forever grateful for the team I did have, every super volunteer, everyone who put up a yard sign.It was supporters like you who kept me going!
Take good care of yourselves this week, and when you’re ready,I hope you’ll join me back in the fight.
Always in your corner,
---
| P.S. If you have a yard sign, please keep and store it! If you are unable to do so and want us to store it, then please fill in [this Google Form](https://drkristinhook.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fd888d2bb4a2d4cad917af4b9&id=fc8042af19&e=395a33a3d2).

Hey team,
Here’s where we stand as of this morning:
* Our House Champions: Steven Horsford, Susie Lee, and Dina Titus won their races and will represent Nevada in Washington!
* We won the majority of the NV State Senate and are on target to win back the majority in the NV State Assembly but there are still NINE races that have not been called.
* Jacky Rosen has the lead to keep our Senate seat **BLUE** but it’s still _too close to call_ with tens of thousands of votes left to count.

We’re raising funds to cure ballots and rescue votes for Democrats up and down the ticket. **Ballot must be cured by 5pm on Tuesday, November 12.**
